title: Align sandbox methods with ops and add effect dry-run repair
description: Make canonical SandboxGame mutator names exactly match the Op union, retaining old convenience names only as hidden aliases. Remove server-only GameEngine methods from the agent-facing reference; expose exact mutator/read/control signatures and snapshot-at-step-start semantics. Add semantic validation for private/unknown members and argument mistakes, a context-bound dry_run_effect tool using the real sandbox/reducers on a clone, mandatory prompt usage for generated code, and one bounded repair call on final validation failure. Fix cross-room snippet cache isolation.
acceptance_criteria: CI parity test fails when an op lacks a same-named sandbox method/fields; skip_turn/change_draw_count/custom_note/unregister_hook exist; state.draw is rejected with a draw_cards recommendation; generated snippets/hooks are dry-run before persistence; one repair can recover invalid code; private facade access and cross-room cache collisions are rejected.
close_reason: Implemented exact SandboxGame/op parity, semantic validation, cloned plan dry-runs, bounded repair, cache isolation, compatibility handling, and tests/docs.

title: Engine resolution plans and atomic hybrid execution
description: Introduce an ordered serializable ResolutionPlan with ops and snippet steps. Lower legacy program/snippet and canonical ops+snippet into ordered steps; execute against a cloned working state with the played card already out of hand; route every step through reducers, the room EventBus, hooks, RNG, and immediate end checks; commit only after the whole plan succeeds. On unrecoverable failure roll back mechanics but consume the card as a visible no-op. Persist and replay the complete canonical without a second LLM call.
acceptance_criteria: Real Room regressions prove Chess Master draws then scores the post-draw hand excluding the played card; canonical Card Counter executes both halves; a failing suffix leaves no partial mechanics but consumes the card; snippet-only and hybrid effects fire score/on_play hooks and end_game immediately; legacy cards remain readable.
close_reason: Ordered ResolutionPlan landed with atomic opsŌåÆsnippet execution, post-effect state barriers, canonical replay, room hook/end parity, docs, and full-suite coverage.

title: deck: premade-only build + finalise (5 blanks/player) + simple source
description: Extend deck.py::build_deck with: a premade-only build (30 venue-filtered premade, no blanks) for lobbyŌåÆsetup; a finalise step (called at setupŌåÆplaying) folding in the player-authored card ids already in state.cards + 5├Śnum_players blanks, then shuffle; and a 'simple' source flag so the premade pool reads seed_cards_simple.json. Blanks are absolute (5/player) for the basic game; keep the BLANK_CARD_RATIO path for other modes. Reuse _make_blank_card.
acceptance_criteria: Premade build yields exactly the venue-filtered premade set with no blanks; finalise produces deck size = premade + 5*authored-per-player + 5*num_players blanks; deterministic given an injected rng.

title: rooms: enter and drive phase=setup
description: Make _handle_start phase-aware. lobbyŌåÆsetup: build the venue-filtered 30-premade pool into state.cards NOW (so SetupPhase can render them for synergy), deal nothing, set phase=setup, broadcast. setupŌåÆplaying: gate on every non-spectator having authored 5 cards; inject 5├Śnum_players blanks; shuffle; deal 5 each; phase=playing; start first turn. Reuses the existing {type:start} message (frontend already sends it from both lobby and setup).
acceptance_criteria: Starting from lobby lands in phase=setup with 30 premade cards visible in the snapshot; starting from setup is rejected until all players authored 5, then deals 5 each and begins play.

title: rooms: manual drawŌåÆplayŌåÆend-turn turn model
description: Adopt the explicit drawŌåÆplayŌåÆend-turn model; drop auto-draw from _start_turn. Add DrawMsg (type=draw) to models/ws_messages.py ClientMsg union and gate in _dispatch (active player, once per turn). Per-turn has_drawn/has_played flags on Room. play allowed only after draw. end_turn advances (keep 'pass' as a back-compat alias). Deck emptied by a draw arms end-of-game after the active player ends their turn.
acceptance_criteria: A turn requires an explicit draw before play; a second draw in one turn is rejected; end_turn advances; drawing the last card arms end-of-game (handled in bead 13).

title: rooms: deterministic play resolution (opsŌåÆapply_effect, LLM fallback, no silent no-op)
description: Rebuild _handle_play resolution order: (1) card has compilable ops ŌåÆ compile_card ŌåÆ apply_effect (NO LLM ŌĆö the simple deck lives here); (2) else best-effort interpret_card in a thread, apply if verdict ok and program present; (3) else deterministic CustomNoteOp fallback that logs the play had no mechanical effect. A play NEVER silently no-ops; card always leaves hand and turn always advances. Keep the existing prompt_choice flow, driven off the compiled program's ops.
acceptance_criteria: A simple point card applies its delta with the LLM asserted un-called; a card with no ops and a blank whose LLM path fails both still log a note line and advance the turn.

title: rooms: played card leaves hand ŌåÆ zone via move_card
description: rooms/room.py::_handle_play never removes the played card from the hand. Rebuild it to move the card via the existing immutable GameState.move_card: destination by placement ŌĆö immediate point cards ŌåÆ discard; placement:self persistent ŌåÆ player in_play; placement:center/modifier ŌåÆ center. Fixes the 'I played a card but it stayed in my hand' bug.
acceptance_criteria: After a play, card_id is gone from the player's hand and present in exactly one destination zone (discard/in_play/center) per its placement; state stays immutable.

title: data: deterministic simple seed deck (seed_cards_simple.json)
description: New data/seed_cards_simple.json of ~30 point-only gold cards using the existing GoldCard/CardCanonical schema (models/card.py). Ops limited to add_points/subtract_points/set_points/steal_points; targets in {self, all_others, chooser, player_with_most_points, player_with_least_points}; all venue=all. Include 2 end-of-game 'worth N points if you keep it' cards to exercise kept-in-hand scoring.
acceptance_criteria: File validates via parse_seed_card; every card compiles via compile_card to a non-None program (except any intentional filler); 2 cards carry an on_game_end/kept-bonus clause.

title: engine: cardŌåÆEffectProgram compiler (compile_card)
description: New pure engine/compile.py::compile_card(card_dict) -> EffectProgram | None. Reads card canonical.ops (authoring CardOp vocab), maps player-target axis via existing effects.map_authoring_target, builds runtime Op objects from the existing discriminated union. Returns None for filler/un-authored cards (no structured ops). Single home for authoringŌåÆruntime translation; no new op vocabulary.
acceptance_criteria: Each seed_cards_simple.json card compiles to the expected runtime Ops; filler cards compile to None; unknown targets fall back safely (chooser) rather than raising.

title: deck: preserve canonical/ops/venue through card normalisation
description: ROOT BLOCKER. deck.py::_normalise_card returns only {id,title,description,creator_id}, silently stripping the card's canonical/ops/venue. Structured cards therefore reach play with no ops, forcing every play through the LLM (which then no-ops if verdict!=ok). Fix _normalise_card to carry canonical (and thus ops) and venue into state.cards. Unlocks the entire deterministic path.
acceptance_criteria: A gold/simple card loaded through collect_cards/build_deck retains its canonical.ops and venue in the returned card dict; existing filler/blank cards unaffected.

title: tbwc engine rebuild: playable deterministic basic game
description: Rebuild the orchestration layer (Room, deck build, setup, turn model, end-game) on top of the sound pure core (typed Ops in models/effects.py, reducers, win eval, immutable GameState). The basic game must be fully playable and testable with a deterministic simple deck ŌĆö NO AI on the critical path. Player-authored/blank cards keep the LLM interpreter but strictly best-effort with a CustomNoteOp fallback so a play never silently no-ops. See plan: alright-we-need-to-zazzy-abelson.md. Confirmed root-cause bugs: (1) played cards never leave hand (move_card unused in play path); (2) deck._normalise_card strips canonical/ops so structured cards no-op and every play is forced through the LLM; (3) no setup/create step; (4) pass always offered; (5) raw-id effect log; (6) can't see others' plays; (7) no end-of-game accountability; (8) no venue filtering.

title: bug(engine): start-game must build a deck of >=30 (shuffle -> create -> start)
description: Starting a game yields an empty deck. _handle_start (src/tbwc/rooms/room.py:82-84) only flips phase to "playing"; GameState.deck (game_state.py:40) defaults to []. There is NO shuffle/create/deal/build-deck code anywhere in src/tbwc/. Seed cards load into Qdrant RAG only (rag/seed.py), never into a game deck; _handle_draw errors "Deck is empty". Implement the intended flow: (1) shuffle/collect existing cards (seed cards + prior-game kept cards from RAG), (2) create cards into state.cards, (3) shuffle >=30 card ids into state.deck, (4) start play and deal starting hands. Reuse rag/seed.py loading and rooms/epilogue.py upsert. Acceptance: a new game starts with len(deck) >= 30 and draw works.

title: Config flag + run_agent dispatch + eval A/B (pipeline vs legacy)
description:
## Why

Ship the pipeline dark behind a config flag, keep the legacy single agent one flag away for rollback, and prove the pipeline on the eval scoreboard before flipping the default.

## What

### 1. `src/config.py` (follow the `triage_agent_*` precedent, lines 91-99)

```python
# --- Interpretation pipeline (intent -> planner -> coder) ---
interpret_pipeline_enabled: bool = False   # land dark; flip after eval A/B
intent_agent_model: str = ""               # "" -> shared llm_chat_model
planner_agent_model: str = ""
coder_agent_model: str = ""
```

### 2. `src/agent/runtime.py` dispatch

Rename the body of `run_agent` to `_run_single_agent` (private, unchanged behavior); `run_agent` becomes a small dispatcher: flag on ŌåÆ `pipeline.run_pipeline(...)`, off ŌåÆ `_run_single_agent(...)`. Signature and docstring UNCHANGED ŌĆö room tests that `patch("agent.runtime.run_agent", ...)` are unaffected, and room.py call sites (1172, ~2576) need zero changes. Keep `_assemble_tools` exported (src/evals/runner.py:149 imports it).

### 3. `src/evals/runner.py`

- Thread the `enabled_tools` name-filter through to per-stage toolboxes (a tools-override behaving like today's `tools=` param at line 193's run_agent call).
- Split caller-passed `max_tool_calls`/`timeout` proportionally (6/8/10) across stages so sweeps stay meaningful.
- Add `pipeline: bool` to `EvalConfig` to force pipeline vs legacy for A/B runs.

### 4. Eval A/B + default flip

Run the benchmark with pipeline=True vs False on the WRITEUP scoreboard metrics (intent_match / dsl_validity / executability / invalid-rate; see docs/WRITEUP.md + src/evals/). If the pipeline matches or beats the single agent, flip `interpret_pipeline_enabled` default to True in a follow-up commit. Record results in the bead notes / bd remember.

## Tests

- Flag off ŌåÆ `_run_single_agent` invoked; flag on ŌåÆ `run_pipeline` invoked (monkeypatch sentinels; settings via `get_settings.cache_clear()` + env, config.py:165 pattern).
- Existing room tests (`tests/test_room_agent.py`, `test_room_agent_context.py`) pass unchanged.
- Eval runner: enabled_tools filtering reaches stage toolboxes; EvalConfig.pipeline honored.

## Acceptance

Default settings byte-identical behavior (flag off = legacy path); pipeline reachable via env/settings; A/B results recorded; full suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: interpret_pipeline_enabled=False lands byte-identical; flag flips to pipeline; room tests unchanged; eval A/B run recorded; default flipped only if pipeline matches/beats legacy

title: LangGraph StateGraph orchestrator: intent -> planner -> coder with conditional edges
description:
## Why

The heart of the conversion: a LangGraph `StateGraph` orchestrating intent ŌåÆ planner ŌåÆ coder with conditional edges for failure/degradation. **A StateGraph is REQUIRED (user-mandated): this is a class project that must demonstrate LangGraph agentic graphs** ŌĆö do not replace with plain sequential function calls.

## What ŌĆö new `src/agent/pipeline.py`

### State

```python
class PipelineState(TypedDict, total=False):
    # inputs
    title: str; description: str; card_art: ...; actor_id: str | None
    creator_id: str | None; card_id: str | None; game_state: ...  # snapshot
    # artifacts
    intent: CardIntent | None
    plan: MechanicsPlan | None
    draft: InterpretResult | None      # coder output pre-validation
    result: InterpretResult | None     # final
    # bookkeeping
    deadline: float                    # monotonic wall-clock deadline
    stage_errors: list[str]
```

### Graph ŌĆö `build_interpret_graph() -> CompiledStateGraph`

Nodes: `intent`, `planner`, `coder`, `validate_repair`, `finalize`. Each stage node invokes its bounded ReAct agent via `stage_runner.run_stage(...)` (each stage's `create_agent` loop is itself a graph ŌĆö invoked as a subgraph with its own recursion_limit). Conditional edges:

- START ŌåÆ `intent`
- `intent` ŌåÆ conditional:
  - stage returned None (timeout/garbage) ŌåÆ `finalize` (today's `_fallback_result`: verdict="invalid", agent_error=True)
  - `ambiguity == "undecipherable"` and `persona_action == "do_nothing"` ŌåÆ `finalize` (empty program, intent's comment preserved)
  - `persona_action == "punish_author"` (rare, abusive-card branch) ŌåÆ `planner`, with the intent rewritten to "dock the author points" so the docking is a REAL effect
  - else ŌåÆ `planner`
- `planner` ŌåÆ conditional:
  - stage returned None ŌåÆ `coder` with a stub plan `MechanicsPlan(strategy=intent.summary, steps=[])` (coder prompt covers "no detailed plan ŌĆö design it yourself"); degrade, don't die
  - `feasible == False` ŌåÆ `finalize`: verdict="invalid" + a `CustomNoteOp` naming the intended effect (keeps the triage/capability-wish flow fed) + intent's comment
  - else ŌåÆ `coder`
- `coder` ŌåÆ `validate_repair`: run `_validate_or_repair_effect` (moved to stage_runner) with the CODER's system prompt ŌĆö sandbox validate + dry-run + ONE tools-disabled repair call, else strip effect to verdict="invalid" (exactly today's semantics)
- `validate_repair` ŌåÆ `finalize`
- `finalize`: assemble `InterpretResult` = coder's effect fields (plan/program/snippet/verdict) + **intent's `comment` and `persona_action`**. Comment survives downstream failures ŌĆö strictly better than today's canned fallback lines. Coder output never carries a comment.

### Entry point

`run_pipeline(title, description, state, actor_id, *, creator_id, card_id, card_art, model, timeout, max_tool_calls, forced_call_timeout, allow_persistent_tools, config) -> InterpretResult` ŌĆö same parameter surface as `run_agent`. Sets `deadline`, invokes the compiled graph, NEVER raises (wrap graph invocation the same way run_agent guards today; any escape ŌåÆ `_fallback_result`).

### Budgets & tools

Pipeline-wide deadline defaults to AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (600s, runtime.py:59); each node budget = `min(stage_default, deadline - now)`; deadline exhausted between nodes = that stage failing. Per-stage defaults (module constants):

| Node | timeout | tool cap | tools |
|---|---|---|---|
| intent | 120s | 6 | web_search, mtg_lookup (glossary-extended), card_rag_hybrid, game_rules, read_game_state, recall_decisions* |
| planner | 180s | 8 | game_rules, read_engine_methods, read_game_state, read_game_history, card_rag_hybrid |
| coder | 240s | 10 | dry_run_effect, read_engine_methods, read_game_state, remember_decision* |

(*) gated by `allow_persistent_tools`, exactly as today. Build context-bound tools from the same factories `_assemble_tools` uses (runtime.py:423; makers in src/agent/tools/). Caller-passed `max_tool_calls`/`timeout` (evals) split proportionally 6/8/10.

- `CardIntent.complexity == "trivial"` shrinks downstream caps (planner 2, coder 4). NO node skipping beyond the conditional edges above ŌĆö the deterministic short-circuit already exists upstream (`compile_card_plan`, room.py:1140).
- Image-rejection retry (`_is_image_rejection`, runtime.py:90; retry logic at ~648) applies to the intent node only ŌĆö planner/coder are text-only.
- Struggling-author HELP MODE params flow into `build_intent_prompt` (same computation as the legacy path).

## Tests ŌĆö new `tests/test_agent_pipeline.py`

Queued `GenericFakeChatModel` fakes (one fake serves all stages ŌĆö enqueue intent JSON, plan JSON, coder JSON; patterns from tests/test_agent_skeleton.py):
- Happy path: final result = coder effect + intent comment/persona_action; artifacts threaded (planner's HumanMessage contains CardIntent.summary ŌĆö capture pattern at test_agent_skeleton.py:250).
- Intent garbage/timeout ŌåÆ bounded fallback, agent_error=True, never raises.
- Planner garbage ŌåÆ coder still runs with stub plan.
- feasible=False ŌåÆ invalid verdict + CustomNoteOp + intent comment preserved.
- Coder invalid snippet ŌåÆ exactly ONE repair call (port of skeleton test at 174); double failure ŌåÆ effect-stripped invalid keeping intent comment (port of 193).
- do_nothing short-circuit skips planner/coder; punish_author path yields a real docking effect.
- Per-stage tool binding: record `bind_tools` per stage (pattern test_agent_skeleton.py:238) and assert each node got its table's toolset.
- Expired deadline between nodes degrades gracefully.
- Graph topology test: assert node names + conditional edge routing (good class-project evidence ŌĆö the compiled graph is inspectable via `graph.get_graph()`).

## Acceptance

`run_pipeline` returns a valid InterpretResult for all branches, never raises; graph built with `langgraph.graph.StateGraph` + conditional edges; layering respected (no board imports); full suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: Built on langgraph StateGraph with conditional edges (class-project requirement); run_pipeline never raises; all degradation branches covered by tests incl. graph topology test; suite + ruff green

title: Stage prompts module: split persona.py into intent/planner/coder prompts
description:
## Why

The single system prompt (`src/agent/persona.py:build_system_prompt`) fuses persona, op-catalog knowledge, sandbox rules, and the output contract. The three stages need focused prompts: persona lives ONLY in the intent stage; the planner is a creative game engineer; the coder is a sandbox specialist. persona.py stays as the legacy prompt + shared-blocks module.

## What

New `src/agent/stage_prompts.py` with three builders (mirror `build_system_prompt`'s signature style ŌĆö plain-string testable, no LLM imports):

### `build_intent_prompt(title, description, state, actor_id, creator_id, *, has_art=False, struggling_author=False, author_fallbacks=0)`
Compose (reusing persona.py blocks ŌĆö the versions REWRITTEN by the persona-retarget bead):
- `PERSONA_PREAMBLE`
- A new INTENT_JOB block: "Determine what the player WANTS this card to do. Resolve slang, memes, and game-jargon (use web_search for pop culture, mtg_lookup for MTG terms/keywords, card_rag_hybrid for similar past cards, game_rules for house context). Do NOT design mechanics or write code ŌĆö capture intent."
- `PERSONA_DECISION_LOGIC` + `COMMENT_REQUIREMENT` (intent stage OWNS comment + persona_action)
- `STRUGGLING_AUTHOR_NOTE` (formatted) when struggling_author is set
- AUTHORSHIP note (same computation as persona.py:237-244) + `CARD_ART_NOTE` when has_art
- `describe_state(state, actor_id)` ŌĆö export persona.py's `_describe_state` as public `describe_state` with a `_describe_state` back-compat alias
- CardIntent JSON output contract (keys mirroring agent.contract.CardIntent; same "single JSON object, no prose/fences" style as OUTPUT_CONTRACT)

### `build_planner_prompt(intent: CardIntent, state, actor_id, creator_id)`
- Neutral game-engineer preamble (no persona, no comment duty)
- The op-catalog / plan-shape half of `INTERPRETER_JOB` (persona.py:54-123: the bullets on set_rule/set_condition/set_card_attribute/destroy-as-discard/create_card/register_hook/reactions/interactions/ordered plans)
- Creativity mandate (verbatim spirit): "BE CREATIVE. When no single op expresses the mechanic ('target player discards a random card', 'target player draws a card from the discard pile'), plan direct state manipulation through SandboxGame ŌĆö zone moves are just pop-from-one-list/push-to-another via the move_card-backed reducers. Call read_engine_methods to see the exact API and targets before declaring anything infeasible. Only set feasible=false when the effect needs capabilities the sandbox truly lacks (e.g. new UI)."
- The rendered CardIntent + `describe_state`
- MechanicsPlan JSON output contract

### `build_coder_prompt(intent: CardIntent, plan: MechanicsPlan, state, actor_id)`
- Sandbox-specialist preamble
- The sandbox-rules half of `INTERPRETER_JOB`: snippet constraints (no imports/exec/eval/open/dunders), SandboxGame not GameEngine, deferred writes semantics, ctx keys, interaction result reading, and the dry_run mandate (persona.py:116-117: MUST call dry_run_effect on every snippet/hook/mixed plan before returning)
- Handle the stub-plan case: "If the plan has no steps, design the mechanics yourself from the intent."
- Effect-only output contract: OUTPUT_CONTRACT (persona.py:162) with the `comment` and `persona_action` keys REMOVED ŌĆö the coder never writes the comment (verdict/plan/program/snippet only)

## Tests

String-content unit tests mirroring `test_build_system_prompt_contains_key_sections` (tests/test_agent_skeleton.py:117): intent prompt contains persona + decision logic + CardIntent keys but NO sandbox ctx rules; planner prompt contains op catalog + creativity mandate but NO comment requirement; coder prompt contains dry_run mandate + snippet constraints but NO persona preamble and NO "comment" key in its contract; HELP MODE block appears only when struggling_author=True.

## Acceptance

Three builders pure-string testable; persona.py unchanged for the legacy path (plus the public `describe_state` export); tests + full suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: Three pure-string builders; intent owns persona+comment; planner has op catalog + creativity mandate, no comment duty; coder has sandbox rules + dry_run mandate, no comment key; legacy prompt unchanged

title: Extract generic stage runner from runtime.py (pure refactor, zero behavior change)
description:
## Why

`src/agent/runtime.py` contains battle-tested machinery for running one bounded ReAct loop: build agent, stream on a worker thread under a wall-clock timeout, handle GraphRecursionError, make a forced tools-disabled final call, parse JSON to a pydantic model, never raise. The three-agent pipeline needs this PER STAGE with different output models. Extract it into a generic, reusable stage runner. PURE REFACTOR ŌĆö zero behavior change.

## What

New `src/agent/stage_runner.py`:

```python
def run_stage(
    system_prompt: str,
    user_content,            # str or content blocks (image support for the intent stage)
    tools: list,
    model,                   # chat model (from agent.llm.get_chat_model)
    output_model: type[M],   # pydantic model to parse the final answer into
    *,
    timeout: float,
    max_steps: int,          # recursion/tool-call cap
    forced_call_timeout: float = 30.0,
    config: dict | None = None,
) -> M | None:               # None = total failure; NEVER raises
```

Extract from runtime.py:
- The build/stream-on-worker-thread/timeout machinery and GraphRecursionError handling (~lines 261-337).
- The forced tools-disabled final call (`_forced_final_result`, line 303 area) generalized to any output model.
- JSON parsing: generalize `_extract_json_object`'s hardcoded `"verdict"` key sniff (runtime.py:218) to "any required key of the target output_model" (derive from `output_model.model_fields`).
- Also MOVE `_validate_or_repair_effect` (line 403), `_repair_effect` (line 370), and `_effect_validation_error` here ŌĆö they are the coder stage's validate/repair loop. runtime.py keeps importing them from stage_runner (re-export or direct import) so existing call sites/tests still resolve.

Re-express the existing single-agent path in runtime.py through `run_stage(output_model=InterpretResult)` so there is ONE copy of the machinery.

## Acceptance criterion (the whole point)

The FULL `tests/test_agent_skeleton.py` suite passes UNMODIFIED ŌĆö happy path, recursion-cap forced-final (line 174), timeout, forced-final sanitization (line 343), repair-once (174/193), fallback degradation. That suite is the regression harness proving zero behavior change. Also: tests/test_layering.py green (stage_runner imports no board), full suite + ruff green.

## Notes

- Keep `_fallback_result` and the InterpretResult-specific fallback comments in runtime.py ŌĆö stage_runner returns None on failure and lets callers decide the fallback (each pipeline stage has its own degradation).
- Image-rejection detection (`_is_image_rejection`, line 90; retry at line 648) can stay in runtime.py for now ŌĆö the pipeline bead wires it to the intent stage.
acceptance_criteria: run_stage(output_model=M) never raises; legacy path re-expressed through it; tests/test_agent_skeleton.py passes UNMODIFIED; layering test green

title: Inter-stage contract models: CardIntent, PlanStep, MechanicsPlan in agent/contract.py
description:
## Why

The three-agent pipeline needs typed inter-stage artifacts: the intent stage emits a `CardIntent`, the planner emits a `MechanicsPlan`, the coder consumes both. They live in `src/agent/contract.py` ŌĆö the dependency-light shared-contract module (imports ONLY models.* + pydantic; that constraint is stated in its docstring and must hold).

## What

Add to `src/agent/contract.py` (purely additive):

```python
class CardIntent(BaseModel):
    summary: str                    # 1-2 sentences: what the player wants the card to do
    effects: list[str] = []         # discrete intended effects, in order
    targets: str = ""               # who/what is affected, plain terms
    persistence: Literal["immediate", "persistent", "reaction", "validation"] = "immediate"
    resolved_references: list[str] = []  # e.g. "trample (MTG): excess combat damage carries over -> here: excess point loss spills to next player"
    ambiguity: Literal["clear", "ambiguous", "undecipherable"] = "clear"
    complexity: Literal["trivial", "standard", "complex"] = "standard"
    comment: str = ""               # in-character persona remark ŌĆö OWNED by the intent stage
    persona_action: Literal["none", "do_nothing", "punish_author", "chaos_monkey", "random_solution"] = "none"

class PlanStep(BaseModel):
    kind: Literal["ops", "snippet", "interaction"]
    description: str                # what this step accomplishes
    engine_ops: list[str] = []      # op names for kind="ops" (e.g. ["add_points"])
    snippet_outline: str = ""       # pseudocode / state-manipulation sketch for kind="snippet"
    interaction: str = ""           # prose spec of the interaction request

class MechanicsPlan(BaseModel):
    strategy: str                   # prose: overall approach and why
    steps: list[PlanStep] = []
    trigger: str | None = None      # mirrors SnippetEffect.trigger (None | GameEvent | "on_reaction")
    scope: Literal["player", "center"] = "center"
    feasible: bool = True
    infeasible_reason: str = ""     # e.g. "needs a visual dice-roll UI the frontend lacks"
```

Design intent (state in docstrings): the plan is a PROMPT ARTIFACT consumed only by the coder ŌĆö prose-heavy, loosely typed. It must NOT become a second effects DSL that can fail validation; keep fields permissive with defaults so partial LLM output still parses.

`persona_action` Literal must match `InterpretResult.persona_action` (contract.py:91) exactly.

## Tests

Round-trip tests in `tests/test_interpret_contract.py`: construct ŌåÆ model_dump ŌåÆ model_validate; defaults tolerate minimal payloads (`CardIntent(summary="x")`, `MechanicsPlan(strategy="y")`); persona_action vocab matches InterpretResult's.

## Acceptance

Models importable from `agent.contract` with models-only imports preserved; tests green; ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: Models importable from agent.contract with models-only imports; permissive defaults parse minimal payloads; persona_action vocab matches InterpretResult; round-trip tests green

title: MTG glossary: mtg_lookup resolves rules terms (trample, split second), not just card names
description:
## Why

Players write MTG rules terms on cards ("split second", "trample") ŌĆö these carry precise intent, but `mtg_lookup` (src/agent/tools/mtg_lookup.py) only looks up CARDS by name via Scryfall `cards/named?fuzzy=` (line 30), so rules terms 404 (`_NOT_FOUND_TEMPLATE`, line 46). The intent agent needs to resolve rules/keyword terminology to meaning.

Options evaluated (decision made ŌĆö do not re-derive):
- Scryfall catalogs (`/catalog/keyword-abilities` etc.): names only, no definitions. Rejected.
- Scryfall search scraping reminder text: indirect, flaky, extra network. Rejected.
- **Bundled glossary data file: CHOSEN** ŌĆö deterministic, offline, testable, zero latency.

## What

1. New `data/mtg_glossary.json`: `{term: definition}` for the ~200 MTG keyword abilities, keyword actions, and ability words, definitions condensed from the MTG Comprehensive Rules glossary. Generated once by a new `scripts/build_mtg_glossary.py` (one-shot generator; document the snapshot provenance the same way `data/game_rules.txt` is documented at src/agent/tools/game_rules.py:14).

2. Extend `_lookup` in `src/agent/tools/mtg_lookup.py` (ONE tool, not a second one ŌĆö the model can't pick the wrong tool and the intent agent's tool count stays flat):
   - First: exact then fuzzy glossary match (`difflib.get_close_matches`) when the query matches a known term ŌåÆ return `"{term} (MTG keyword): {definition}"`.
   - Else: Scryfall card lookup as today.
   - On Scryfall 404: glossary fuzzy match as fallback before returning the not-found string.
   - Glossary loaded once at module level, cached; missing/corrupt file degrades silently to today's behavior. Never raises (same contract as today).

3. Update the `@tool` docstring (line 118): "...by card name, or an MTG rules keyword/term ('trample', 'split second')."

## Tests

Extend `tests/test_tool_mtg_lookup.py`: glossary exact hit; fuzzy hit ("tramplle"); Scryfall-404 ŌåÆ glossary fallback; missing glossary file degrades to current behavior. Existing tests stay green.

## Acceptance

`mtg_lookup("trample")` returns a definition without network; card lookups unchanged; never raises; full suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: mtg_lookup('trample') returns a definition offline; card lookups unchanged; 404 falls back to glossary fuzzy match; never raises; suite + ruff green

title: HELP MODE: struggling-author context in the agent prompt (no phrasing tips)
description:
## Why

When a player's authored cards keep failing, the agent should be told the author is struggling and try HARDER to make their card work ŌĆö interpret generously, prefer any plausible reading over rejection. This is the prompt-side half of escalating help (the boon ladder is the mechanical half).

## What

### 1. Compute struggling status ŌĆö `src/agent/runtime.py`

In `run_agent` (~line 546, after settings are loaded):

```python
author_fallbacks = 0
if state is not None and creator_id:
    from engine.history import fallback_counts  # agent may import engine (never board)
    author_fallbacks = fallback_counts(state).get(creator_id, 0)
threshold = settings.struggling_author_threshold
struggling_author = bool(threshold) and author_fallbacks >= threshold
```

Pass `struggling_author=struggling_author, author_fallbacks=author_fallbacks` into `build_system_prompt` (call at ~line 549). Threshold logic stays OUT of persona.py ŌĆö persona.py must remain config-free (its stated constraint, lines 3-6: imports only `models` + stdlib).

The preview path benefits automatically: `preview_state` is a deep copy carrying the same `history_events`.

### 2. Prompt block ŌĆö `src/agent/persona.py`

New module-level constant (module constant so the future three-agent pipeline's intent stage can reuse it verbatim):

```python
STRUGGLING_AUTHOR_NOTE = """\
HELP MODE: this card's author has already had {n} card(s) fail to work. They are
almost certainly still learning how to phrase cards, not trolling you. TRY HARDER:
re-read the card assuming best intent, prefer chaos_monkey (a generous, plausible
reading) over giving up, and only return "invalid" if you truly cannot construct
any effect. Stay witty ŌĆö but aim the wit at the cosmos, not at this player.
"""
```

Add keyword-only params to `build_system_prompt` (line 218): `struggling_author: bool = False, author_fallbacks: int = 0` ŌĆö defaults keep every existing call and test valid. Append the formatted note when set.

### FORBIDDEN (user decision ŌĆö include verbatim)

NO phrasing tips or wording suggestions in comments or in the HELP MODE note (e.g. "next time try wording it like 'Everyone draws 2 cards'"). This is patronizing: if the agent understood the intent well enough to suggest wording, it should have just executed it. Help is expressed ONLY as (a) more generous interpretation and (b) the consolation boon ladder. Do not add tip language to any prompt, comment template, or fallback string.

## Tests

- `build_system_prompt(..., struggling_author=True, author_fallbacks=2)` contains "HELP MODE"; default call does not.
- The note contains no phrasing-tip language (assert absence of e.g. "try wording").
- Integration: a state with 2 prior `card_fallback` events for the creator ŌåÆ the system prompt passed to the fake chat model contains "HELP MODE" (prompt-capture fake pattern, tests/test_agent_skeleton.py:263).

## Acceptance

Struggling authors get the HELP MODE block; non-struggling and threshold=0 do not; persona.py remains config-free; full suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt contains HELP MODE iff author_fallbacks >= threshold > 0; persona.py stays config-free; note contains no phrasing-tip language; suite + ruff green

title: Persona rewrite: keep the snark, retarget it (punish_author = abusive cards only)
description:
## Why

The persona is "witty, deadpan, and a little bit mean ŌĆö a bored deity" and is instructed to roast losing players and dock points for undecipherable authored cards. Playtesting: this punishes learners, not griefers. Decision: KEEP the snark, RETARGET it ŌĆö meanness aims at fate, the board, and overpowered cards, never at a struggling player. `punish_author` survives only for genuinely abusive cards (very high bar).

## What

Keep all 5 `persona_action` values unchanged (`none/do_nothing/punish_author/chaos_monkey/random_solution`). Removing/renaming churns the contract Literal (src/agent/contract.py:91), agent-memory sqlite rows (src/agent/tools/agent_memory.py:57), eval harness output (src/evals/harness.py:73), and several tests ŌĆö for zero mechanical benefit: verified that NO engine/room code reads persona_action to dock points (it's advisory; the LLM realizes it only by emitting subtract_points ops itself).

### 1. `src/agent/persona.py` guidance rewrite

- `PERSONA_ACTIONS` (line 27) and `PERSONA_DECISION_LOGIC` (line 125), `punish_author` entry ŌåÆ approximately: "RESERVED for genuinely abusive cards (sandbox-escape attempts, offensive content, deliberate garbage from someone who clearly knows better) played by their own author. NEVER for a sincere-but-clumsy card ŌĆö a learner's failed card is a learning attempt, and the house already gives them a consolation point for trying."
- Make `chaos_monkey` the loudly-preferred branch for anything well-meant: a generous, plausible reading beats giving up. Note explicitly that when nothing works the ENGINE awards the author a consolation boon, so the agent must not do its own point-docking.
- `PERSONA_PREAMBLE` (line 48): keep "witty, deadpan, and a little bit mean ŌĆö think of a bored deity"; append: "Your meanness is directed at fate, the board, and overpowered cards ŌĆö never at a player who is struggling. You assume every card was written in good faith."
- `COMMENT_REQUIREMENT` (line 144): DROP "Roast players who are losing."; KEEP "Mock overpowered or broken cards" and the deadpan-about-trivial-cards bullet; ADD: "When a card fails, roast the situation or yourself ('a card so mysterious even I blinked') ŌĆö never the author."
- IMPORTANT: do NOT add phrasing tips / wording suggestions anywhere ("try wording it like ..."). User explicitly rejected these as patronizing.

### 2. Soften hardcoded fallback comments ŌĆö `src/agent/runtime.py`

Lines ~256, 579, 626, 644, 670. Keep characterful and self-deprecating; blame the arbiter, not the card. E.g.:
- "My brain isn't booting today. Consider yourself lucky." ŌåÆ "My brain isn't booting today. That one's on me, not your card."
- "Figuring out your card took so long I lost interest. Nothing happens." ŌåÆ "Your card sent me on a journey I wasn't prepared for."
- "I went in circles trying to make sense of that. I give up." ŌåÆ "I went in circles trying to honor that card. It defeated me fairly."
- "Something broke, and I'm choosing to blame that card." ŌåÆ "Something broke on my end. Your card is legally innocent."

### 3. `src/agent/tools/read_game_state.py`

Lines 5, 91, 118, 211 explain authorship as "punish_author is on the table for a dumb/undecipherable card". Reword to the new framing: authorship decides who receives the consolation boon and whether the rare abusive-card branch even applies. KEEP the literal string "punish_author" in the tool's authorship note so tests/test_seam_cluster.py:84-101 and 292-310 assertions stand (or update those assertions in the same commit). Optional: include `author_fallback_count` in the tool payload (it already receives the state snapshot; use `engine.history.fallback_counts`).

### 4. `src/evals/game_fixtures.py:13-15, 25`

Update docstrings ("do_nothing vs punish_author" framing) to the new philosophy. The actor-!=-author fixture stays valuable (now exercises "who gets the consolation boon"). Harness/scorers need NO code change (vocabulary unchanged; verified `data/eval/*.json` inputs carry no persona expectations ŌĆö only archived run outputs mention persona_action).

## Acceptance

- tests/test_agent_skeleton.py:111-128 passes UNCHANGED (vocabulary kept; the `"punish_author" in prompt` assertion at 128 still holds).
- tests/test_seam_cluster.py read_game_state assertions green (term kept or assertions updated in-commit).
- No prompt text anywhere instructs roasting losing players or docking points for clumsy cards; no phrasing-tip language introduced.
- Full test suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: No prompt roasts losing players or docks points for clumsy cards; punish_author high-bar wording; test_agent_skeleton 111-128 unchanged; no phrasing-tip language; suite + ruff green

title: Room-level consolation boon ladder at the four fallback sites (+1 for trying, escalating boons)
description:
## Why

Today a card that can't be made to work resolves as a bare `CustomNoteOp` no-op ŌĆö the author gets nothing. New philosophy: assume best intent and throw them a bone ŌĆö "+1 point for trying", escalating to bigger creative boons for players whose cards keep failing.

## What

New helper on Room (`src/board/rooms/room.py`):

```python
def _consolation_ops(self, card, card_id: str) -> list[Op]:
    """Ops for a card that couldn't be made to work: a visible note, plus a
    consolation boon to the AUTHOR 'for trying' (authored cards only, author
    still seated, consolation_point_enabled on)."""
```

Boon ladder, driven by `engine.history.fallback_counts(self.state)` for the author ŌĆö let n = the author's count INCLUDING this failure (the `card_fallback` event is appended by `_set_card_mechanical_status` before the plan is built, so read the count after that, or +1 accordingly ŌĆö verify ordering when implementing):

- n below `struggling_author_threshold`: `AddPointsOp(target=f"id:{creator_id}", amount=settings.consolation_points)` ŌĆö "+1 for trying".
- n >= threshold (escalating help; rotate by `(n - threshold) % 3`):
  1. `AddPointsOp(+2)`
  2. `DrawCardsOp` for 3 cards to the author
  3. one-shot score double: `SetPointsOp` setting the author to 2x their current score (read current score from `self.state`)
- Plus always a `CustomNoteOp` like `"Played {title} (no mechanical effect ŌĆö {boon description} to {author_name} for trying)"`. MUST keep the substring `"no mechanical effect"` ŌĆö existing test assertions match on it (e.g. tests/test_snippet_play_resolution.py:65's negative assertion semantics).
- Degrade to today's bare `CustomNoteOp(note=f"Played {title} (no mechanical effect)")` when: `consolation_point_enabled=False`, card has no `creator_id` (seed/blank cards), or author no longer seated.

A persistent "double all their gains" `register_hook` boon is explicitly OUT of scope (on_score_change hook recursion risk); may be a follow-up bead.

### Call sites (replace the bare CustomNoteOp construction)

1. `_resolve_plan` fallback, room.py:1235-1236 ŌåÆ `return ResolutionPlan(steps=[OpsStep(ops=self._consolation_ops(card, card_id))])`
2. `_finish_play` interaction-setup failure, room.py:1584-1587
3. `_finish_play` execution failure, room.py:1612
4. `_fail_pending_resolution`, room.py:2098

Also soften the public `mechanical_reason` strings at room.py:1201, 1226, 2588 ŌĆö e.g. "The arbiter couldn't build this one yet ŌĆö the author gets a point for trying." (Keep private/triage diagnostics at 1578/1602 as-is.)

## Why ROOM level, not inside the agent's `_fallback_result`

- The preview path (`_handle_preview_card`, room.py:2539-2643, run_agent call at 2575) reuses `run_agent` against a cloned state ŌĆö an agent-level AddPointsOp would DISPLAY phantom points in previews.
- Agent-level coverage is incomplete: `_fallback_result` only fires on timeout/recursion/error; it misses the LLM returning verdict="invalid" with no program, verdict="ok" with an empty plan (room.py:1219), and execution-time failures (room.py:1602, 1578, 2079). The room's fallback sites cover all of these.
- Deterministic: it must work precisely when the agent has failed.
- Survives the three-agent pipeline refactor (epic 2 replaces runtime internals; `Room._resolve_plan`'s contract is stable).

## Mechanics notes

- `AddPointsOp(target=f"id:{creator_id}")` resolves via `_resolve_targets` (src/engine/reducers.py:59) ŌĆö works even when actor != author.
- The ops flow through `apply_effect`, so `record_op_history` auto-records a public `score_change` event and score broadcasts just work.
- Self-limiting: one boon per failed play; not farmable at scale (max one per turn).
- Do NOT touch `engine/facade.py:93` (pure-engine non-LLM fallback path).

## Tests

Update (verified assertion sites):
- tests/test_play_resolution.py:93 (`score == 0` after agent failure ŌåÆ author p1 now +1) and :104-109 (invalid-verdict test ŌĆö add score assertion); log-line assertions at 95/109 keep matching via the preserved "no mechanical effect" substring.
- tests/test_room_agent_context.py:86-89 (score 0 ŌåÆ 1).
- tests/test_snippet_play_resolution.py:80-81, 109, 125-129, 148 ŌĆö sandbox-failure fallbacks: creator "p1" (fixture line 48 sets creator_id) now ends at +1; log assertions at 65/87/110/171 same substring caveat.

New:
- Boon goes to the AUTHOR including when actor != author.
- No boon for seed cards (no creator_id) / departed author / `consolation_point_enabled=False` (settings via `get_settings.cache_clear()` + env, config.py:165 pattern).
- Ladder escalates at threshold and rotates (+2 ŌåÆ draw 3 ŌåÆ score double).
- Preview: `_handle_preview_card` result contains no add_points op on failure.

## Acceptance

All four fallback sites award via `_consolation_ops`; previews clean; updated + new tests green; full suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: All four fallback sites award via _consolation_ops; ladder escalates at threshold (+2 / draw 3 / score double); previews show no phantom points; updated+new tests green

title: Config + per-author rejection tracking via card_fallback history events
description:
## Why

To help struggling players we must know who is struggling. Track how many times each player's AUTHORED cards failed interpretation/execution, derived from the history ledger, plus the Settings knobs the rest of the epic consumes.

## What

### 1. Settings ŌĆö `src/config.py`

Add after the `triage_agent_*` block (~line 101), following the existing commented-field pattern:

```python
# --- New-player kindness ---
# Award the card's author a consolation boon when their card can't be made to work.
consolation_point_enabled: bool = True
consolation_points: int = Field(default=1, ge=0)
# After this many of a player's authored cards have failed, escalate boons and
# tell the agent the author is struggling (HELP MODE). 0 disables escalation.
struggling_author_threshold: int = Field(default=2, ge=0)
```

### 2. History kind ŌĆö `src/models/game_state.py:13`

Extend the Literal: `HistoryKind = Literal[..., "card_fallback"]` (add to the existing members). Additive ŌĆö old persisted states still validate (Literal only constrains new events).

### 3. Counter helper ŌĆö `src/engine/history.py`

Add (and export in `__all__`), mirroring `draw_totals` (line 153):

```python
def fallback_counts(state: GameState) -> dict[str, int]:
    """Per-player count of authored-card interpretation/execution failures.

    Keyed by the card AUTHOR (event.target_player_ids), mirroring draw_totals.
    """
```

Count events with `kind == "card_fallback"`, summing over `target_player_ids`. Games are short; an O(events) scan per interpretation is fine.

### 4. Record at the choke point ŌĆö `src/board/rooms/room.py`

`Room._set_card_mechanical_status` (room.py:996-1013) is already called with `status="fallback"` at EVERY failure site (1222, 1578, 1602, 2079). Inside it, when `status == "fallback"`, append the ledger event before `_notify_change()`:

```python
if status == "fallback":
    creator_id = card.get("creator_id")
    if creator_id and any(p.id == creator_id for p in self.state.players):
        # append a card_fallback history event:
        # actor_id=creator_id, target_player_ids=[creator_id], card_id=card_id
```

Use the existing history-event append mechanism (see `GameState.with_history_event` / how other events are appended in room.py). Guards: only authored cards (seed/blank cards have no `creator_id`) and only authors still seated. Statuses "rejected"/"countered" (room.py:1403, 1546, 1551) deliberately do NOT count ŌĆö a countered card isn't a learning failure.

## Why derived-from-ledger (not a Player field / conditions)

- `Player.conditions` is card-writable via `set_condition` (open `id:`/`has:` targeting) ŌĆö a card could tamper with or clobber the counter; `engine.loop` consumes reserved keys. Wrong bag for engine bookkeeping.
- `history_events` already persist (store.py full model_dump), survive reconnects, ride every snapshot, and flow to the agent via the `read_game_history` tool (`public_history` passes any `kind` string through, engine/history.py:147).

## Acceptance

- `card_fallback` event appended on `status="fallback"` only (not "rejected"/"countered"); no event for cards without `creator_id` or departed authors.
- `fallback_counts` sums correctly across multiple events/players.
- `public_history(kind="card_fallback")` filters correctly.
- New settings readable via `get_settings()`; tests use the `get_settings.cache_clear()` + env pattern (config.py:165).
- Full test suite + ruff green.
acceptance_criteria: card_fallback recorded only on status=fallback for seated authors; fallback_counts sums per author; new Settings fields readable; full suite + ruff green

title: Three-agent LangGraph pipeline: intent -> planner -> coder
description:
## Why

The card interpreter is a single LangChain ReAct agent (`src/agent/runtime.py:run_agent`) that fuses three jobs: understanding what the player wants, deciding what to do about it, and writing the code. Splitting these into specialized stages should improve interpretation quality (docs/WRITEUP.md Task 7 already sketches exactly this pipeline). This is also a class project: **demonstrating a LangGraph agentic StateGraph is a requirement**, not an implementation detail.

## Target architecture

An outer LangGraph `StateGraph` (in new `src/agent/pipeline.py`) with nodes intent ŌåÆ planner ŌåÆ coder ŌåÆ validate_repair ŌåÆ finalize, where each stage node invokes a bounded ReAct agent (built with the existing `create_agent`) as a subgraph with its own recursion_limit, and **conditional edges** encode the failure/degradation branches.

- **Intent agent** ŌĆö what does the player want? Tools: web_search, mtg_lookup (extended with an MTG rules-term glossary), card_rag_hybrid, game_rules, read_game_state. Owns the persona `comment` + `persona_action`.
- **Planner agent** ŌĆö given this intent, what do we do? Tools: game_rules, read_engine_methods, read_game_state, read_game_history, card_rag_hybrid. Must be CREATIVE: when no single op expresses a mechanic ("target player discards a random card"), plan direct state manipulation via SandboxGame (zone moves are pop-from-one-list/push-to-another via move_card-backed reducers). Emits a `MechanicsPlan`.
- **Coder agent** ŌĆö turn the plan into working ops/snippets. Tools: dry_run_effect, read_engine_methods, read_game_state. Owns the validate/repair loop.

## Invariants

- Everything stays behind the existing `run_agent` signature / `InterpretResult` contract ŌĆö room.py call sites (src/board/rooms/room.py:1172 and ~2576) unchanged. Rollout behind a config flag; legacy single-agent path retained for A/B and rollback.
- The deterministic `compile_card_plan` short-circuit (room.py:1140) is untouched.
- Never-raise guarantee preserved end to end (today's `_fallback_result` degradation).
- `agent` layer never imports `board` (tests/test_layering.py).
- Never write `except (A, B):` ŌĆö ruff formatter corrupts the parens.

## Children

1. MTG glossary for rules terms in mtg_lookup
2. Inter-stage contract models (CardIntent, MechanicsPlan)
3. Extract generic stage runner (pure refactor)
4. Stage prompts module (split persona.py)
5. LangGraph orchestrator graph
6. Config flag, run_agent dispatch, eval A/B
7. (optional) learner-phrased eval cards

title: Kindness overhaul: assume best intent, consolation boons, HELP MODE
description:
## Why

Playtesting showed the "mean deity" persona punishes learners: new players don't know the right lingo, and their failed cards get snark + nothing (or point docking) instead of help. The meanness was designed to punish griefers, but in practice it hits people trying to learn the game.

## Goal (user decisions, do not relitigate)

- KEEP the snark, DROP the punishment: the witty/deadpan/mean voice stays, but its meanness targets fate, the board, and overpowered cards ŌĆö never a struggling player. Assume every card was written in good faith.
- Cards that can't be made to work earn the author a consolation "+1 for trying" instead of a pure no-op.
- Track per-player rejection counts; past a threshold, escalate help: bigger/creative boons (extra points, draw 3 cards, one-shot score double) AND a "try harder" HELP MODE prompt telling the agent to interpret generously.
- NEVER phrasing tips in comments ("try wording it like..."). Patronizing ŌĆö a player will rightly ask "if you understood my intent, why didn't you just do it?" Help is expressed ONLY as more generous interpretation and boons.
- `punish_author` survives ONLY for genuinely abusive cards (sandbox-escape attempts, offensive content, deliberate garbage from someone who clearly knows better). Very high bar.

## Children

1. Config + rejection tracking via `card_fallback` history events
2. Room-level consolation boon ladder at the four fallback sites
3. Persona rewrite: keep snark, retarget it
4. HELP MODE: struggling-author context in the agent prompt

## Shared constraints

- `agent` layer never imports `board` (enforced by tests/test_layering.py).
- Never write `except (A, B):` in this repo ŌĆö the ruff formatter corrupts the parens.
- Do NOT touch `engine/facade.py:93` ŌĆö that's the pure-engine (non-LLM) fallback path; awarding points there would change deterministic-engine semantics.

title: Author ~30 new gold seed cards targeting capability gaps (seed-gold-029 through seed-gold-058)
description:
WHY: The RAG corpus teaches the agent what cards can do. A measured gap analysis over all 69 current canonicals against the 22-op union in src/models/effects.py:423-449 shows large blind spots. User decision: ALL ~30 new cards are GOLD (data/seed_cards_gold.json) - do NOT add filler cards.

MEASURED GAP LIST (target these):
- Ops with ZERO usage: set_points, unregister_hook.
- create_card appears only inside steps (seed-gold-019/023), never as a plain op.
- Ops with 1-2 uses: steal_points, reverse_order, scramble_order, counter_play, set_card_attribute, destroy_card, end_game, extra_turn.
- Ops with 3-5 uses: change_draw_count, subtract_points, transfer_card (fillers only), draw_cards, skip_turn, set_win_condition.
- Triggers: 63/69 cards have trigger null. GameEvent enum (src/engine/events.py:15-29) events with NO exemplar: on_turn_end, on_draw_step, on_score_change, on_validate_play.
- Choice targets: only ~4 cards use chosen_card or chosen_player in ops - this is the RAG material for the 13 needs_choice eval cards, badly thin.
- Rich player targets validated by src/models/effects.py:30-45 but nearly unused: player_with_most_points, player_with_least_points, player_with_empty_hand, right_neighbor, next_player, left_neighbor (1 use), has:<condition> forms.
- Few genuine 3+ mechanical-op chains.

COVERAGE TARGETS FOR THE 30 CARDS (one capability anchor each, spread the rest):
1. set_points absolute reset (e.g. everyone score becomes 10)
2. register_hook + unregister_hook pair (enact then repeal a house rule)
3. steal_points with from_target=player_with_most_points (rob the rich)
4. a second counter_play reaction card (mode steal_hand or redirect, trigger on_reaction)
5. register_hook on on_score_change (e.g. 1-point tax on every gain)
6. register_hook on on_draw_step
7. register_hook on on_turn_end
8. register_hook on on_validate_play (a play-restriction / reject_play exemplar)
9. create_card as a PLAIN op (destination hand or deck_top)
10. genuine multi-op chain with 3+ mechanical ops (e.g. draw_cards + add_points + set_condition)
11. dual-choice card: transfer_card card_target=chosen_card PLUS subtract_points target=chooser (both choice axes in one plan)
12. end_game with winners, or set_win_condition kind=first_to
13-30. several more choice-based cards (chosen_player, chosen_card), more multi-op chains, cards spreading extra_turn / reverse_order / scramble_order / change_draw_count / destroy_card / set_card_attribute, rich targets (neighbors, most/least points, has:<condition> paired with an earlier set_condition), 1-2 trigger on_game_end cards, placement=player and placement=center examples.

AUTHORING WORKFLOW:
1. First write a 30-row spec table (id, title, one-line effect, capability tags from the gap list) as the review artifact - include it in the PR/commit description.
2. Draft each card JSON from the spec, using existing cards as few-shot style guides: seed-gold-002 (choice ops), seed-filler-015 (chosen_card chain), seed-gold-019/023 (steps). Match tone: short punchy titles, playful descriptions.
3. Ops MUST use the authoring shape {"op": ..., "args": {...}} (flattened by _flatten_op_args, src/models/effects.py:480-502) and the authoring target alias vocabulary (alias table at src/models/effects.py:131-147 - e.g. chosen_player, next_player).
4. Card object shape (copy from existing gold cards): {id, title, description, alt_text, canonical: {target, placement, venue, trigger, ops, sandbox}}.
5. IDs: sequential zero-padded seed-gold-029 ... seed-gold-058 (000-028 are taken; convention per scripts/data_prep/migrate_card_schema.py:37-39).
6. alt_text: null for every card (all 69 existing seeds have null alt_text). venue: all unless the card genuinely requires physical presence (then in_person); online only if digital-only. Description prose must LITERALLY match the mechanics - the LLM judge scorers compare prose to plan.
7. Every card needs a sandbox field: a def apply(state, ctx): snippet behaviorally equivalent to the ops. It must pass engine.sandbox.validate.validate_snippet. Spot-check equivalence via engine.sandbox.runner.execute_snippet against the fixture states in src/evals/fixtures.py.
8. Validate mechanically - NO new scripts needed, the lint parametrizes from the data files: uv run pytest tests/test_corpus_lint.py tests/test_seed_cards.py tests/test_gold_exemplars.py -q. Gold cards must compile (compile_card, zero drift warnings, tests/test_corpus_lint.py:61-67) AND behaviorally dry-run with non-empty emitted_ops (tests/test_corpus_lint.py:92-106 - the lint state passes chosen ids, so choice cards work).
9. After adding cards: regenerate the combined corpus with uv run python scripts/data_prep/build_seed_corpus.py (NEVER hand-edit data/seed_cards.json). Update hardcoded counts: tests/test_seed_cards.py line 19 (29 -> 59) and the combined-total assertions in tests/test_seed_cards.py line 63 and tests/test_rag_seed.py line 69 (add 30 to whatever the current combined total is - 101 if the include-simple-cards sibling task landed first, 69 otherwise).
10. Every card gets a human read-through before merge - these are RAG exemplars that teach the agent; a bad card teaches bad habits.

DEPENDS ON: the include-simple-cards task (shared combined-file counts) - coordinate the count bumps if executing out of order.

GOTCHAS: dev branch only, never merge to main. NEVER write except with a parenthesized tuple - ruff format corrupts it (relevant for sandbox snippets too). Do NOT re-run the seed eval - the user does that manually. Do NOT touch the no-op joke cards or the did_something metric - user decision to keep both as-is.
acceptance_criteria: data/seed_cards_gold.json contains 59 cards (029-058 new). Every new card: parses as GoldCard via parse_seed_card (src/models/card.py); full canonical incl. sandbox; compile_card emits zero drift warnings; behavioral dry-run returns ok=True with non-empty emitted_ops; sandbox passes validate_snippet. All coverage targets 1-12 present. Combined corpus regenerated via build_seed_corpus.py and --check exits 0. Count tests updated. Full uv run pytest passes, ruff clean. Spec table included in commit/PR description.
close_reason: Added 30 gold cards (seed-gold-029..058), gold 29->59, combined 131. All pass parse/compile/dry-run/validate_snippet lints. Docs updated. Merged to dev.

title: normalise_agent_output loses valid plans emitted in the comment field (harness bug 1)
description:
WHY: In seed eval run 20260714-173730, card seed-filler-006 (Point at the Ceiling) got verdict=invalid even though the agent produced a valid resolution plan - the plan arrived as raw JSON text in the comment field. Chain of failure: the model final-message JSON failed json.loads or the embedded-object scan in _extract_json_object (src/agent/runtime.py:197-221, which only accepts embedded dicts carrying a verdict key), so _parse_result fell back at src/agent/runtime.py:249 setting comment=text[:280]. normalise_agent_output (src/evals/harness.py:63-81) then found no plan and the row scored invalid. Fix the eval-side recovery here; the upstream runtime cause is a SEPARATE issue (see sibling task about runtime truncation).

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. In src/evals/harness.py add a module-level helper:
   def _recover_plan_from_comment(comment):
       """Best-effort: parse plan JSON the agent mistakenly emitted as prose. Returns (plan_or_None, embedded_verdict_or_None)."""
   Algorithm:
   a. Strip markdown code fences (lines starting with three backticks, optionally followed by json).
   b. Scan the text: from each opening brace position, try json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode - same technique as _extract_json_object in src/agent/runtime.py:197 - so leading/trailing prose is tolerated.
   c. For each candidate dict, try IN ORDER: payload["plan"], payload["resolution_plan"], the payload itself if it has a steps key, then InterpretResult.model_validate(payload).to_plan() when it looks contract-shaped (has plan/program/snippet keys). Validate candidates with ResolutionPlan.model_validate. Accept the FIRST result whose steps list is non-empty.
   d. On ANY exception at any stage, move on / return (None, None). This helper must never raise.
2. In normalise_agent_output (harness.py:63-81): ONLY when to_plan() produced no steps (the existing if plan.steps branch around line 79 not taken), call the helper on out comment. On success:
   - set out["resolution_plan"] = plan.model_dump()
   - set out["plan_recovered_from_comment"] = True
   - ONLY if the original verdict was invalid (i.e. the runtime fallback, not a genuine judgment): override out["verdict"] with the embedded payload verdict if present, else ok, and keep the original under out["raw_verdict"].
   - leave agent_error untouched so agent_error_rate (src/evals/runner.py:336) stays honest.
3. Recovery must NOT run when the agent produced a real plan - the real plan always wins.
4. Add ~6 tests to tests/test_harness.py:
   a. comment containing a bare {"plan": {...}} JSON object -> plan recovered.
   b. comment with a fenced json block surrounded by prose -> recovered.
   c. comment containing a {"steps": [...]} shaped object -> recovered.
   d. truncated/unclosed JSON -> no recovery, no exception, output unchanged.
   e. result already has a plan AND comment contains different plan JSON -> recovery skipped, original plan kept.
   f. verdict override happens only from invalid, raw_verdict and plan_recovered_from_comment markers set.

KNOWN LIMIT (note in commit message): the original seed-filler-006 failure is NOT recoverable post-hoc because runtime.py:249 truncates the comment to 280 chars - this fix helps future runs where the plan JSON fits (small ops-only plans do). The upstream truncation is tracked separately.

GOTCHAS: dev branch only. NEVER write except with a parenthesized tuple like except (ValueError, KeyError): - ruff format corrupts it in this repo; use separate except clauses (this helper will need exception handling, so this WILL come up). Do not re-run the seed eval.
acceptance_criteria: _recover_plan_from_comment exists in src/evals/harness.py, never raises, and normalise_agent_output uses it only when no real plan was produced. All ~6 new tests in tests/test_harness.py pass. Existing harness tests (e.g. test_normalise_omits_plan_when_no_effect) still pass. Full uv run pytest passes. ruff check and ruff format --check pass.
close_reason: Added _recover_plan_from_comment + 7 tests in test_harness. Merged to dev.

title: Eval fixture supplies no choice context - 13 needs_choice cards unscoreable (harness bug 2)
description:
WHY: In seed eval run 20260714-173730 (opus-4-8), 13 of 69 cards got verdict=needs_choice, and choice-target plans fail the executability/did_something dry-run with error: CardTarget chosen_card requires ctx.chosen_card_id. Example: seed-filler-015 Borrow a Card - even its GOLD canonical would fail. The eval fixture just never supplies choice ids. This is the single biggest legitimate score mover.

HOW PRODUCTION WORKS (verified, do not re-derive): client play messages carry chosen_player_id / chosen_card_id (src/models/ws_messages.py:91-92), validated in src/board/rooms/room.py:1441-1503, threaded into HookContext (src/engine/events.py:42-43). Reducers resolve chooser / target_player -> ctx.chosen_player_id (src/engine/reducers.py:82-84) and chosen_card -> ctx.chosen_card_id (src/engine/reducers.py:124-126), raising ValueError when absent. dry_run_resolution_plan ALREADY accepts chosen_player_id and chosen_card_id kwargs (src/agent/tools/dry_run_effect.py:47-55) and threads them into both HookContext and the sandbox ctx dict (lines 63-77). NO engine changes are needed. Precedent already in repo: the gold behavioral lint passes chosen_player_id and chosen_card_id (tests/test_corpus_lint.py:96-103), and the sandbox_behavior fixtures already carry them (src/evals/fixtures.py:74-75).

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Edit src/evals/game_fixtures.py: next to EVAL_ACTOR_ID (line 24) add two module constants:
   EVAL_CHOSEN_PLAYER_ID = "p2"   (Bob - exists in the fixture state and is NOT the actor, so pick-another-player semantics hold)
   EVAL_CHOSEN_CARD_ID = "hand-c" (a card in Bobs hand, present in the cards registry around line 56 - realistic for borrow/steal/destroy effects)
   Extend the module docstring: the deterministic choice context mirrors the production prompt_choice flow so choice-target plans are scoreable.
2. Edit src/evals/scorers.py around line 180 (_dry_run_output): the dry_run_resolution_plan call currently passes build_eval_state(), plan, EVAL_ACTOR_ID, EVAL_CARD_ID. Add keyword args chosen_player_id=EVAL_CHOSEN_PLAYER_ID, chosen_card_id=EVAL_CHOSEN_CARD_ID. Import the new constants where EVAL_ACTOR_ID is already imported (local import site around line 173).
3. Do NOT change src/evals/runner.py - the agent-side dry_run_effect tool already exposes chosen-id parameters the agent can fill itself.
4. Add tests to tests/test_scorers.py (existing scorer test classes are around lines 132-160):
   a. executability returns 1.0 for a plan containing a transfer_card op with card_target=chosen_card.
   b. did_something returns 1.0 for a plan containing add_points with target=chooser and output verdict=needs_choice.
   c. Regression: load seed-filler-015 (Borrow a Card) canonical from data/seed_cards.json, compile via engine.compile.compile_card_plan, and assert _dry_run_output reports ok=True.

SCORER IMPLICATIONS (put in commit message): executability (scorers.py:190) and did_something (scorers.py:212) share _dry_run_output, so both now pass for choice-target plans; needs_choice verdicts pass the verdict!=invalid check in did_something, so the 13 needs_choice cards become scoreable. Mild leniency: a plan that WRONGLY uses chooser/chosen_card on a non-choice card will now also execute - acceptable, the LLM judge scorers still catch intent mismatch. sandbox_behavior is unaffected (its fixtures already carry choice ids).

GOTCHAS: dev branch only. NEVER write except with a parenthesized tuple. Do NOT re-run the seed eval - user does that manually.
acceptance_criteria: New constants EVAL_CHOSEN_PLAYER_ID and EVAL_CHOSEN_CARD_ID exist in src/evals/game_fixtures.py and are passed to dry_run_resolution_plan in src/evals/scorers.py _dry_run_output. The three new tests in tests/test_scorers.py pass. Full uv run pytest passes. ruff check and ruff format --check pass.
close_reason: Added EVAL_CHOSEN_PLAYER_ID/EVAL_CHOSEN_CARD_ID to game_fixtures; threaded into _dry_run_output. 3 new tests. Merged to dev via feat/2jp.2-eval-choice-context.

title: Include simple cards in the combined RAG corpus (seed_cards.json = gold + fillers + simple)
description:
WHY: data/seed_cards.json is the RAG corpus loaded into Qdrant at app startup. It is currently gold (29) + fillers (40) = 69 cards. The 32 simple cards (data/seed_cards_simple.json) cover many common scenarios worth RAG-retrieving and evaluating against, so the user wants them included. New combined total: 101 cards.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Edit scripts/data_prep/build_seed_corpus.py (the combine logic is around lines 12-19). Add data/seed_cards_simple.json as a THIRD source. Deterministic output order must be: gold, then fillers, then simple. Update the module docstring. The --check drift mode (around lines 27-31) rebuilds and compares, so it should pick up the change automatically - verify it does.
2. Run: uv run python scripts/data_prep/build_seed_corpus.py
   This regenerates data/seed_cards.json. Confirm it now contains 101 cards (python3 -c "import json; print(len(json.load(open(chr(100)+chr(97)+chr(116)+chr(97)+chr(47)+chr(115)+chr(101)+chr(101)+chr(100)+chr(95)+chr(99)+chr(97)+chr(114)+chr(100)+chr(115)+chr(46)+chr(106)+chr(115)+chr(111)+chr(110))))" - or just open the file and check).
3. Edit tests/test_corpus_lint.py lines 109-114: the assertion that combined == gold + fillers must become combined == gold + fillers + simple.
4. Update hardcoded counts: tests/test_seed_cards.py line 63 (69 -> 101) and tests/test_rag_seed.py line 69 (69 -> 101). Grep both files for any other occurrence of 69 that refers to corpus size.
5. NEAR-DUPLICATE DECISION: seed-gold-000 and seed-simple-000 are both a Gain 5 Points card. IDs do not collide (seed-simple-NNN namespace) and RAG embeds title+description only (src/agent/rag/store.py _card_text line 60). RECOMMENDED: include all cards, no dedupe - a couple of near-duplicates only mildly bias retrieval. Document this choice in the commit message. If you find MANY exact title duplicates instead of a couple, stop and note it on this issue rather than inventing a dedupe scheme.
6. Do NOT touch src/board/rooms/deck.py - simple mode (_simple_card_source, SIMPLE_SEED_PATH lines 221-228) keeps reading data/seed_cards_simple.json directly and is unaffected.
7. Do NOT touch src/agent/rag/seed.py or src/board/app.py - they read the combined file and need no change.

GOTCHAS: work on dev branch only, never main. NEVER write except with a parenthesized tuple - ruff format corrupts it in this repo. After editing the data files, always regenerate via the script - never hand-edit data/seed_cards.json (it is a build artifact with a CI drift guard).
acceptance_criteria: uv run python scripts/data_prep/build_seed_corpus.py --check exits 0. data/seed_cards.json contains exactly 101 cards in order gold, fillers, simple. uv run pytest tests/test_corpus_lint.py tests/test_seed_cards.py tests/test_rag_seed.py -q passes. Full uv run pytest passes. uv run ruff check . and uv run ruff format --check . pass. Dedupe decision documented in commit message.
close_reason: build_seed_corpus.py now includes simple deck (101 cards). Tests derive counts from sources. Docs updated. No dedup (4 tiny overlaps, documented). Merged to dev.

title: Improve and expand the seed-card corpus
description:
WHY: Investigation of data/eval/runs/20260714-173730_seed_us-anthropic-claude-opus-4-8.json showed the seed cards are NOT broken. The alarming ~80% figure is the did_something scorer (0.7971); actual executability is 0.9565 (only 3/69 cards fail to produce a runnable plan, none of them genuinely broken generated code).

Breakdown of the 14 did_something failures:
(1) 10 correct no-op joke cards (e.g. seed-gold-018 Nothing Happens Officially, seed-filler-005 Invisible Card) whose gold canonical is itself only a custom_note. did_something excludes non-mechanical ops by design (src/evals/scorers.py:209-225, _NON_MECHANICAL_EMITTED). DECISION (user): leave both the cards and the metric as-is. Do NOT change them.
(2) 3 verdict=invalid cards. One (seed-filler-006 Point at the Ceiling) is a real harness bug: the agent emitted a valid resolution plan as raw JSON inside the comment field and normalise_agent_output lost it.
(3) Choice-target cards (e.g. seed-filler-015 Borrow a Card) fail dry-run with error: CardTarget chosen_card requires ctx.chosen_card_id - the eval fixture supplies no choice context. 13 of 69 cards got verdict=needs_choice.

SCOPE (user decisions, fixed): fix both harness bugs; add the 32 simple cards into the combined RAG corpus (seed_cards.json becomes gold + fillers + simple); author ~30 NEW GOLD cards targeting capability gaps (NO new filler cards - existing fillers already skew in-person in tone); investigation-only tasks for sandbox_behavior=0.42 and the upstream runtime truncation.

DATASET ROLES: data/seed_cards_gold.json = 29 hand-reviewed executable exemplars. data/seed_cards_fillers.json = 40 flavor/social cards. data/seed_cards.json = combined RAG corpus loaded into Qdrant at startup (src/board/app.py:155-157 via src/agent/rag/seed.py load_seed_cards), mechanically built by scripts/data_prep/build_seed_corpus.py with CI drift guard. data/seed_cards_simple.json = 32 point-only cards for the no-AI game mode (src/board/rooms/deck.py:221-228) - stays as its own file.

RULES FOR ALL CHILD TASKS: work on the dev branch ONLY, never merge to main (homework repo policy). Do NOT re-run the seed eval - the user runs evals manually. Repo gotcha: NEVER write except with a parenthesized tuple like except (A, B): - ruff format corrupts it in this repo; use separate except clauses.

title: Prod: set DEV_MODE=false on Render service (unauthenticated dev endpoints live)
description: Smoke probe returned HTTP 200 from POST /rooms/{code}/dev/skip-setup on https://a-thousand-blank-white-cards.onrender.com, proving DEV_MODE=true was copied from local .env into the Render environment. Dev endpoints (skip-setup, end-game) mutate any room unauthenticated, and dev_mode also switches to file-backed room persistence. Fix: Render dashboard -> service Environment -> set DEV_MODE=false or delete the var (default is false); Render auto-redeploys. Verify: same probe returns 404.
close_reason: Won't fix by choice: DEV_MODE stays true in prod deliberately ŌĆö the dev skip-setup/end-game endpoints are demo conveniences. Verified live 2026-07-15 (dev endpoint returns 200 on a real room). Exposure documented in docs/deploy/render-steps.md footgun #2; flip to false when demos are done.

description: src/board/rooms/room.py: add _report_effect_failure(kind, card, correlation_id, *, exc=None, verdict=None, comment=None) ŌĆö non-blocking, never raises: return if not eval_agent_enabled; dedupe on (card_id, kind) via self._reported_failures when eval_agent_dedupe; build EffectFailurePayload (state_summary via read_game_state._summarize_state, history_summary via engine.history.public_history+draw_totals, run_metrics via self._last_run_metrics.pop(card_id, None)); lazy import schedule_effect_failure_report and fire. Wire trigger sites: _resolve_plan (~1098) no_op/invalid_verdict; _finish_play generic except (~1460) sandbox_failure, interaction-setup except (~1437) interaction_setup (NOT PlanPaused). Wrap _emit_hooks (and _check_play_veto) with collect_hook_errors and report hook_failure per error; thread correlation_id through _emit_hooks. RunMetrics: attach UsageCallback at run_agent call in _resolve_plan, store asdict(snapshot) in self._last_run_metrics keyed by card_id; init _reported_failures/_last_run_metrics in __init__; clear metrics on turn end. LangSmith metadata when settings.langsmith_tracing.
