title: Task6: BM25+RRF hybrid retriever in agent/rag/retrievers.py
description: Add rank-bm25 dep. In src/agent/rag/retrievers.py add _tokenize (re.findall lowercase alnum), _rrf(ranked_lists, limit, rrf_constant=60) keyed on card_id fallback title, and hybrid_retriever() -> Retriever: dense=store.search(query, k=10), BM25Okapi built per-call over store.list_all_cards() using 'title\ndescription' text, fuse with RRF, return top-k payload dicts (score=RRF). Empty store/corpus -> dense-only fallback. Keep dense_retriever untouched. Tests in tests/test_hybrid_retriever.py: RRF fusion order/dedup, tokenizer, patched-store fusion win for rare-keyword card, empty-store fallback. WHY: card queries hinge on rare exact mechanic keywords (draw/discard/steal/swap) that dense similarity blurs; BM25 excels at rare tokens, RRF fuses ranked lists without score-scale issues.
acceptance_criteria: hybrid_retriever returns fused top-k dicts matching store.search payload shape; pytest green
close_reason: hybrid_retriever + _rrf + _tokenize landed on main, 11 tests, full suite green

title: Fix reconnect brewing-deadlock + reaction-path freeze gap + stale arch docs (review findings)
description:
Adversarial review of the phy session surfaced these confirmed defects:
1. HIGH frontend/lib/ws.ts case 'state' never clears brewing. Since phy.2 gates hand+End Turn on brewing, a reconnect (or any state push arriving after a missed clearing push) soft-locks the active player permanently. Fix: clear brewing on authoritative state. Safe because the room lock is held across interpretation so no state snapshot interleaves brewing->card_interpreted in normal flow.
2. MEDIUM src/board/rooms/room.py _handle_reaction_play (~2122) runs _resolve_plan (LLM round-trip) WITHOUT setting _resolving_play, so the stale-queue race phy.2 fixed for direct plays still exists on the reaction path ŌĆö actions queued behind the lock execute against post-resolution state. Fix: set/clear _resolving_play (try/finally) around the reaction resolve + commit, mirroring the _dispatch play branch. Add a test in test_play_freeze.py (also covers the currently-untested freeze x reaction exemption).
3. LOW docs/architecture.md:297 still says 'Turn model is draw->play->end (draw first, explicit...)' and :274 mermaid note 'draw-first' guard ŌĆö contradicts the auto-draw model; :702 calls CreateCardDialog 'setup / mid-game authoring' after mid-game create_card was deleted. Fix all three lines.
Verified against HEAD during the review.
close_reason: Fixed: ws.ts clears brewing on state (reconnect deadlock); _resolving_play now wraps reaction resolve path (stale-queue race); 3 stale architecture.md lines corrected. Tests: 2 reaction-freeze + brewing hook lifecycle. 1376 backend + 78 frontend pass.

title: Auto-draw at turn start (remove explicit draw step)
description: Playtest: draw was supposed to be automatic. Reverses 70n.6 (explicit draw). Server: draw rules.draw cards in _start_turn (src/board/rooms/room.py:556-568) and on setup->playing first turn; keep _has_drawn bookkeeping/end-of-deck latch semantics intact (deck-exhausted end-game trigger fires on the auto-draw). Decide: keep DrawMsg as no-op/back-compat or remove from protocol + ws_messages docstrings. Frontend: remove Draw button (page.tsx:482-491) and 'draw a card to begin' prompt (page.tsx:568-572); canPlay no longer waits on a manual action. Update tests that script explicit draws.
close_reason: Merged: _auto_draw at turn start (extracted from _handle_draw), first-player covered via _start_playing->_start_turn, deck-exhaustion latch preserved, DrawMsg removed from protocol + docs, Draw button/prompt removed, 12 test files updated + new pins. Full suite 1363 passed.

title: Restrict card authoring to setup phase + blank-card play
description: Remove the always-available 'Author a card' button (frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:492-497) during playing phase. Server: create_card is currently allowed in phases {setup, playing} (src/board/rooms/room.py:312-314) and is UNCAPPED mid-game (room.py:2260-2273) ŌĆö gate create_card to setup only. Blank authoring-on-play (PlayMsg title/description/art) is unaffected and stays. Remove/adjust CreateCardDialog mounting for playing phase; keep it for setup.
close_reason: Merged: create_card+preview_card gated to setup phase (gate above pending checks), ~80-line mid-game LLM interpretation branch deleted, blank author-on-play untouched (existing tests), Author-a-card button + page-level dialog removed, protocol docs updated. Full suite 1372 passed.

title: Lock hand while a blank-card play is being interpreted (brewing)
description: Playing a blank authors+plays it, but while the LLM interprets, the player can play more cards. Server: in Room._dispatch (src/board/rooms/room.py:258-364) reject play/pass/draw from the active player while the just-played card is still brewing (interpretation in flight), same UX as the pending_resolution freeze at room.py:299-311. Client: while brewing is set (frontend/lib/ws.ts, effect-log uses it), disable hand selection + action buttons ŌĆö mirror the not-your-turn treatment (canPlay in app/room/[code]/page.tsx:593, hand.tsx).
close_reason: Merged: Room._resolving_play freeze (try/finally, checked before lock wait) rejects play/pass/end_turn/create_card/start mid-brew; reactions exempt (window opens post-resolution); hand non-selectable + End Turn hidden while brewing; 15 backend + 4 frontend tests.

title: Fix WS RuntimeError: receive after 4009 duplicate-socket replacement
description: src/board/ws.py: when a second socket for the same player connects, ws.py:82 closes the old socket with 4009. The old handler coroutine is still blocked in receive_text (ws.py:95); Starlette raises RuntimeError('WebSocket is not connected...') for ANY non-CONNECTED state, which is not WebSocketDisconnect, so it escapes the except at ws.py:102 -> observed ASGI traceback. Fix: treat non-CONNECTED receive as a normal disconnect (catch RuntimeError or check client_state before receive). ALSO fix latent bug: the old handler's cleanup calls room.connections.disconnect(player_id) (ws.py:104), which by then evicts the NEW socket registered at ws.py:86 ŌĆö disconnect must only unregister if the mapped websocket is this websocket. Add tests for the replace path.
close_reason: Merged: RuntimeError on replaced socket handled as disconnect; identity-aware ConnectionManager.disconnect prevents evicting replacement; 5 regression tests (2 verified failing pre-fix).

Covers: WS crash on duplicate connection, authoring lockdown (setup + blank-play only), blank-play brewing lockout, auto-draw at turn start, shuffled turn order, GET /rooms listing, play log redesign (vertical + readable arbiter comments), always-visible discard w/ ghost outline, taller felt, DynamicStatePanel moved below log (dev-only), drag-and-drop play, expanded smoke suite. Related existing bead: av5 (discard gallery modal) confirmed wanted by playtest.

All merged to main, gated (1378 backend/91.4% cov, 84 frontend, build clean), pushed.

description: Perform a requirement-by-requirement audit of the full initiative, refactor rough seams, update architecture/self-modification/eval/wire-protocol docs, and run all backend/frontend/corpus/eval gates. Exercise Chess, Uno, history winner, auction, and cat drawing/vote flows across reconnect and persistence. Reconcile beads, commit, push, and verify remote state.

title: Generic interaction panel and vector drawing workflow
description: Build a global InteractionPanel for all v1 field kinds, waiting/progress/countdown/sealed states, reconnect recovery, and safe unknown-kind fallback. Implement bounded normalized vector strokes for drawing responses and render prior drawing submissions as later vote choices. Keep fixed lifecycle phases and display card-defined interaction stages as overlays.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend can submit/render every interaction field; drawing payload caps and hostile input checks hold; cat drawing -> sealed submissions -> vote -> tied winners works; reconnect restores pending UI; unknown descriptors never blank/crash; Vitest and React Testing Library cover renderers, serialization, secrecy, timeout, and recovery.

title: Persisted generic interaction protocol and resolution barriers
description: Add versioned interaction_request/interaction_response envelopes and InteractionStep resolution barriers for choice, number, text, card_pick, confirm, and drawing. Persist PendingResolution with plan cursor, cloned working state, sealed responses, resolved audience, and deadline outside shared snapshots. Freeze gameplay while pending, replay requests on reconnect, resume exactly once with ctx.interactions, and commit only after full success.
acceptance_criteria: Audience/auth/schema/privacy checks hold; sealed values never enter shared snapshots; disconnect/rehydration/60s timeout continue with submitted responses and zero responses produce visible no-op; legacy prompt_choice remains compatible; auctions and chained submission-vote plans resolve exactly once with deterministic defaults.
close_reason: Implemented persisted generic interaction barriers, restart-safe deadlines, atomic chained resolution, bounded schemas, real auction/card transfer parity, and executable auction/drawing-vote exemplars; full suite and independent audits pass.

title: Dynamic board state and adjudication diagnostics
description: Align frontend snapshots with backend turn_order/rules/conditions/attributes/hooks and render the active dynamic game. Replace fake preview with real non-mutating interpretation+dry-run. Extend interpretation/preview responses with mechanical status, sanitized reason, and correlation id; show durable failures and capability gaps in the UI and structured backend logs. Add capability-wish storage/export without runtime bd or source edits.
acceptance_criteria: Board shows mutable rules, turn order, conditions, and card attributes; obsolete direction UI/types are gone; preview catches invalid sandbox/Uno plans without mutation; applied/pending/fallback/rejected states and reasons survive reconnect; capability wishes are persisted/exportable and no runtime process invokes bd.

title: Structured game history and history-derived effects

Record actual amounts for every draw path plus plays, score/rule changes, interactions, and game termination. Expose bounded privacy-safe history queries and aggregates to SandboxGame and a read_game_history agent tool. Support multiple explicit winner overrides for deterministic ties.
acceptance_criteria: Explicit draws, multi-card reducer draws, hook/snippet draws, and cannot-play draws record exact amounts once; a sandboxed most-cards-drawn card selects the correct winner/co-winners without parsing prose; history persists and reconnects without duplication; private card contents are not leaked.

Add executable examples for static multi-op chains, post-effect computation, win+end, history scoring, and the basic/spicy/wild Uno ladder. Generate the combined seed corpus from gold+fillers, preserve complete canonical code for retrieved top hits, and upgrade corpus lint/evals from shape checks to behavioral dry-runs of full plans.
acceptance_criteria: RAG no longer claims empty-hand Uno is unsupported; every gold canonical executes end to end on representative state; Chess and all Uno ladder examples pass Room-level tests; combined/gold corpora cannot drift; eval normalization/scorers understand ResolutionPlan and include the new capability cases.

Runtime source editing remains prohibited; all effects flow through validated reducers, sandbox code, hooks, and reviewed data descriptors.

title: fix(review): confirmed findings from wave 1-2 merge review
description: 20 confirmed findings from the adversarial review workflow over 2dfe5b6..2cf96f5. Deduped: (1) HIGH snippet diff with chooser/target_player passes parse_diff then ValueError escapes _apply_snippet and kills the ws task; (2) first_to threshold None/0/negative/already-met ends game instantly all-tied (win_condition_met + 'threshold or 0'); (3) no phase gate in _dispatch: post-end draw/play re-runs _end_game (game_over_requested never cleared) double-applying end scoring and wiping votes; (4) forced-final call omits system prompt (create_agent injects at model-call time, not in state); (5) forced-final ThreadPoolExecutor context-manager shutdown(wait=True) blocks past the 30s timeout; (6) snippet path ignores verdict (runs even for invalid/needs_choice); (7) compile maps win_game/win_the_game to bare EndGameOp so the LEADER wins, not the player ŌĆö give EndGameOp an optional winner target (self) stored as winner_override; (8) test gaps: unequal-score end_game winners, _sanitize_forced_messages, store rehydration of conditions/turn_order/game_over_requested; (9) comment noise: room.py:342/786-789/888-890, game_state.py:86 direction-flag narration, reducers.py:166 docstring opener, scoring.py:102-117 duplicated rationale.
acceptance_criteria: All confirmed findings addressed or explicitly waived: snippet-diff choice-target crash (reject at revalidate + belt-catch), first_to degenerate thresholds inert, post-end phase gate + game_over_requested cleared, forced-final call carries system prompt, forced-call timeout actually returns, snippet respects verdict gate, win_the_game makes the PLAYER win, new tests (unequal-score end_game, sanitization, store round-trip of conditions/turn_order/game_over_requested), narrating comments removed; full suite green.
close_reason: All confirmed findings fixed across two branches (6mt-runtime merged earlier, 6mt-engine now): snippet choice-target rejection + belt, first_to degenerate-threshold guard, phase gate + flag clearing, verdict gate, EndGameOp.winner (You-win cards crown the player), forced-call system prompt + non-blocking timeout, new tests (store round-trip, unequal scores, sanitization), comment cleanup. 933 tests green.

title: feat(agent): force a final answer at tool/timeout budget instead of giving up
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest): the model burns far more tool calls than expected, but lowering the cap makes it GIVE UP instead of answer: MAX_TOOL_CALLS=100 / AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600 (src/agent/runtime.py:50-54) are passed as LangGraph recursion_limit (:248, :272), and on GraphRecursionError (:287-292) or timeout (:280-286) or any exception (:293-298) the runtime returns _fallback_result(...) with verdict="invalid" ŌĆö a deterministic no-op. The budget is a cliff, not a forcing function.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/agent/runtime.py ŌĆö on GraphRecursionError/timeout, make ONE final LLM call with tools disabled, feeding the conversation so far plus "Budget exhausted ŌĆö output your final JSON interpretation NOW using what you already know." Parse via the existing _parse_result (:136-173). Only if that fails, fall back to _fallback_result (:101-117).
- Then TUNE MAX_TOOL_CALLS down (e.g. 15-25) ŌĆö a low cap should force an answer, not an "invalid". Validate against src/evals/ harness.
- Fix the stale module docstring/comment still claiming the limit is 8 (runtime.py header).

TESTS: fake-model unit test that exceeds the limit and asserts the forced-final call runs and its JSON is returned; timeout path likewise.
acceptance_criteria: When the recursion limit or timeout is hit, the runtime makes one final tools-disabled LLM call and returns its parsed InterpretResult; _fallback_result(verdict=invalid) only fires if that forced call also fails; MAX_TOOL_CALLS tuned down (with evals confirming no give-up regressions); stale '8' docstring fixed; unit test with a fake model exercising the forced-final path; full suite green.

title: fix(rag): gold seed cards use vocabulary the compiler silently drops
description:
THE PROBLEM: several gold seed cards ŌĆö the exemplars the agent learns from ŌĆö are silently broken at compile time:
1. target:"chosen_player" and target:"next_player" appear in data/seed_cards_gold.json but are in NEITHER _AUTHORING_TARGET_ALIASES (src/models/effects.py:80-123) NOR _VALID_TARGETS (:12-23). _compile_op passes default="self" for add_points/skip_turn/draw_cards (src/engine/compile.py:86-123), so they silently become self: "Steal 3 Points" (seed_cards_gold.json:38-50) compiles to self -3 / self +3 = NET ZERO; "Give 5 Points to Any Player" self-awards.
2. set_win_condition {condition:"instant"} plus count/winner args don't match the runtime schema (kind/threshold, effects.py:191-195) and are skipped ŌĆö the gold "Win the Game" card does nothing (same failure family as the YOLO playtest card).

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/effects.py ŌĆö add authoring aliases where a runtime target exists (chosen_player ŌåÆ chooser/target_player with requires_choice; next_player ŌåÆ left/right neighbor per turn order).
- data/seed_cards_gold.json ŌĆö fix the broken cards; AUDIT data/seed_cards.json, seed_cards_simple.json, seed_cards_fillers.json for the same vocabulary drift.
- src/engine/compile.py (:16-22, :86-123, :135) ŌĆö stop failing silently: log every skipped op and every defaulted target so corpus drift is visible in future.

TESTS: per-card compile assertions for the fixed cards; a corpus lint test that iterates every seed file and fails on any silently-defaulted target / skipped op.
acceptance_criteria: Every card in data/seed_cards*.json compiles with no silently-defaulted target and no skipped op (asserted by a corpus lint test); 'Steal 3 Points' actually transfers points; 'Win the Game' has a working interpretation; compile_card logs (or surfaces) skipped/defaulted ops instead of silent degradation; full suite green.

title: feat(rag): seed multi-effect and game-altering exemplars
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest): the agent doesn't treat cards as MULTI-effect. Chess ("draw 2, then gain points per card in hand") and combo cards (set win condition AND end game; add + subtract + skip + extra turn + destroy in one card) all got weak or single-op interpretations. The gold corpus has only two 2-op examples ("Steal 3 Points" ŌĆö currently net-zero broken, see the seed-vocabulary bug bead ŌĆö and "Discard Your Hand"); nothing teaches "cards can do MANY things at once and nothing limits them".

WHAT TO ADD (data/seed_cards_gold.json + main corpus, loaded via src/agent/rag/seed.py):
- Chess shape: "draw 2 cards, then gain 1 point per card in hand" ŌĆö canonical ops draw_cards + snippet (or dynamic op once available) with hand-count scoring.
- Combo: set_win_condition + end_game in one program (requires the EndGameOp bead).
- Chaos: 4-6 op card (add_points + subtract_points + skip_turn + extra_turn + destroy_card).
- Uno family: "the rules and win condition are the same as Uno" ŌĆö win condition empty_hand, end trigger empty_hand, draw step 0; spicier variants (created Draw 2/Draw 4/Reverse cards, color attributes) as the dynamic-engine phases land.

COORDINATION: use whatever op surface exists when this lands ŌĆö start with ops+snippets now, upgrade exemplars as rules-as-data / dynamic targets / CreateCardOp phases ship. Pairs with the card_rag formatter bead (ops must be VISIBLE in retrieval for exemplars to teach anything).
acceptance_criteria: Corpus contains, with full canonical ops (and snippet where needed): a draw-AND-score Chess-shape card, a set_win_condition+end_game combo, a 4+ op chaos card, and the Uno family (rule change + win condition + end trigger); all new exemplars pass the corpus compile-lint; retrieving 'chess'/'uno'-like queries surfaces them; full suite green.

title: feat(rag): include canonical ops in card_rag exemplar output
description:
THE PROBLEM: retrieved RAG exemplars are formatted as "title ŌĆö description ŌĆö score" only (_format_hits, src/agent/tools/card_rag.py:27-45). The canonical ops payload IS stored at upsert time (src/agent/rag/seed.py:59-68, canonical serialized as JSON string) but is dropped by the formatter ŌĆö so the model retrieving "Steal 3 Points" sees prose, never the two-op program that interpreted it. This is a key reason multi-effect cards get weak single-op (or note-only) interpretations: precedent mechanics are invisible.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/agent/tools/card_rag.py _format_hits (:27-45) ŌĆö append the canonical ops JSON per hit (compact; truncate very long snippets/op lists to keep prompt cost sane). Handle records with missing/empty canonical.

Formatting-only change; no store/schema migration needed.
acceptance_criteria: card_rag tool output includes each hit's canonical ops (compact JSON) alongside title/description/score; hits lacking canonical payload degrade gracefully; formatter unit test asserts ops presence; full suite green.

title: feat(board): execute agent snippets in Room._resolve_program
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest Chess card): the agent correctly emitted a snippet (draw 2, then score points per card in hand ŌĆö not expressible in the op set) but the game showed "[note] Played Chess (no mechanical effect)". Root cause: the snippet sandbox is DEAD CODE in the serving path. Room._resolve_program (src/board/rooms/room.py:579-656) applies only result.program; result.snippet is stringified/broadcast/stored on the card (room.py:874) but never validated or executed. grep confirms zero board call sites for execute_snippet / make_snippet_handler / apply_snippet_diff / fire_hooks. The entire src/engine/sandbox/ pipeline (validate ŌåÆ subprocess runner ŌåÆ revalidate) is built and tested but unreachable in production.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/board/rooms/room.py _resolve_program (:579-656) ŌĆö when result.snippet is present (and program is null or note-only), run validate_snippet ŌåÆ execute_snippet (src/engine/sandbox/runner.py:26) ŌåÆ apply_snippet_diff (src/engine/sandbox/revalidate.py:53) so recorded ops flow through the SAME reducers as programs. On SnippetExecutionError/DiffValidationError, log a visible "[snippet error] <card>: <exc>" line via _log_and_broadcast (room.py:951-960) ŌĆö never silent, never crash the turn.
- Honor Settings.snippet_execution_enabled (src/config.py:81) ŌĆö when off, keep today's CustomNoteOp fallback (room.py:654-656).
- Contract/docs mismatch: hooks.py:100-105 builds ctx keys {actor_id, event, card_id, amount} but src/agent/contract.py:29 documents {player_id, card, event} ŌĆö align contract.py (and persona guidance) with the real ctx the snippet receives, or the agent will keep writing snippets against a phantom API.

DEPENDS ON: the _child_runner SyntaxError fix bead (child process cannot start today).

TESTS: room-level: snippet-only interpretation mutates state per its recorded ops; failing snippet logs [snippet error] and state is unchanged; flag-off path preserves current behavior.
acceptance_criteria: An interpretation whose only mechanics are a snippet mutates game state via the sandbox (validate ŌåÆ execute_snippet ŌåÆ apply_snippet_diff ŌåÆ reducers); sandbox failures produce a visible '[snippet error] ŌĆ”' log line and leave state unchanged; Settings.snippet_execution_enabled=False falls back to the current note behavior; contract docs match the real ctx keys; room-level tests for success + failure paths; full suite green.

title: feat(engine): EndGameOp + evaluate win/end conditions during play
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest): an "End the Game" card claimed to end the game but play continued until the deck ran out; YOLO ("You win the game") could only set_points 999999 and could not end the game. Root cause: there is NO end_game op in the vocabulary (Op union src/models/effects.py:207-223; dispatch table src/engine/reducers.py:227-240) and the win condition is NEVER evaluated during play ŌĆö evaluate_win_condition/check_win only run inside _end_game (src/board/rooms/room.py:484). The ONLY live end path is deck exhaustion: _handle_draw latches _deck_exhausted (room.py:389-391) ŌåÆ _advance_turn calls _end_game (room.py:424-425). The agent literally cannot end the game, no matter what a card says.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/effects.py ŌĆö add EndGameOp (op="end_game") to the discriminated union (:207-223).
- src/engine/reducers.py ŌĆö new reducer that marks the state as ending via a SERIALIZED signal (not a Room-private latch); register in _REDUCERS (:227-245).
- src/board/rooms/room.py ŌĆö after apply_effect in _handle_play (~:784) and in _advance_turn (:408): check the end signal AND evaluate_win_condition (so first_to/threshold conditions set by set_win_condition become live); route to _end_game. Keep deck-empty semantics (drawer finishes turn).
- src/engine/scoring.py ŌĆö evaluate_win_condition reused as the in-play check; no logic fork.
- src/agent/persona.py:60-62 ŌĆö add end_game to the hardcoded op list (read_engine_methods introspects the union automatically).
- src/engine/compile.py ŌĆö map authoring-vocabulary "end_game"/"win the game" shapes.

COORDINATION: the dynamic-engine epic Phase A (rules-as-data) later refactors this into rules.end_condition={type:"now"} evaluation ŌĆö keep the two check sites exactly as named above so that refactor is mechanical.

TESTS: reducer unit test; room-level test playing an end-game card mid-deck ŌåÆ epilogue/ended without exhaustion; room-level test set_win_condition(first_to, N) then a scoring play reaching N ends the game.
acceptance_criteria: A card whose program contains end_game transitions the room out of playing immediately (deck untouched); set_win_condition first_to N ends the game the moment a player reaches N mid-play; deck-empty end preserves drawer-finishes-turn timing; end_game appears in persona op list and read_engine_methods output; reducer + room-level tests green; full suite green.

title: fix(sandbox): repair _child_runner Py2 except-tuple SyntaxError + close validator holes
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest root cause for ALL snippet failures): src/engine/sandbox/_child_runner.py:54 reads `except ImportError, ValueError, OSError:` ŌĆö Python 2 syntax, a SyntaxError in Python 3. The child module cannot even be imported, so execute_snippet() ALWAYS fails ("Child produced no output") before any user code runs. Every agent snippet crashes at the boundary. Likely cause: the known ruff-format hazard ŌĆö the formatter strips the parens from `except (A, B, C):` (see bd memory ruff-format-corrupts-except-tuples).

SECOND HOLE: src/engine/sandbox/validate.py blocks dunder ATTRIBUTE access (ast.Attribute) but not string-literal dunders ŌĆö `getattr(x, "__class__")` passes validation. getattr/setattr/delattr/vars/globals/locals are absent from _FORBIDDEN_CALLS.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/engine/sandbox/_child_runner.py:54 ŌĆö rewrite so ruff format CANNOT corrupt it: use separate `except ImportError:` / `except ValueError:` / `except OSError:` clauses (do NOT write a paren tuple in this repo; the formatter strips the parens). Sweep the rest of src/engine/sandbox/ for the same pattern.
- src/engine/sandbox/validate.py (_FORBIDDEN_CALLS) ŌĆö add getattr, setattr, delattr, vars, globals, locals.

TESTS: add a subprocess ROUND-TRIP smoke test that runs a trivial snippet through execute_snippet (src/engine/sandbox/runner.py:26) end-to-end ŌĆö this is the test that would have caught the SyntaxError (existing tests apparently mock or never reach the child). Add validate tests rejecting each newly forbidden call and getattr(x, "__class__").

WHY P1: blocks wiring snippets into the serving path (Chess-card fix bead) and the whole dynamic-engine hooks track.
acceptance_criteria: A trivial snippet executes end-to-end through execute_snippet in the real subprocess and returns a valid diff; _child_runner.py imports cleanly (py_compile); validator rejects getattr/setattr/delattr/vars/globals/locals and the string-literal dunder bypass getattr(x, "__class__"); running ruff format leaves the except clauses valid; full suite green.
close_reason: Merged. NOTE: on Python 3.14 (PEP 758) 'except A, B:' is VALID syntax ŌĆö the SyntaxError diagnosis was wrong; child runner worked all along. Hardening + validator forbidden-calls + round-trip tests landed anyway. Chess-card root cause is solely the unwired snippet path (bead bsv).

title: feat(board): FileRoomStore so games survive reload (DEV_MODE)
description:
Add a FileRoomStore implementing the existing RoomStore Protocol (src/board/rooms/store.py:22) that persists each room's GameState to a JSON file and rehydrates all rooms on startup, so in-progress games survive an API reload. Wire it in ONLY when DEV_MODE is true; production keeps the current InMemoryRoomStore (restart-clears-everything).

CONTEXT: Room/game state is 100% in-memory ŌĆö a plain dict[str, Room] in InMemoryRoomStore (store.py:56). No game-state DB exists. On uvicorn --reload the process restarts, the room_manager singleton (manager.py:141) is re-instantiated, and all games vanish. The RoomStore Protocol (store.py:22, methods get/put/exists/count) is the designed seam; a new impl is injected via RoomManager(store=...) (manager.py:50).

WHAT SERIALIZES / WHAT DOES NOT:
- Persist: room.state.model_dump(mode="json"), plus room.code and room._simple. GameState is fully Pydantic and round-trips.
- Do NOT persist transient/non-serializable Room attrs ŌĆö reconstruct fresh on load: connections=ConnectionManager() (empty; clients reconnect via WS), _lock=asyncio.Lock(), _epilogue=None, _has_drawn=False, _deck_exhausted=False, _comment_logged=set().
- DOCUMENT-IN-CODE caveat: two PrivateAttr sets on GameState (_skip_next, _extra_turn, game_state.py:99-100) are not in model_dump and reset on reload ŌĆö acceptable for a dev convenience, note it in a comment. Also _deck_exhausted resetting means a reload exactly between "last card drawn" and "turn ends" loses the end-game latch ŌĆö acceptable, document it.

FILES & CHANGES:
1. src/board/rooms/store.py ŌĆö add class FileRoomStore implementing get/put/exists/count. Constructor takes a dir path (default `.devstate/rooms`). Keep an in-process dict too so live Room objects (with their lock/connections) are reused within one process ŌĆö only rehydrate from disk on a cold miss. put(code, room) writes <dir>/<code>.json. Add load_all() (or lazy scan) that reconstructs Room objects from the dir on init. Add _room_to_dict / _room_from_dict helpers. A malformed/old JSON file must be SKIPPED with a logged warning (use `except Exception:`), never crash.
2. PERSISTENCE HOOK ŌĆö put() is only called by RoomManager.create_room; game mutations reassign room.state in place and do NOT call put again. So add a save hook after each action: add an optional `on_change: Callable[[Room], None] | None = None` param to Room.__init__ (src/board/rooms/room.py:64) defaulting to None (=no-op=current behavior); invoke it at the END of Room.handle_action (room.py:114, the single serialized entry point, INSIDE the lock, after _dispatch): `if self.on_change: self.on_change(self)`.
3. src/board/rooms/manager.py ŌĆö the module-level singleton (line 141) currently hard-defaults to InMemoryRoomStore. Choose the store based on get_settings().dev_mode: if dev_mode -> FileRoomStore + call load_all() at construction (rooms survive reload) + ensure create_room wires Room(..., on_change=self._persist) so mutations persist; else -> InMemoryRoomStore (unchanged default). Add a _persist method on RoomManager that calls store.put(room.code, room).
4. .gitignore ŌĆö add `.devstate/`.

DEPENDS ON the DEV_MODE flag bead (sot.1) ŌĆö needs get_settings().dev_mode.

GOTCHA: never write `except (A, B):` ŌĆö ruff-format corrupts it. Use `except Exception:`.
acceptance_criteria: With DEV_MODE=true: create a room, drive it to playing (reuse drive_to_playing in tests/conftest.py), simulate restart by constructing a fresh RoomManager/FileRoomStore pointed at the same dir, assert the room is present with same phase, turn_index, players, and cards. With DEV_MODE unset/false: RoomManager uses InMemoryRoomStore, no files written, behavior identical to today. A malformed/old JSON file in the dir is skipped with a logged warning, not a crash. Existing room/ws tests pass unchanged.

title: perf(rag): disk content-hash cache for seed embeddings
description:
Avoid re-calling the embedding API for unchanged cards on every reload. Add a disk-backed cache in src/agent/rag/embeddings.py keyed by a content hash so load_seed_cards() only embeds new/changed text. ALWAYS-ON (not gated by DEV_MODE) ŌĆö it is a pure optimization producing identical output.

CONTEXT: On every uvicorn --reload, src/board/app.py lifespan calls agent.rag.seed.load_seed_cards(), which loops over 60 seed cards calling upsert_card() -> embed_text() -> one embedding API round-trip each (src/agent/rag/store.py:79, embeddings.py:51-53). No caching exists. The @lru_cache on get_embeddings() caches the CLIENT object, not vectors.

DESIGN:
- Cache key: hashlib.blake2b(f"{model}\n{dimensions}\n{text}".encode()).hexdigest(). MUST include model + dimensions so switching embedding model/provider invalidates automatically (a 1536-dim vector must never be reused for a 768-dim model ŌĆö see dimension warnings in embeddings.py:12-17 and store.py:44-46). Read model via get_settings().embedding_model and dims via embedding_dimensions().
- Cache location: gitignored file `.embedding_cache.json` at repo root. Format: JSON dict mapping hash -> list[float]. Load lazily once per process (module global or lru_cache); write back after new embeds.
- Concurrency: single-writer dev use, so read-all/write-all JSON is fine. Guard file read AND write in try/except (use `except Exception:`, NOT a tuple) so a cache failure NEVER breaks embedding ŌĆö log at debug and fall through to live API.

FILES & CHANGES:
1. src/agent/rag/embeddings.py:
   - Add helpers: _cache_path() -> Path, _load_cache() -> dict[str, list[float]], _save_cache(cache) -> None.
   - Add def embed_text_cached(text: str) -> list[float]: compute key, return cached vector on hit; else call existing embed_text(text), store, return. KEEP embed_text untouched (still the raw live call, used by query-time search() where caching a query vector is pointless).
   - PREFERRED: also add embed_texts_cached(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]] that partitions hits/misses, calls get_embeddings().embed_documents(misses) ONCE for all misses (cuts 60 round-trips to <=1), fills cache, returns vectors in input order. This fixes sequential-call latency, not just $$.
2. src/agent/rag/store.py: upsert_card() currently calls embed_text(text) at line 79 ŌĆö change to embed_text_cached(text). If adding the batch helper, EITHER add an upsert_cards(...) batch fn, OR have seed.load_seed_cards embed in one batch then upsert with precomputed vectors. Whatever you choose, KEEP upsert_card's single-card API intact ŌĆö the epilogue caller (src/board/rooms/epilogue.py:52,67) upserts player-kept cards one at a time and must keep working.
3. .gitignore: add `.embedding_cache.json`.

This bead is INDEPENDENT ŌĆö no dependency on the DEV_MODE flag bead. Can be done in parallel.

GOTCHA: never write `except (A, B):` ŌĆö ruff-format corrupts it. Use `except Exception:` or separate clauses.
acceptance_criteria: First load_seed_cards() run populates the cache; a second run with same cards/model makes ZERO embedding API calls ŌĆö verify with a test monkeypatching get_embeddings() (embed_documents/embed_query) with a call-counting fake, asserting 0 live calls on the second load. Changing a card's title/description OR LLM_EMBEDDING_MODEL/LLM_EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS causes a cache miss + fresh embed for affected entries only. Query-time search() behavior unchanged. Cache read/write failures degrade gracefully (correct vectors via live API). Existing RAG tests pass.

description:

1. src/config.py ŌĆö add field `dev_mode: bool = False` in a new `# --- Developer experience ---` section, immediately after `snippet_execution_enabled` (~line 81). pydantic-settings is case-insensitive so the env var is DEV_MODE automatically; no alias needed.
2. .env.example (repo root) ŌĆö add a documented line: `# DEV_MODE=false  # enable dev-loop features: room persistence + /dev endpoints`.
3. src/board/app.py ŌĆö in the lifespan() startup log block (where it already logs langsmith status, ~lines 125-140), add a WARNING-level log when get_settings().dev_mode is true: logger.warning("DEV_MODE enabled ŌĆö room persistence and /dev endpoints are ACTIVE (do not use in production)"). Makes accidental prod-on obvious.

This bead is the FOUNDATION ŌĆö beads for FileRoomStore, skip-setup endpoint, and end-game endpoint all depend on it.

acceptance_criteria: DEV_MODE=true uv run python -c 'from config import get_settings; print(get_settings().dev_mode)' prints True; unset prints False. Existing tests pass. A new unit test monkeypatches DEV_MODE env and asserts the flag, using the monkeypatch.setenv + get_settings.cache_clear() pattern already used in tests (see tests/conftest.py _hermetic_settings fixture).

title: epic: developer-experience improvements for the dev loop
description: Four dev-loop pain points found during play-testing, all stemming from running the API with 'uv run uvicorn board.app:app --reload': (1) every reload re-embeds all 60 seed cards (60 sequential embedding API calls, wasted latency/$$); (2) every reload destroys in-progress games (state is 100% in-memory, no DB); (3) no way to skip author-creation (setup phase) to reach the agent/playing phase faster; (4) no fast path to end-game/epilogue to test end-game triggers, scoring, and the epilogue vote UI. Goal: cut the dev feedback loop WITHOUT changing production behavior. All new behavior is gated behind a new DEV_MODE flag (default False), except the embedding cache which is a safe always-on optimization. Full plan: ~/.claude/plans/we-need-to-add-recursive-candy.md
acceptance_criteria: All 5 child beads closed; DEV_MODE flag exists and gates dev-only features; embedding cache eliminates redundant embed calls; games survive reload under DEV_MODE; /dev/skip-setup and /dev/end-game endpoints work; full pytest + ruff green; prod behavior unchanged when DEV_MODE unset.

title: bug(frontend): recoverable errors kick player to lobby via full-screen takeover
description:
Manual playtest bug. Clicking an action that errors (e.g. 'You have already drawn this turn') ejects the player from the game entirely, offering only 'Back to lobby'. Errors should NOT kick a player out.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): any {type:'error'} message sets 'error' in frontend/lib/ws.ts:139-141; frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:188-197 renders a FULL-SCREEN 'Back to lobby' takeover whenever 'error' is truthy, unmounting the game table. That branch was designed for hard join rejections (close codes >=4000, ws.ts:147-163), but it fires for every 'error' message including benign gameplay validation errors sent via connections.send (e.g. room.py:315-317). 'error' is never auto-cleared (only reset on socket onopen, ws.ts:100).

Related to the draw-broadcast bug (fixing that removes the main trigger) but this is an independent hardening fix ŌĆö an errant error must never eject a seated player.
design: Distinguish fatal connection errors (close-code driven, ws.ts:147-163) from message-level 'error' payloads in ws.ts. Show message-level errors as a transient, dismissible banner/toast in page.tsx instead of tearing down the game, and auto-clear them. Reserve the full-screen 'Back to lobby' screen for hard rejections arriving via onclose code >=4000. Layout note: frontend is in frontend/.
acceptance_criteria: A gameplay validation error shows an in-place, dismissible message and the player STAYS in the game; only close-code >=4000 rejections show the full-screen lobby screen.
close_reason: reconcile: re-close after rebase-abort reverted live Dolt DB; all merged to main

title: bug(draw): drawing a card doesn't broadcast state ŌĆö player must refresh to see the card
description:
Manual playtest bug. After a player draws, they must refresh the page to see the drawn card; until they do, the hand stays unplayable and only the Draw button is available.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): _handle_draw (src/board/rooms/room.py:307-329) mutates hand/deck then calls _log_and_broadcast (room.py:837-846), which only emits an 'effect_applied' log message ŌĆö it never pushes a fresh state snapshot. Every OTHER mutating handler calls _broadcast_state() (room.py:868); _handle_draw is the only one that doesn't. So the client's gameState keeps the old hand and has_drawn:false; the UI (frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:275-307) stays gated on the stale has_drawn, leaving only the Draw button. A refresh works because reconnect replays the full snapshot (src/board/ws.py:90).

The empty-deck early-return (room.py:318-322) also sets _has_drawn=True without broadcasting ŌĆö same class of bug.
design: Add 'await self._broadcast_state()' after the successful draw in _handle_draw, and also for the empty-deck early-return at room.py:318-322 (which sets _has_drawn=True). Note _log_and_broadcast intentionally only sends effect_applied for the live log; drawing needs the full snapshot in addition. Layout note: code lives in src/board/, NOT src/tbwc/.
acceptance_criteria: Drawing a card updates the hand and enables play with NO refresh. Regression test asserts _handle_draw triggers a 'state' broadcast (mirror existing room/ws tests under tests/).
close_reason: reconcile: re-close after rebase-abort reverted live Dolt DB; all merged to main

title: C10: Wire resolve_card to pass GameState + actor_id to the agent

Keep the deterministic compile_card bypass (the basic/simple seed deck must never call the LLM) and the CustomNoteOp fallback (a play never silently no-ops). MITIGATE room-lock-held-across-agent-run: _resolve_program runs interpret_card in asyncio.to_thread WHILE HOLDING self._lock; a multi-tool ReAct loop hitting Scryfall/Tavily/Wikipedia/RAG can stall the whole room. Enforce the C1 cap + wall-clock timeout, and consider snapshot-then-release-lock with re-validate-on-apply.

title: C1: Single tool-calling agent skeleton (create_agent/ReAct)
description: Rewrite the agent from scratch as ONE LangGraph prebuilt tool-calling agent (create_agent / ReAct) ŌĆö 'just middleware with tool-call limits', replacing the 9-node StateGraph. Includes: system prompt + persona decision logic (do-nothing when undecipherable & player!=author / punish-author when player==author authored a dumb card / chaos-monkey when well-meant but ambiguous / random-among-solutions when multiple valid readings); a HARD recursion/tool-call cap + wall-clock timeout so a multi-tool loop can't hang or hold the room lock; LangSmith tracing wired behind the existing langsmith_* settings (off by default); uses gpt-5.4-mini via the existing agent/llm.py get_chat_model factory. Tools are added in C2-C9; the skeleton binds an (initially empty/stub) tool list and returns the C0 result shape.
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic test with a fake chat model (LangChain FakeListChatModel or a bound stub) drives >=1 tool call and returns {program|snippet, verdict, comment, persona_action}; a stubbed over-cap loop terminates with a bounded fallback (never hangs); missing/invalid model config degrades to the deterministic CustomNoteOp fallback (mirrors today's interpret_card failure path), asserted by test; LangSmith off by default.
notes: Coverage risk: prebuilt agents are hard to unit-test deterministically ŌĆö rely on the fake chat model for tool-routing tests. Per-bead coverage is an explicit AC across C.
