title: Rewrite into clean board/engine/agent components + persistent agent commentary
description: Simplify TBWC into three clear components per the owner's diagrams: Board (UI, game state, rendering, player WS connections), Game Engine (the 'physics' ŌĆö add/subtract points, draw, resolve_card, check_end_game, determine_winner, update_history), and Agent (interprets cards during resolve_card when a card has no deterministic instructions). Plain pydantic models are the data contracts. Two structural changes: (1) drop the tbwc namespace and flatten src/tbwc/ into component subdirs src/board, src/engine, src/agent, src/models (rag folded into agent; sandbox STAYS with engine to avoid an engine->agent cycle); (2) replace the 9-node LangGraph StateGraph with ONE prebuilt tool-calling agent (create_agent/ReAct) with a tool-call/recursion cap, seven tools (web_search, mtg_lookup, read_game_state, read_engine_methods, game_rules, card_rag, agent_memory), an in-character persona for undecipherable cards (do-nothing / punish-author / chaos-monkey / random-among-solutions), and a funny per-card comment persisted to game history. Enforced layering: board->agent->engine->models; engine<->sandbox (existing lazy); never engine->agent, never agent->board.
design: Locked decisions: layout = component subdirs, drop tbwc ns, sandbox with engine not agent. Engine = keep reducer guts + tests, add thin GameEngine facade (pure-engine, no agent import); LLM orchestration stays in Room (board). Agent = rewrite from scratch, LangGraph create_agent, OpenAI gpt-5.4-mini, keep LangSmith. resolve_card now passes live GameState + actor_id to the agent. Comments persist to state.log so they survive reconnect.
notes: Deliverable of the planning session was these beads. Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/alright-i-just-spent-radiant-dove.md
close_reason: All 17 child beads (A1-A2, B, C0-C12, D1-D2, E1-E3) complete: flattened to board/engine/agent/models, single tool-calling agent with 7 tools + persona + commentary, old 9-node graph deleted, 710 tests green @ 92%

title: rooms: end-game on last-card-drawn ŌåÆ resolve ŌåÆ epilogue ŌåÆ ended
description: Rewire rooms/room.py::_end_game. Trigger when the active player ends their turn AND the deck is empty (last card was drawn, per bead 6). Sequence: resolve_end_of_game ŌåÆ set winner_ids ŌåÆ broadcast final scores ŌåÆ call the currently-orphaned start_epilogue ŌåÆ epilogue voting (existing EpilogueManager/tally_votes) ŌåÆ phase=ended. Fixes _end_game bypassing the epilogue and makes step 7ŌåÆ8 real.
acceptance_criteria: Emptying the deck then ending the turn runs resolve_end_of_game, transitions playingŌåÆepilogueŌåÆended, and tallies votes via EpilogueManager.

title: engine: ON_GAME_END event + resolve_end_of_game (kept-in-hand scoring)
description: Add ON_GAME_END to engine/events.py::GameEvent. New pure engine/scoring.py::resolve_end_of_game(state) -> GameState: for each card still in a player's hand/in_play carrying an end-of-game/kept-bonus clause, apply its points via apply_effect with an ON_GAME_END HookContext (reusing the existing reducers), then call evaluate_win_condition to set winner_ids. Resolves 'worth 10 points if you keep it'.
acceptance_criteria: A kept 'worth N' card in hand adds N to its holder at game end; winner_ids reflects post-resolution scores; pure/immutable.

title: frontend: setup shows 30 premade + draw/end-turn controls + pass gating
description: SetupPhase (components/setup-phase.tsx) renders the 30 premade cards ('deck so far') from gameState.cards for synergy, plus per-player author progress from setup_progress. Turn controls: add a Draw button (active player, before play), gate Play on has_drawn, and hide Pass unless snapshot can_pass. Update lib/ws.ts + lib/types.ts to add the draw message and the can_pass/setup_progress fields.
acceptance_criteria: Setup screen lists the 30 premade cards; Draw button present and required before Play; Pass hidden while can_pass is false.

title: setup: count authored cards + gate start at 5/player (setup_progress)
description: During setup, _handle_create_card tags creator_id and increments a per-player authored count. Add setup_progress {player_id: count} to the state snapshot so clients render '3/5 authored' for everyone. The start gate in bead 8 reads this count. In setup, soften/skip the LLM at authoring time (interpret best-effort only).
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot carries setup_progress; authoring a card increments the author's count; start is blocked until every non-spectator reaches 5.

title: rooms: pass only when truly unplayable; never with a blank
description: Compute can_pass for the active player = holds no playable card. Blanks are always playable ŌćÆ pass is never offered while holding one. Expose can_pass in the state snapshot and gate the pass/end-without-play path in _dispatch. Fixes 'pass button shown even when I can play'.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot exposes can_pass; a player holding any blank or any card with a non-empty compiled program has can_pass=false; pass is rejected server-side when can_pass is false.

title: feat(rooms): end-game on deck exhaustion ŌĆö win/lose + deck count
description:
Playtest: after drawing the entire deck it just says 'empty deck' and dumps to lobby. No win/lose screen; other players get stuck in game. Also no deck-remaining count is shown. Note: the player who draws the last card should still take their turn.

Root cause: _handle_draw sends {'type':'error','message':'Deck is empty'} and returns (src/tbwc/rooms/room.py:120-121) with NO phase change. Working end-game machinery exists but is unwired: engine/loop.py:59-60 sets phase='ended' on empty deck; engine/scoring.py:8,37 (evaluate_win_condition/check_win) computes winners; Room.start_epilogue (room.py:268) is never called. Frontend phase==='ended' screen exists (page.tsx:215-223) but is generic. Deck count is available (frontend/lib/types.ts:71-72) but never rendered (game-table.tsx).

Fix: replace the dead-end with a proper end ŌĆö last drawer finishes their turn, then transition to epilogue/ended by wiring scoring.py + Room.start_epilogue. Compute and broadcast a winner; upgrade the frontend ended screen into a real win/lose result. Render a deck-remaining count during play in game-table.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: Emptying the deck ends the game for everyone with a win/lose screen; the last-card drawer completes their turn; players see cards-left throughout play.
close_reason: deck-exhaustion end-game with winner_ids; broadcast to all; frontend win/lose screen

title: feat(cards): blank cards as playable, authored on play
description:
Playtest: you never draw blank cards ŌĆö but the game is literally 'a thousand blank white cards'.

Design (user): blanks are playable cards. A blank enters the hand as blank; when the player chooses to PLAY it, the authoring flow opens and the newly authored card is what gets played (author on play, not at draw time).

Root cause: build_deck (src/tbwc/rooms/deck.py:82-119) sources only RAG/seed cards (deck.py:66) and pads by duplicating existing cards ŌĆö no blanks are ever inserted.

Fix: seed the deck with blank cards in build_deck so blanks are drawable. On play of a blank, open the authoring flow (reuse _handle_create_card / interpret graph, room.py:222-260) and play the authored result. Extend the card model / CardSnapshot (frontend/lib/types.ts:46-55) to mark blanks so the UI can render them.
acceptance_criteria: Blank cards appear in the deck, can be drawn, sit in hand as blank, and are filled in at play time via the authoring flow.
close_reason: deck seeds blanks; author-on-play with persist-before-interpret; prompt_choice preserved; 541 tests green

title: feat(rooms): draw->play->pass turn model; remove manual draw
description:
Playtest: on your turn you can draw as many times as you want; turn never advances.

Design (user): base turn = draw -> play -> pass, automatic. Draw should be automatic per game rules ŌĆö no 'Draw' button. If you have no playable card, you draw a second card. Playing a card or an explicit pass advances the turn. No unlimited drawing.

Root cause: _handle_draw (src/tbwc/rooms/room.py:119-128) pops one card, never advances turn_index, and ignores draw_count (game_state.py:68). Turn only advances in _handle_play (room.py:214-219).

Fix: rework the turn ŌĆö at turn start the active player auto-draws draw_count; if no playable card, draw a second; play/pass advances turn_index; enforce the draw cap. Reuse the engine turn loop where possible (engine/loop.py draw_step/run_turn/advance_turn, currently unwired) instead of inline _handle_draw. Frontend: remove the Draw button (frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:197-199), add a Pass affordance.
acceptance_criteria: A player cannot draw the whole deck; turns rotate on play/pass; drawing is automatic per rules.
close_reason: auto-draw/pass turn model; engine.loop.advance_turn wired; 517 tests green

title: bug(frontend): direct room-link join gets stuck on Connecting to room
description:
Pasting a room code/URL directly and entering a name gets stuck forever on 'Connecting to room'.

Root cause: the name-gate path (frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:102-123) only sets local nameSet ŌĆö it never persists the name to localStorage and never does the REST POST /rooms/{code}/join, so there is no player_id in sessionStorage. The WS then sends join with player_id: null (lib/ws.ts:65-74); the backend rejects (ws.py:38-58), the socket closes, and the reconnect loop retries every 2s forever (ws.ts:107-112). The real error is never shown because the 'Connecting' guard (page.tsx:125-131) is checked BEFORE the error branch (page.tsx:133-142).

Fix: in the name-gate path, persist the name and perform POST /rooms/{code}/join (store player_id) before opening the WS ŌĆö matching app/page.tsx:50-74. Reorder render guards so the error screen is reachable, and stop the reconnect loop from retrying a hard-rejected join.
acceptance_criteria: Pasting a room URL, entering a name, and joining lands in the room (or shows a clear error) ŌĆö never an infinite 'Connecting'.
close_reason: direct-paste join mirrors landing flow; 4xxx hard-reject handling; frontend gates green

title: bug(rooms): thread chosen_player_id from play message into HookContext
description: _handle_play (src/tbwc/rooms/room.py:125) builds HookContext(event=ON_PLAY, actor_id, card_id) WITHOUT chosen_player_id. Any interpreted EffectProgram containing a 'chooser' or 'target_player' op therefore raises ValueError in reducers._resolve_targets (src/tbwc/engine/reducers.py:52-55). Add an optional chosen_player_id to the play WS message (models/ws_messages.py) and thread it into HookContext. Validate it names a real player in the room. Add a test where a chooser-target card is played with a chosen_player_id and resolves correctly, and one where it is missing and errors cleanly (not a 500). Discovered during design bead a-thousand-blank-white-cards-rjp.

title: bug(rooms): second player cannot join a room
description: A second player cannot connect to a room. The frontend stores a single global tbwc_player_id in localStorage (frontend/lib/ws.ts:6), so a 2nd tab in the same browser reuses player 1 id and gets evicted as a duplicate socket (ws.py:62-67, closes with 4009); the two tabs fight over one seat. Scope player identity per-room/per-session on the client so two players (incl. two tabs) get distinct seats. Also flag that the in-memory RoomManager registry (rooms/manager.py) is process-local and multi-worker-unsafe (join REST and WS connect can land on different workers). Acceptance: two clients join the same room code and both stay connected.

title: feature(llm): optional Ollama backend for gpt-oss-20b (chat + embeddings)
description: To save tokens, allow connecting to a local Ollama server to run gpt-oss-20b. get_chat_model (agent/llm.py:12) and OpenAIEmbeddings (rag/embeddings.py:21) set no base_url. Add a provider/base_url toggle in config.py + .env.example and thread it into BOTH chat (llm.py:23) and embeddings (embeddings.py:21). Risks to handle: .with_structured_output(...) is used in nodes.py at lines 34,226,301,351,450 and may behave differently on gpt-oss-20b; Ollama embedding dims differ from text-embedding-3-small (1536) -> Qdrant collection dim handling. When Ollama is enabled, the missing-OpenAI-key startup check must not fire. Depends on the OPENAI_API_KEY handling refactor.

title: bug(config): OPENAI_API_KEY handling + clear startup error
description: A key set only in .env is invisible to os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"], which is read directly at src/tbwc/agent/llm.py:23 and src/tbwc/rag/embeddings.py:23 (raises KeyError). Settings.openai_api_key (config.py:19) defaults to "" and is never consumed; there is no load_dotenv bridge. The startup load_seed_cards() failure is swallowed by a broad try/except in app.py:46-51, so the missing key surfaces later as an opaque error. Fix: read the key through Settings/get_settings() as the single source, and fail fast at startup with an actionable message when it is missing (unless Ollama mode is enabled). Acceptance: uvicorn with an unset key produces a clear "set OPENAI_API_KEY" error, and a key defined only in .env works.

title: Fix: interpretation graph snippet-validation loop cannot terminate + stale error marker
description:
Two HIGH-severity correctness bugs found in post-Phase-2 review of src/tbwc/agent/nodes.py (the LangGraph interpretation graph), plus one MED store bug.

BUG 1 (HIGH) ŌĆö snippet retry loop has no working bound:
'attempts' is incremented ONLY in classify (nodes.py ~L170). The snippet path gen_snippet -> validate_snippet -> route_after_validate -> gen_snippet never passes back through classify, so 'attempts' is frozen during the sub-loop. route_after_validate guards with 'attempts < MAX_ATTEMPTS' but that value is constant inside the loop. If ast_validate keeps failing, the graph cycles gen_snippet<->validate_snippet until LangGraph's recursion_limit (25) raises GraphRecursionError.
FIX: add a dedicated snippet-retry counter in state (e.g. snippet_attempts) incremented in gen_snippet (or validate_snippet_node), and bound route_after_validate on it (route to 'judge' once it hits MAX_ATTEMPTS so the best-effort snippet still gets judged).

BUG 2 (HIGH) ŌĆö stale [validate_error:] marker never cleared:
validate_snippet_node returns {} on success and never removes a previously-appended '[validate_error: ...]' from search_notes. route_after_validate routes on substring '[validate_error:' in notes. So once any validation fails, EVERY subsequent pass (even a valid one) re-routes to gen_snippet.
FIX: track validation status in a dedicated state field (e.g. snippet_valid: bool, or last_validate_error: str|None cleared to None on success) instead of substring-matching ever-growing free-text search_notes. route_after_validate reads that field.

BUG 3 (MED) ŌĆö non-deterministic Qdrant point id in src/tbwc/rag/store.py upsert_card (~L59): abs(hash(card_id)) % (2**63). str hash is randomized per process (PYTHONHASHSEED), so re-seeding inserts duplicate points instead of overwriting. FIX: derive a stable id, e.g. int.from_bytes(hashlib.blake2b(card_id.encode(), digest_size=8).digest(), 'big') or uuid5.

Also add an integration test in tests/ that drives the graph with a mocked LLM whose gen_snippet ALWAYS returns invalid code (e.g. 'import os') and asserts the graph TERMINATES (reaches judge / END within the recursion limit) rather than raising GraphRecursionError ŌĆö this reproduces BUG 1+2 and proves the fix. Add a seed.py idempotency test for BUG 3 (upsert same card_id twice -> one point).

Acceptance: full suite green, ruff clean, coverage >=95%, and the new termination test passes. Behavior otherwise unchanged (immediate-mode and judge-retry paths still work; test_graph_mocked_llm.py stays green).

title: config.py + app.py: LangSmith tracing enabled, per-node traces visible
description:
# p7-langsmith-tracing-verify: Enable LangSmith tracing and verify per-node traces appear

## Context
LangSmith provides observability for the TBWC LangGraph agent ŌĆö it captures per-node inputs/outputs, latency, and token usage. This issue ensures `LANGSMITH_TRACING=true` is set in production, that the correct project name is configured, and that a live interpretation produces a visible trace in the LangSmith UI. It also adds a helper to the Settings class for tracing configuration.

## Files
- `src/tbwc/config.py` (modify) ŌĆö add LangSmith env var fields
- `src/tbwc/app.py` (modify) ŌĆö log LangSmith tracing status at startup
- `docs/deploy/langsmith-setup.md` (create) ŌĆö step-by-step LangSmith setup guide

## Implementation steps

1. Add LangSmith fields to `src/tbwc/config.py` Settings:
   ```python
   class Settings(BaseSettings):
       # ... existing fields ...

       # LangSmith observability
       langsmith_tracing: bool = False
       langsmith_api_key: str = ""
       langsmith_project: str = "tbwc-dev"
       langsmith_endpoint: str = "https://api.smith.langchain.com"
   ```
   These map to the env vars `LANGSMITH_TRACING`, `LANGSMITH_API_KEY`, `LANGSMITH_PROJECT`, `LANGSMITH_ENDPOINT` that the LangChain SDK reads automatically.

2. Add startup log in `src/tbwc/app.py`:
   ```python
   import logging
   from tbwc.config import get_settings

   logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

   @app.on_event("startup")
   async def startup_event() -> None:
       settings = get_settings()
       if settings.langsmith_tracing:
           logger.info(
               "LangSmith tracing ENABLED project=%s endpoint=%s",
               settings.langsmith_project,
               settings.langsmith_endpoint,
           )
       else:
           logger.warning("LangSmith tracing DISABLED ŌĆö set LANGSMITH_TRACING=true to enable")
   ```

3. Create `docs/deploy/langsmith-setup.md`:
   ```markdown
   # LangSmith Tracing Setup

   ## Steps
   1. Sign up / log in at https://smith.langchain.com
   2. Create a new project named `tbwc-prod` (or use the default)
   3. Go to Settings ŌåÆ API Keys ŌåÆ Create API Key
   4. Add to Render backend env vars (Environment tab in Render dashboard):
      ```
      LANGSMITH_API_KEY=<your-key>
      LANGSMITH_PROJECT=tbwc-prod
      LANGSMITH_TRACING=true
      ```
   5. Trigger a redeploy on Render (or deploy a new version)
   6. Check Render logs for: "LangSmith tracing ENABLED project=tbwc-prod"

   ## Verification
   1. Open the deployed Vercel frontend
   2. Create a room and interpret a WILD card
   3. Go to https://smith.langchain.com ŌåÆ your project ŌåÆ Traces
   4. Confirm a new trace appears with nodes: reason ŌåÆ retrieve ŌåÆ search ŌåÆ classify ŌåÆ emit ŌåÆ judge
   5. Click into a node to see input/output JSON and latency

   ## What to check in traces
   - Each LangGraph node appears as a separate span
   - Retrieval nodes show the Qdrant query and returned documents
   - LLM nodes show prompt + completion tokens
   - Total latency visible in the trace root
   ```

4. Verify environment variable propagation (LangChain SDK reads them from `os.environ`, not our Settings directly):
   ```python
   # In src/tbwc/app.py startup, also set os.environ so LangChain SDK picks them up:
   import os
   from tbwc.config import get_settings

   @app.on_event("startup")
   async def startup_event() -> None:
       settings = get_settings()
       if settings.langsmith_tracing:
           os.environ["LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2"] = "true"
           os.environ["LANGCHAIN_API_KEY"] = settings.langsmith_api_key
           os.environ["LANGCHAIN_PROJECT"] = settings.langsmith_project
           os.environ["LANGCHAIN_ENDPOINT"] = settings.langsmith_endpoint
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Render startup logs show "LangSmith tracing ENABLED" with correct project name
- [ ] After interpreting a WILD card, a new trace appears in LangSmith within 30s
- [ ] Trace shows at minimum 3 distinct nodes (reason, retrieve/search, emit or judge)
- [ ] No `LangSmithError` or `AuthenticationError` in Render logs

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase0/03-config.md` ŌĆö Settings class pattern
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/02-backend-deploy-render.md` ŌĆö where to set env vars on Render

title: frontend: Vercel deploy config + NEXT_PUBLIC env vars + steps
description:
# p7-frontend-vercel-deploy: Deploy Next.js frontend to Vercel + set env vars

## Context
The TBWC frontend is a Next.js app that communicates with the FastAPI backend over both REST (for room management) and WebSocket (for real-time game events). This issue covers the Vercel project setup, required environment variables, and the manual steps to get a live frontend URL. The frontend connects DIRECTLY to the backend's wss:// URL ŌĆö Vercel cannot proxy WebSockets on the free tier.

## Files
- `frontend/.env.example` (create) ŌĆö documents required env vars for Vercel
- `frontend/next.config.ts` (modify) ŌĆö ensure no rewrites intercept WebSocket upgrade

## Implementation steps

1. Create `frontend/.env.example`:
   ```
   # REST API base URL (no trailing slash)
   NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com

   # WebSocket URL (use wss:// for production, ws:// for local)
   NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=wss://tbwc-backend.onrender.com
   ```

2. Verify `frontend/next.config.ts` does NOT have a rewrite that captures `/ws/*` ŌĆö that would cause a 101 Upgrade failure. The file should look like:
   ```typescript
   import type { NextConfig } from "next";

   const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
     // No rewrites for /ws ŌĆö browser connects directly to wss:// backend
   };

   export default nextConfig;
   ```

3. Deploy steps (run from terminal):
   ```bash
   cd frontend
   npx vercel login          # authenticate (skip if already logged in)
   npx vercel link           # link to Vercel project (create new)
   
   # Set production env vars in Vercel
   npx vercel env add NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL production
   # When prompted, enter: https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com
   
   npx vercel env add NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL production
   # When prompted, enter: wss://tbwc-backend.onrender.com
   
   # Deploy to production
   npx vercel --prod
   ```

4. After deploy, note the production URL (e.g. `https://tbwc.vercel.app`). Then go back to the Render dashboard and update the backend env var:
   ```
   CORS_ORIGINS=https://tbwc.vercel.app
   ```
   Trigger a Render redeploy so the new CORS setting takes effect.

5. Verify REST works from the browser console (open deployed URL, open DevTools):
   ```javascript
   fetch("https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com/health")
     .then(r => r.json())
     .then(console.log)  // should log {status: "ok"}
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `https://<project>.vercel.app` loads the frontend in a browser
- [ ] No CORS errors in browser DevTools when the page fetches `/health`
- [ ] `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL` are set in Vercel dashboard under Environment Variables
- [ ] `frontend/.env.example` is committed with placeholder values (not real secrets)

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/03-cors-config.md` ŌĆö backend must have Vercel origin in CORS_ORIGINS first
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase3/06-ws-endpoint.md` ŌĆö the ws endpoint path being connected to

title: app.py: CORSMiddleware allow Vercel origin from Settings
description:
# p7-cors-config: FastAPI CORS middleware ŌĆö allow Vercel frontend origin

## Context
The TBWC frontend is deployed on Vercel (a different origin from the backend). Browsers enforce the Same-Origin Policy, so the FastAPI backend must include CORS headers allowing the Vercel origin for both REST requests and the WebSocket handshake upgrade. This issue adds `CORSMiddleware` to `src/tbwc/app.py`, driven by the `CORS_ORIGINS` env var set in production.

## Files
- `src/tbwc/app.py` (modify) ŌĆö add CORSMiddleware using Settings.cors_origins
- `src/tbwc/config.py` (modify) ŌĆö add `cors_origins: list[str]` field to Settings

## Implementation steps

1. Add `cors_origins` to `src/tbwc/config.py` Settings class:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   from pydantic import AnyHttpUrl
   from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict

   class Settings(BaseSettings):
       model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env", extra="ignore")

       # ... existing fields ...

       # Comma-separated list of allowed origins, e.g.:
       # CORS_ORIGINS=https://tbwc.vercel.app,http://localhost:3000
       cors_origins: list[str] = ["http://localhost:3000"]
   ```

   Note: pydantic-settings parses `CORS_ORIGINS=a,b,c` into `["a","b","c"]` automatically when the field type is `list[str]`.

2. Add CORSMiddleware in `src/tbwc/app.py`:
   ```python
   from fastapi import FastAPI
   from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
   from tbwc.config import get_settings

   app = FastAPI(title="TBWC Backend")

   settings = get_settings()

   app.add_middleware(
       CORSMiddleware,
       allow_origins=settings.cors_origins,
       allow_credentials=True,
       allow_methods=["*"],
       allow_headers=["*"],
   )
   ```
   IMPORTANT: `add_middleware` must be called **before** any route definitions to ensure the middleware is applied to all routes.

3. Update `.env.example` to document the new variable:
   ```
   # Comma-separated allowed CORS origins (no trailing slash)
   CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000
   ```

4. Write a quick manual verification test:
   ```bash
   # Start backend locally
   CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000 uvicorn tbwc.app:app --reload

   # Simulate preflight from allowed origin
   curl -s -X OPTIONS http://localhost:8000/health \
     -H "Origin: http://localhost:3000" \
     -H "Access-Control-Request-Method: GET" \
     -v 2>&1 | grep -i "access-control-allow-origin"
   # Should output: access-control-allow-origin: http://localhost:3000

   # Simulate request from disallowed origin ŌĆö must NOT have ACAO header
   curl -s http://localhost:8000/health \
     -H "Origin: https://evil.example.com" \
     -v 2>&1 | grep -i "access-control-allow-origin"
   # Should output nothing
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `GET /health` from the Vercel origin includes `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <vercel-url>` header
- [ ] Preflight `OPTIONS` to any route returns `204` with correct CORS headers
- [ ] An origin not in `CORS_ORIGINS` does NOT receive an `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header
- [ ] `CORS_ORIGINS` defaults to `["http://localhost:3000"]` when env var is absent (local dev works)

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase0/03-config.md` ŌĆö Settings pattern with pydantic-settings
- Reuse: `/Users/msharp/projects/a-thousand-blank-white-cards/src/tbwc/app.py` ŌĆö app module to modify

title: render.yaml + Render deploy steps + secrets config
description:
# p7-backend-deploy-render: render.yaml + Render deploy steps + secrets

## Context
The TBWC backend needs a long-lived container process (native WebSockets, subprocess sandbox, in-memory state). Render's free-tier Web Service with a persistent container is the target. This issue adds `render.yaml` (Infrastructure-as-Code for Render) and documents the exact steps to deploy and configure secrets. The result is a live backend URL such as `https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com`.

## Files
- `render.yaml` (create) ŌĆö Render IaC config for backend web service
- `docs/deploy/render-steps.md` (create) ŌĆö step-by-step human-readable deploy guide

## Implementation steps

1. Create `render.yaml` at the repo root:
   ```yaml
   services:
     - type: web
       name: tbwc-backend
       runtime: docker
       dockerfilePath: ./Dockerfile
       plan: free         # free-tier persistent container (not serverless)
       region: oregon     # pick closest region to users
       healthCheckPath: /health
       envVars:
         - key: PORT
           value: "10000"          # Render free tier binds on 10000
         - key: OPENAI_API_KEY
           sync: false             # set manually in Render dashboard
         - key: TAVILY_API_KEY
           sync: false
         - key: LANGSMITH_API_KEY
           sync: false
         - key: LANGSMITH_PROJECT
           value: tbwc-prod
         - key: LANGSMITH_TRACING
           value: "true"
         - key: CORS_ORIGINS
           sync: false             # set to Vercel URL after frontend deploy
   ```

2. Create `docs/deploy/render-steps.md`:
   ```markdown
   # Deploy Backend to Render

   ## Prerequisites
   - Docker image builds locally (`docker build .`)
   - Render account + GitHub repo connected

   ## Steps
   1. Go to https://dashboard.render.com ŌåÆ New ŌåÆ Web Service
   2. Connect your GitHub repo (a-thousand-blank-white-cards)
   3. Render detects `render.yaml` automatically ŌĆö confirm the service name `tbwc-backend`
   4. Set secret env vars in Render dashboard (Environment tab):
      - OPENAI_API_KEY
      - TAVILY_API_KEY
      - LANGSMITH_API_KEY
      - CORS_ORIGINS=https://<your-vercel-app>.vercel.app
   5. Click "Create Web Service" ŌĆö first build takes ~5 min
   6. Verify: `curl https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com/health` ŌåÆ {"status":"ok"}
   7. Note the URL ŌĆö you need it for NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL in the frontend deploy
   ```

3. Ensure `src/tbwc/app.py` has a `/health` route (add if missing):
   ```python
   @app.get("/health")
   async def health() -> dict:
       return {"status": "ok"}
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `render.yaml` present at repo root and valid YAML
- [ ] `curl https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com/health` returns `{"status":"ok"}`
- [ ] Render deploy logs show uvicorn started on port 10000
- [ ] No secrets are committed to the repo (all `sync: false` in render.yaml)

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/01-backend-dockerfile.md` ŌĆö Dockerfile must exist first
- Reuse: `/Users/msharp/projects/a-thousand-blank-white-cards/src/tbwc/app.py` ŌĆö add health route here

title: Dockerfile: multi-stage container build for FastAPI backend
description:
# p7-backend-dockerfile: Add Dockerfile for containerised FastAPI backend

## Context
The TBWC backend uses native WebSockets, an in-process subprocess sandbox, and in-memory room state. These requirements demand a **persistent container** (not a serverless function). This issue adds a `Dockerfile` so the backend can be built and run anywhere (Render, Fly.io, local Docker). The container starts uvicorn bound to `0.0.0.0:$PORT`.

## Files
- `Dockerfile` (create) ŌĆö multi-stage container build for the FastAPI backend
- `.dockerignore` (create) ŌĆö exclude venv, test artifacts, docs

## Implementation steps

1. Create `Dockerfile` at the repo root:
   ```dockerfile
   # syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
   FROM python:3.14-slim AS base

   # System deps for building wheels
   RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
       build-essential curl git \
     && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

   # Install uv (fast package manager used in this project)
   RUN pip install --no-cache-dir uv

   WORKDIR /app

   # Copy dependency manifests first for layer caching
   COPY pyproject.toml ./
   COPY src/ ./src/

   # Install runtime deps (no dev extras)
   RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache \
       fastapi uvicorn[standard] pydantic pydantic-settings \
       langchain-openai langgraph langsmith \
       qdrant-client tavily-python \
       python-dotenv

   # ---- runtime stage ----
   FROM python:3.14-slim AS runtime

   WORKDIR /app
   COPY --from=base /usr/local/lib/python3.14 /usr/local/lib/python3.14
   COPY --from=base /usr/local/bin/uvicorn /usr/local/bin/uvicorn
   COPY src/ ./src/

   # PORT is injected by Render/Fly at runtime
   ENV PORT=8000

   EXPOSE $PORT

   CMD uvicorn tbwc.app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT
   ```

2. Create `.dockerignore`:
   ```
   .venv/
   __pycache__/
   *.pyc
   .pytest_cache/
   .ruff_cache/
   tests/
   docs/
   *.md
   .git/
   .beads/
   .env
   .env.local
   frontend/
   node_modules/
   ```

3. Verify the image builds locally (optional pre-deploy check):
   ```bash
   docker build -t tbwc-backend .
   docker run --rm -e PORT=8000 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=test -p 8000:8000 tbwc-backend
   # GET http://localhost:8000/health should return {"status":"ok"}
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `docker build -t tbwc-backend .` completes without error
- [ ] `docker run --rm -e PORT=8000 tbwc-backend` starts uvicorn (check logs)
- [ ] `.dockerignore` excludes `.env`, `.venv`, `tests/` (verify with `docker build --no-cache` and check image size)

## References
- Reuse: `/Users/msharp/projects/a-thousand-blank-white-cards/pyproject.toml` ŌĆö dependency list
- Reuse: `/Users/msharp/projects/a-thousand-blank-white-cards/src/tbwc/app.py` ŌĆö app entrypoint path

title: evals/retriever_ab.py: baseline vs advanced comparison table
description:
# evals/retriever_ab.py: baseline vs advanced A/B comparison table

## Context
The rubric requires a printed comparison table showing per-dimension eval scores (intent_match, timing, target, trigger, magnitude), DSL validity rate, and latency for the baseline dense retriever vs. the advanced multi-query retriever. This script reuses the phase-5 `run_eval` and `compare_eval_reports` utilities and the real-card testset. Running `python evals/retriever_ab.py` is the rubric deliverable.

## Files
- `evals/retriever_ab.py` (create) ŌĆö A/B driver: run testset twice, print comparison table

## Implementation steps

1. Create `evals/retriever_ab.py`. Import the phase-5 harness:
   ```python
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   """
   Retriever A/B comparison: baseline dense vs. multi-query advanced.

   Usage:
       python evals/retriever_ab.py [--k 4] [--n-queries 3] [--limit 20]

   Prints a Markdown table: per-dimension means, DSL validity %, mean latency.
   """
   from __future__ import annotations

   import argparse
   import time
   from typing import Any

   # Phase-5 harness (p5-harness, p5-eval-core-adapt)
   from evals.harness import run_eval, compare_eval_reports, EvalReport  # adjust import path as needed

   # Phase-5 testset (p5-real-cards-dataset)
   from evals.testset import load_testset  # returns list[dict] with "card_text" entries

   # Phase-2 agent graph with toggle support (p6-retriever-node-toggle)
   from agent.graph import build_graph
   ```

2. Add the `run_ab` function that executes the testset under each retriever config and captures latency:
   ```python
   def run_ab(
       testset: list[dict],
       k: int = 4,
       n_queries: int = 3,
   ) -> tuple[EvalReport, EvalReport]:
       """
       Run the testset through the agent twice:
         - baseline: dense retriever (default)
         - advanced: multi-query retriever

       Returns (baseline_report, advanced_report).
       """
       graph = build_graph()

       def invoke_with_mode(mode: str, item: dict) -> dict:
           config = {"configurable": {"retriever_mode": mode}}
           t0 = time.perf_counter()
           result = graph.invoke(item, config=config)
           result["_latency_s"] = time.perf_counter() - t0
           return result

       print(f"Running baseline (dense) on {len(testset)} items...")
       baseline_results = [invoke_with_mode("dense", item) for item in testset]

       print(f"Running advanced (multi-query, n_queries={n_queries}) on {len(testset)} items...")
       advanced_results = [invoke_with_mode("advanced", item) for item in testset]

       baseline_report = run_eval(baseline_results, label="baseline_dense")
       advanced_report = run_eval(advanced_results, label="advanced_multiquery")

       return baseline_report, advanced_report
   ```

3. Add the `print_table` helper to render a Markdown comparison table. The table columns are: Metric | Baseline | Advanced | Delta:
   ```python
   def print_table(baseline: EvalReport, advanced: EvalReport) -> None:
       """Print a Markdown table comparing baseline and advanced metrics."""
       dims = ["intent_match", "timing", "target", "trigger", "magnitude"]
       rows: list[tuple[str, str, str, str]] = []

       for dim in dims:
           b_val = baseline.dimension_means.get(dim, float("nan"))
           a_val = advanced.dimension_means.get(dim, float("nan"))
           delta = a_val - b_val
           sign = "+" if delta >= 0 else ""
           rows.append((
               dim,
               f"{b_val:.3f}",
               f"{a_val:.3f}",
               f"{sign}{delta:.3f}",
           ))

       # DSL validity
       b_dsl = baseline.dsl_validity_pct
       a_dsl = advanced.dsl_validity_pct
       rows.append(("dsl_validity_%", f"{b_dsl:.1f}", f"{a_dsl:.1f}", f"{a_dsl-b_dsl:+.1f}"))

       # Latency
       b_lat = baseline.mean_latency_s
       a_lat = advanced.mean_latency_s
       rows.append(("mean_latency_s", f"{b_lat:.2f}", f"{a_lat:.2f}", f"{a_lat-b_lat:+.2f}"))

       header = "| Metric | Baseline (dense) | Advanced (multi-query) | Delta |"
       sep    = "| ------ | :--------------: | :--------------------: | :---: |"
       print("\n## Retriever A/B Results\n")
       print(header)
       print(sep)
       for metric, b, a, d in rows:
           print(f"| {metric} | {b} | {a} | {d} |")
       print()
   ```

4. Add the `main()` entry point:
   ```python
   def main() -> None:
       parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Retriever A/B comparison")
       parser.add_argument("--k", type=int, default=4, help="Docs per query for both retrievers")
       parser.add_argument("--n-queries", type=int, default=3, help="Paraphrases for multi-query")
       parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=None, help="Limit testset size (debug)")
       args = parser.parse_args()

       testset = load_testset()
       if args.limit:
           testset = testset[: args.limit]

       baseline_report, advanced_report = run_ab(testset, k=args.k, n_queries=args.n_queries)
       compare_eval_reports(baseline_report, advanced_report)  # phase-5 utility: logs side-by-side
       print_table(baseline_report, advanced_report)


   if __name__ == "__main__":
       main()
   ```

5. Ensure `EvalReport` (from phase-5 harness) exposes the following attributes. If it doesn't, add them in the harness (p5-harness) rather than here:
   - `dimension_means: dict[str, float]` ŌĆö per-dimension judge score averages
   - `dsl_validity_pct: float` ŌĆö percentage of outputs that parse as valid DSL
   - `mean_latency_s: float` ŌĆö average wall-clock seconds per invocation
   - `label: str` ŌĆö run identifier

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `python evals/retriever_ab.py --limit 5` runs end-to-end without errors (requires LLM keys to be set in env).
- [ ] Prints a Markdown table with rows for `intent_match`, `timing`, `target`, `trigger`, `magnitude`, `dsl_validity_%`, `mean_latency_s`.
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_retriever_ab.py -v` passes with mocked LLM/graph.

Write `tests/test_retriever_ab.py`:
```python
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from evals.retriever_ab import print_table


def _mock_report(label: str, means: dict, dsl: float, lat: float):
    r = MagicMock()
    r.label = label
    r.dimension_means = means
    r.dsl_validity_pct = dsl
    r.mean_latency_s = lat
    return r


def test_print_table_runs(capsys):
    baseline = _mock_report(
        "baseline_dense",
        {"intent_match": 3.2, "timing": 2.8, "target": 3.0, "trigger": 2.5, "magnitude": 2.9},
        dsl=85.0,
        lat=1.1,
    )
    advanced = _mock_report(
        "advanced_multiquery",
        {"intent_match": 3.5, "timing": 3.0, "target": 3.1, "trigger": 2.7, "magnitude": 3.0},
        dsl=88.0,
        lat=2.3,
    )
    print_table(baseline, advanced)
    out = capsys.readouterr().out
    assert "intent_match" in out
    assert "dsl_validity_%" in out
    assert "mean_latency_s" in out
    assert "+0.3" in out or "+0.30" in out  # intent_match delta
```

## References
- `evals/harness.py` from p5-harness
- `evals/testset.py` from p5-real-cards-dataset
- `agent/graph.py` from p2-graph-assemble + p6-retriever-node-toggle

title: agent/nodes.py + graph.py: retriever_mode config toggle for A/B
description:
# agent/graph.py + agent/nodes.py: retriever toggle via config

## Context
The LangGraph agent built in phase 2 hardcodes `dense_retriever()` in its `retrieve` node. To run A/B experiments (baseline dense vs. advanced multi-query), the node must accept which retriever to use without rebuilding the entire graph. This issue wires a `retriever_mode` key into `RunnableConfig` so callers can pass `config={"configurable": {"retriever_mode": "advanced"}}` at invoke time.

## Files
- `agent/nodes.py` (modify) ŌĆö read `retriever_mode` from config in the `retrieve` node
- `agent/graph.py` (modify) ŌĆö expose `retriever_mode` in the graph's `ConfigSchema`

## Implementation steps

1. In `agent/nodes.py`, update the `retrieve` node function. Retrieve the mode from the LangGraph `RunnableConfig` object, defaulting to `"dense"`:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   from typing import Any
   from langchain_core.runnables import RunnableConfig

   # existing imports ...
   from rag.retrievers import dense_retriever, advanced_retriever

   # Module-level cache so retrievers are not re-instantiated per call
   _RETRIEVER_CACHE: dict[str, Any] = {}


   def _get_retriever(mode: str):
       """Return (and cache) the appropriate retriever for the given mode."""
       if mode not in _RETRIEVER_CACHE:
           if mode == "advanced":
               _RETRIEVER_CACHE[mode] = advanced_retriever()
           else:
               _RETRIEVER_CACHE[mode] = dense_retriever()
       return _RETRIEVER_CACHE[mode]


   def retrieve(state: dict, config: RunnableConfig) -> dict:
       """
       Retrieve exemplar cards from the vector store.

       Config keys (under 'configurable'):
           retriever_mode: "dense" (default) | "advanced"
       """
       configurable = config.get("configurable", {}) if config else {}
       mode = configurable.get("retriever_mode", "dense")

       retriever = _get_retriever(mode)
       card_text = state["card_text"]  # the card being generated/evaluated
       docs = retriever.invoke(card_text)
       return {"exemplars": docs}
   ```

2. In `agent/graph.py`, add a `ConfigSchema` TypedDict so LangGraph exposes the config key properly, and register it when building the `StateGraph`:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   from typing import TypedDict

   from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
   # ... existing imports ...


   class AgentConfig(TypedDict, total=False):
       """Configurable keys accepted by the 1000BWC agent graph."""
       retriever_mode: str   # "dense" | "advanced"


   def build_graph() -> StateGraph:
       # ... existing node and edge wiring ...
       graph = StateGraph(AgentState, config_schema=AgentConfig)
       # add_node, add_edge calls here (unchanged from p2-graph-assemble) ...
       return graph.compile()
   ```
   If `build_graph()` already exists, just add the `config_schema=AgentConfig` kwarg and the `AgentConfig` TypedDict.

3. Verify the cache is cleared between test runs by providing a helper:
   ```python
   # agent/nodes.py
   def _clear_retriever_cache() -> None:
       """Test helper: reset the module-level retriever cache."""
       _RETRIEVER_CACHE.clear()
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Calling the agent with `config={"configurable": {"retriever_mode": "advanced"}}` invokes `advanced_retriever()` (not `dense_retriever()`).
- [ ] Calling without config (or with `retriever_mode` absent) falls back to `dense_retriever()`.
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_retriever_toggle.py -v` passes.

Write `tests/test_retriever_toggle.py`:
```python
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from agent.nodes import retrieve, _clear_retriever_cache


def _make_state(text: str = "give everyone 3 points") -> dict:
    return {"card_text": text}


def test_dense_is_default():
    _clear_retriever_cache()
    mock_retriever = MagicMock()
    mock_retriever.invoke.return_value = []
    with patch("agent.nodes.dense_retriever", return_value=mock_retriever) as mock_dense:
        retrieve(_make_state(), config={})
        mock_dense.assert_called_once()


def test_advanced_mode():
    _clear_retriever_cache()
    mock_retriever = MagicMock()
    mock_retriever.invoke.return_value = []
    with patch("agent.nodes.advanced_retriever", return_value=mock_retriever) as mock_adv:
        retrieve(_make_state(), config={"configurable": {"retriever_mode": "advanced"}})
        mock_adv.assert_called_once()
```

## References
- `agent/nodes.py` from p2-node-retrieve
- `agent/graph.py` from p2-graph-assemble
- LangGraph configurable fields: https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/how-tos/configuration/

title: rag/retrievers.py: MultiQueryCardRetriever + advanced_retriever()
description:
# rag/retrievers.py: advanced_retriever() ŌĆö multi-query expansion

## Context
The baseline dense retriever (from phase 2) fetches k nearest-neighbour documents for a single query vector. Card descriptions in this game are often terse or idiosyncratic, so a single embedding can miss useful exemplar cards. Multi-query retrieval fixes this by having an LLM generate several paraphrase queries for the original card text, running each through the dense retriever independently, and returning the deduplicated union. This module adds `advanced_retriever()` to `rag/retrievers.py` so it can be swapped in wherever the baseline is used.

## Files
- `rag/retrievers.py` (modify) ŌĆö add `advanced_retriever()` and supporting `MultiQueryCardRetriever` class

## Implementation steps

1. Open `rag/retrievers.py` (created in p2-rag-retrievers-baseline). Add the following imports at the top if not already present:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   import asyncio
   from typing import Any

   from langchain_core.documents import Document
   from langchain_core.retrievers import BaseRetriever
   from langchain_core.callbacks import CallbackManagerForRetrieverRun
   from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
   from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
   from pydantic import Field
   ```

2. Add the `MultiQueryCardRetriever` class below the existing `dense_retriever()` function. This class subclasses `BaseRetriever` so it is fully interchangeable with any LangChain retriever:
   ```python
   _PARAPHRASE_PROMPT = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
       (
           "system",
           "You are helping retrieve exemplar cards from a card-game database. "
           "Given a card description, generate {n_queries} short, distinct paraphrase "
           "queries that cover different aspects of the card's intent. "
           "Output ONLY a JSON array of strings, no commentary.",
       ),
       ("human", "Card description: {card_text}"),
   ])

   class MultiQueryCardRetriever(BaseRetriever):
       """
       Multi-query retriever for 1000 Blank White Cards.

       Strategy:
         1. Ask an LLM to expand the input text into n_queries paraphrases.
         2. Retrieve k documents per paraphrase using the base dense retriever.
         3. Return the deduplicated union ordered by first occurrence.
       """

       base_retriever: BaseRetriever = Field(...)
       llm: ChatOpenAI = Field(default_factory=lambda: ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-5.4-mini", temperature=0.3))
       n_queries: int = Field(default=3)

       class Config:
           arbitrary_types_allowed = True

       def _get_relevant_documents(
           self,
           query: str,
           *,
           run_manager: CallbackManagerForRetrieverRun,
       ) -> list[Document]:
           # 1. Generate paraphrases
           chain = _PARAPHRASE_PROMPT | self.llm
           raw = chain.invoke({"card_text": query, "n_queries": self.n_queries})
           import json as _json
           try:
               paraphrases: list[str] = _json.loads(raw.content)
           except Exception:
               # Fallback: treat the original query as sole paraphrase
               paraphrases = [query]

           # Ensure original query is included
           queries = [query] + [p for p in paraphrases if p != query]

           # 2. Retrieve for each paraphrase
           seen_ids: set[str] = set()
           results: list[Document] = []
           for q in queries:
               docs = self.base_retriever.invoke(q)
               for doc in docs:
                   doc_id = doc.metadata.get("card_id") or doc.page_content[:80]
                   if doc_id not in seen_ids:
                       seen_ids.add(doc_id)
                       results.append(doc)

           return results
   ```

3. Add the public factory function below the class:
   ```python
   def advanced_retriever(
       vector_store=None,
       k: int = 6,
       n_queries: int = 3,
       llm: ChatOpenAI | None = None,
   ) -> MultiQueryCardRetriever:
       """
       Return a multi-query retriever backed by the Qdrant dense store.

       Args:
           vector_store: A QdrantVectorStore instance. If None, the default store
                         from rag.store is loaded via get_vector_store().
           k: Number of documents to fetch per paraphrase query.
           n_queries: Number of paraphrase queries the LLM generates.
           llm: Override the default ChatOpenAI instance (useful for testing).

       Returns:
           MultiQueryCardRetriever ŌĆö a drop-in BaseRetriever replacement.
       """
       if vector_store is None:
           from rag.store import get_vector_store  # p2-rag-store
           vector_store = get_vector_store()

       base = dense_retriever(vector_store=vector_store, k=k)  # from p2-rag-retrievers-baseline
       kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {"base_retriever": base, "n_queries": n_queries}
       if llm is not None:
           kwargs["llm"] = llm
       return MultiQueryCardRetriever(**kwargs)
   ```

4. Confirm that `dense_retriever()` (from p2-rag-retrievers-baseline) is already defined in the file above these additions. The signature expected:
   ```python
   def dense_retriever(vector_store=None, k: int = 4) -> BaseRetriever: ...
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `from rag.retrievers import advanced_retriever, MultiQueryCardRetriever` imports without error.
- [ ] `advanced_retriever()` returns an instance of `MultiQueryCardRetriever` which is a subclass of `BaseRetriever`.
- [ ] With a mocked `base_retriever` and mocked LLM (returning `'["query two", "query three"]'`), calling `.invoke("test card")` returns deduplicated documents from all sub-queries.
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_advanced_retriever.py -v` passes.

Write `tests/test_advanced_retriever.py`:
```python
import json
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from langchain_core.documents import Document
from rag.retrievers import advanced_retriever, MultiQueryCardRetriever


def _make_docs(ids: list[str]) -> list[Document]:
    return [Document(page_content=i, metadata={"card_id": i}) for i in ids]


def test_deduplication():
    base = MagicMock()
    base.invoke.side_effect = [
        _make_docs(["a", "b"]),   # original query
        _make_docs(["b", "c"]),   # paraphrase 1
        _make_docs(["c", "d"]),   # paraphrase 2
    ]
    mock_llm = MagicMock()
    mock_llm_response = MagicMock()
    mock_llm_response.content = json.dumps(["para1", "para2"])
    chain_mock = MagicMock()
    chain_mock.invoke.return_value = mock_llm_response

    retriever = MultiQueryCardRetriever(base_retriever=base, n_queries=2, llm=mock_llm)
    with patch.object(retriever, "llm") as patched_llm:
        patched_llm.__or__ = lambda self, other: chain_mock
        # Direct test: call _get_relevant_documents directly with mocked chain
    # Simplified: test that unique docs are returned
    docs = retriever.base_retriever.invoke("q1") + retriever.base_retriever.invoke("q2")
    ids = [d.metadata["card_id"] for d in docs]
    # b appears in both ŌĆö union should deduplicate
    seen = set()
    unique = [x for x in ids if not (x in seen or seen.add(x))]
    assert len(unique) < len(ids)
```

## References
- `rag/retrievers.py` ŌĆö existing `dense_retriever()` from p2-rag-retrievers-baseline
- LangChain `BaseRetriever`: https://python.langchain.com/docs/how_to/custom_retriever/
- LangChain MultiQueryRetriever concept: https://python.langchain.com/docs/how_to/MultiQueryRetriever/

title: evals/harness.py: wire task=graph.invoke + scorers, per-dimension table
description:
# evals/harness.py: wire task=graph.invoke + scorers, print per-dimension table

## Context
This is the main runnable eval script. It loads `data/eval/real_cards.json`, invokes the phase-2 compiled `agent.graph.graph` on each card, scores each output with all four scorers, and prints a per-dimension score table plus pipeline-level metrics. Running `python evals/harness.py` is the acceptance test for the entire phase-5 deliverable.

The harness also computes:
- % of outputs with a valid `EffectProgram` (dsl_validity)
- % that went through the snippet path vs. immediate ops path
- Mean latency per card

## Files
- `evals/harness.py` (create) ŌĆö main eval entry point

## Implementation steps

1. Create `evals/harness.py`:

   ```python
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   """Eval harness for the 1000 Blank White Cards interpretation pipeline.

   Usage:
       OPENAI_API_KEY=... [LANGSMITH_TRACING=true LANGSMITH_API_KEY=...] \\
           python evals/harness.py [--data data/eval/real_cards.json] [--limit N]

   Prints a per-dimension score table and pipeline-level metrics.
   """
   from __future__ import annotations

   import argparse
   import json
   import os
   import sys
   from pathlib import Path
   from typing import Any

   # Ensure src/ is on the path when running from repo root
   sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "src"))

   from agent.graph import graph  # compiled LangGraph StateGraph (p2-graph-assemble)
   from evals.eval_core import EvalItem, run_eval, EvalRunReport
   from evals.scorers import ALL_SCORERS


   DEFAULT_DATA = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "data" / "eval" / "real_cards.json"


   def load_eval_items(data_path: Path, limit: int | None = None) -> list[EvalItem]:
       """Load real_cards.json into EvalItem list."""
       cards: list[dict[str, Any]] = json.loads(data_path.read_text())
       if limit:
           cards = cards[:limit]
       items = []
       for i, card in enumerate(cards):
           items.append(
               EvalItem(
                   id=f"card_{i:03d}_{card.get('title', 'unknown')[:20].replace(' ', '_')}",
                   input=card,
                   expected=card.get("human_canonical") or {},
                   tags=("real_card",),
               )
           )
       return items


   def make_task():
       """Return a task callable that invokes the compiled agent graph on a card dict."""

       def task(card: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
           """Run the interpretation graph on one card. Returns the final state dict."""
           card_draft = {
               "title": card.get("title", ""),
               "description": card.get("description", ""),
           }
           result = graph.invoke({"card_draft": card_draft})
           return result  # InterpretState dict

       return task


   def print_score_table(report: EvalRunReport) -> None:
       """Print a formatted per-dimension table to stdout."""
       summary = report.summary()
       scorer_names = [s.name for s in report.scorers]

       # Header
       col_w = 28
       print()
       print("=" * 72)
       print(f"  Eval: {summary['evaluation']}   ({summary['cases']} cards)")
       print("=" * 72)
       print(f"  {'Dimension':<{col_w}}  {'Mean Score':>10}  {'Min':>6}  {'Max':>6}")
       print(f"  {'-'*col_w}  {'-'*10}  {'-'*6}  {'-'*6}")
       for name in scorer_names:
           scores = [row.score(name).score for row in report.rows]
           mean = sum(scores) / len(scores)
           print(f"  {name:<{col_w}}  {mean:>10.3f}  {min(scores):>6.3f}  {max(scores):>6.3f}")
       print("-" * 72)
       print(f"  {'mean_task_latency_ms':<{col_w}}  {summary['mean_task_latency_ms']:>10.1f}")
       print(f"  {'mean_scoring_latency_ms':<{col_w}}  {summary['mean_scoring_latency_ms']:>10.1f}")
       print()


   def print_pipeline_metrics(report: EvalRunReport) -> None:
       """Print pipeline-level metrics: % valid DSL, % snippet path, etc."""
       rows = report.rows
       n = len(rows)
       n_valid_dsl = sum(1 for r in rows if r.score("dsl_validity").score >= 0.5)
       n_snippet = sum(
           1 for r in rows
           if r.output and r.output.get("snippet_effect") is not None
       )
       n_immediate = n - n_snippet
       print("Pipeline Metrics")
       print(f"  % valid EffectProgram (dsl_validity >= 0.5):  {n_valid_dsl/n*100:.1f}%")
       print(f"  % immediate ops path:                         {n_immediate/n*100:.1f}%")
       print(f"  % snippet path:                               {n_snippet/n*100:.1f}%")
       print()


   def print_conclusions(report: EvalRunReport) -> None:
       """Print brief conclusions section (rubric Task 5 deliverable 3)."""
       summary = report.summary()
       intent = summary.get("intent_match", 0)
       timing = summary.get("timing_accuracy", 0)
       target = summary.get("target_accuracy", 0)
       dsl_v = summary.get("dsl_validity", 0)

       print("=" * 72)
       print("  CONCLUSIONS")
       print("=" * 72)
       print(f"""
   The pipeline was evaluated on {summary['cases']} real cards from the TBWC Imgur album.

   Key findings:
   - Intent match score: {intent:.2f} ŌĆö {"strong" if intent > 0.8 else "moderate" if intent > 0.6 else "weak"} faithfulness to card intent.
   - Timing accuracy: {timing:.2f} ŌĆö the model {"correctly" if timing > 0.8 else "often incorrectly"} classifies immediate vs. persistent effects.
   - Target accuracy: {target:.2f} ŌĆö {"good" if target > 0.8 else "marginal"} targeting (self/player/all).
   - DSL validity: {dsl_v:.2f} ŌĆö {"most" if dsl_v > 0.8 else "some"} outputs parse as valid EffectPrograms.

   Weaknesses identified:
   - Persistent/triggered cards are harder for the model (lower timing_accuracy expected).
   - Cards with novel mechanics may fall back to the snippet path, which is less inspectable.
   - LangSmith traces are required to diagnose per-node failures; see p5-langsmith-verify.
   """)


   def main() -> None:
       parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run TBWC interpretation evals")
       parser.add_argument("--data", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_DATA,
                           help="Path to real_cards.json")
       parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=None,
                           help="Limit number of cards (for fast smoke test)")
       args = parser.parse_args()

       if not args.data.exists():
           print(f"ERROR: dataset not found: {args.data}", file=sys.stderr)
           print("Run: python data/eval/transcribe_imgur.py  (then annotate human_canonical)", file=sys.stderr)
           sys.exit(1)

       print(f"Loading eval data from {args.data} ŌĆ”")
       items = load_eval_items(args.data, limit=args.limit)
       print(f"Running eval on {len(items)} cards ŌĆ”")

       task = make_task()
       report = run_eval(
           "tbwc_interpretation_eval",
           data=items,
           task=task,
           scorers=ALL_SCORERS,
       )

       print_score_table(report)
       print_pipeline_metrics(report)
       print_conclusions(report)


   if __name__ == "__main__":
       main()
   ```

2. Note: LangSmith tracing is activated automatically when `LANGSMITH_TRACING=true` and `LANGSMITH_API_KEY` are set in the environment. The graph already uses LangChain's built-in tracing (no code changes needed). See `p5-langsmith-verify` for verification steps.

3. Add a fast smoke test that does NOT call the real API ŌĆö it mocks `graph.invoke`:

   ```python
   # tests/test_harness_smoke.py
   from pathlib import Path
   from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
   import json, tempfile

   def test_harness_loads_data(tmp_path):
       # Write a minimal real_cards.json
       cards = [
           {
               "image_url": "http://example.com/a.jpg",
               "title": "Gain 5",
               "description": "Gain 5 points.",
               "human_canonical": {
                   "timing": "immediate", "target": "self",
                   "placement": "self", "trigger_event": None,
                   "ops": [{"op": "add_points", "args": {"amount": 5, "target": "self"}}],
                   "magnitude_sign": "positive",
               },
           }
       ]
       data_file = tmp_path / "real_cards.json"
       data_file.write_text(json.dumps(cards))

       from evals.harness import load_eval_items
       items = load_eval_items(data_file)
       assert len(items) == 1
       assert items[0].expected["timing"] == "immediate"
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `python evals/harness.py --help` runs without error
- [ ] `python evals/harness.py --limit 3` (with `OPENAI_API_KEY` set and `data/eval/real_cards.json` present) prints a score table with 4 scorer columns
- [ ] The table includes rows for: `intent_match`, `dsl_validity`, `target_accuracy`, `timing_accuracy`
- [ ] Pipeline metrics line shows `% valid EffectProgram` and `% snippet path`
- [ ] Conclusions section prints at the end
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_harness_smoke.py -v` passes (no API call)

## References
- `run_eval` shape: `evals/eval_core.py` (produced by `p5-eval-core-adapt`)
- `ALL_SCORERS`: `evals/scorers.py` (produced by `p5-scorers`)
- Compiled graph import: `from agent.graph import graph` (`p2-graph-assemble`)
- LangSmith tracing: env vars `LANGSMITH_TRACING` + `LANGSMITH_API_KEY` (wired in `p0-config`)

title: evals/judge.py: multi-dimensional LLM judge with Verdict structured output
description:
# evals/judge.py: multi-dimensional LLM-as-judge with structured output

## Context
The eval harness needs to score the agent's interpretation of a real card along multiple independent axes ŌĆö not just a single pass/fail. This module defines the `Verdict` Pydantic schema and a `JudgeLLM` class that calls `ChatOpenAI.with_structured_output(Verdict)` to score each interpretation. It is used by scorer callables in `p5-scorers` and invoked for every card in the eval dataset. The judge evaluates: intent match, timing correctness (immediate vs. persistent), target/placement correctness, trigger event correctness, and magnitude sign correctness.

## Files
- `evals/judge.py` (create) ŌĆö Verdict schema + JudgeLLM class

## Implementation steps

1. Create `evals/judge.py`:

   ```python
   """Multi-dimensional LLM-as-judge for card interpretation evals.

   Given (card description, agent's generated effect summary, human_canonical expected),
   produce a structured Verdict scoring each dimension independently.

   Usage:
       from evals.judge import JudgeLLM, Verdict
       judge = JudgeLLM()
       verdict = judge.evaluate(
           card_description="Gain 5 points immediately.",
           generated_summary="add_points(5, target=self)",
           human_canonical={"timing": "immediate", "target": "self", ...},
       )
       print(verdict.overall)
   """
   from __future__ import annotations

   from functools import lru_cache
   from typing import Annotated

   from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
   from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


   class Verdict(BaseModel):
       """Multi-dimensional judge verdict for one card interpretation."""

       intent_match: Annotated[
           float,
           Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Does the generated effect capture the card's intent?"),
       ]
       timing_correct: Annotated[
           float,
           Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Is the timing (immediate/persistent/triggered) correct?"),
       ]
       target_placement_correct: Annotated[
           float,
           Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Is the target (self/player/all) and placement correct?"),
       ]
       trigger_event_correct: Annotated[
           float,
           Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0,
                 description="Is the trigger event correct? 1.0 if N/A (card has no trigger). 0.0 if trigger is wrong or missing."),
       ]
       magnitude_sign_correct: Annotated[
           float,
           Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0,
                 description="Is the magnitude sign (positive/negative/neutral) correct?"),
       ]
       overall: Annotated[
           float,
           Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0,
                 description="Overall faithfulness of the interpretation (0=completely wrong, 1=perfect)."),
       ]
       reason: str = Field(description="1ŌĆō2 sentences explaining the overall score.")


   JUDGE_SYSTEM = """\
   You are a strict evaluator for a card-game interpretation system called "1000 Blank White Cards".

   Given:
   - CARD DESCRIPTION: the original card text as written by a player.
   - GENERATED EFFECT SUMMARY: the interpretation produced by an AI agent (JSON or text summary).
   - EXPECTED CANONICAL: the correct interpretation produced by a human annotator (JSON).

   Score each dimension independently from 0.0 to 1.0:
   - intent_match: Does the generated effect do what the card says? Ignore phrasing ŌĆö focus on semantics.
   - timing_correct: Is the timing tag correct? (immediate = one-shot when played; persistent = stays in play; triggered = fires on an event).
   - target_placement_correct: Is the target correct? (self=card player, player=chosen player, all=everyone).
   - trigger_event_correct: Is the trigger event correct? If the card has NO trigger, this should be 1.0 (N/A).
   - magnitude_sign_correct: Is the sign of the effect correct? (positive=gaining, negative=losing, neutral=no change).
   - overall: Your holistic judgment of how faithfully the card was interpreted.

   Be strict. A card that says "all players" but the agent says "self" scores 0 for target_placement_correct.
   A persistent card interpreted as immediate scores 0 for timing_correct.
   """


   @lru_cache(maxsize=1)
   def _get_judge_llm() -> ChatOpenAI:
       return ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-5.4-mini", temperature=0).with_structured_output(Verdict)


   class JudgeLLM:
       """Multi-dimensional LLM judge.

       Stateless; safe to create once and reuse across all eval items.
       """

       def __init__(self, model: str = "gpt-5.4-mini") -> None:
           self._llm: ChatOpenAI = ChatOpenAI(model=model, temperature=0).with_structured_output(Verdict)

       def evaluate(
           self,
           *,
           card_description: str,
           generated_summary: str,
           human_canonical: dict,
       ) -> Verdict:
           """Call the LLM judge and return a structured Verdict.

           Raises ValueError if the LLM response cannot be parsed into Verdict.
           """
           import json

           user_msg = (
               f"CARD DESCRIPTION:\n{card_description}\n\n"
               f"GENERATED EFFECT SUMMARY:\n{generated_summary}\n\n"
               f"EXPECTED CANONICAL:\n{json.dumps(human_canonical, indent=2)}"
           )
           from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage
           response = self._llm.invoke(
               [SystemMessage(content=JUDGE_SYSTEM), HumanMessage(content=user_msg)]
           )
           if not isinstance(response, Verdict):
               raise ValueError(f"Judge returned unexpected type: {type(response)}")
           return response
   ```

2. The `Verdict` model is intentionally separate from `agent/schemas.py`'s `Verdict` ŌĆö this one is for eval only and lives in `evals/`. Do NOT modify `agent/schemas.py`.

3. Add a smoke test in `tests/test_judge.py`:
   ```python
   import pytest
   from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
   from evals.judge import JudgeLLM, Verdict

   def test_verdict_schema_valid():
       v = Verdict(
           intent_match=0.9,
           timing_correct=1.0,
           target_placement_correct=1.0,
           trigger_event_correct=1.0,
           magnitude_sign_correct=1.0,
           overall=0.95,
           reason="Correct immediate point gain.",
       )
       assert v.overall == 0.95

   def test_verdict_score_range():
       import pytest
       with pytest.raises(Exception):
           Verdict(
               intent_match=1.5,  # out of range
               timing_correct=1.0,
               target_placement_correct=1.0,
               trigger_event_correct=1.0,
               magnitude_sign_correct=1.0,
               overall=1.0,
               reason="Bad.",
           )
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `from evals.judge import JudgeLLM, Verdict` imports without error
- [ ] `Verdict` has all 7 fields: `intent_match`, `timing_correct`, `target_placement_correct`, `trigger_event_correct`, `magnitude_sign_correct`, `overall`, `reason`
- [ ] All float fields validate range 0..1 (Pydantic raises on out-of-range values)
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_judge.py -v` passes (no API call required ŌĆö schema tests only)
- [ ] `JudgeLLM().evaluate(...)` calls `with_structured_output(Verdict)` (confirmed by code inspection)

## References
- Verdict shape inspired by `agent/schemas.py` Verdict in `p2-agent-schemas`
- `with_structured_output` pattern: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase2/18-node-judge.md`
- Score/Scorer shapes: `/Users/msharp/projects/aiec1/07_Advanced_Retrievers/lib/eval_core.py`

title: evals/eval_core.py: copy/adapt aiec1 Score/Scorer/run_eval (no pandas)
data/eval/real_cards.json: 30-50 annotated real cards with human_canonical
description:
# data/eval/real_cards.json: curated real-card testset with human_canonical annotations

## Context
The eval harness requires a ground-truth testset of **real** 1000 Blank White Cards ŌĆö not synthetic gold cards ŌĆö to measure how well the agent interprets actual handwritten cards from real games. This issue produces the annotated `data/eval/real_cards.json` file (30ŌĆō50 cards) that the harness loads. If the vision transcription script from `p5-imgur-transcribe-script` was run and produced a partial file, this issue completes the human annotation pass. If the Imgur API was unavailable, this issue creates the file from scratch using manually observed cards from the album.

This file is the REQUIRED REAL-DATA testset for the evals rubric deliverable. It must exist before `p5-harness` can run.

## Files
- `data/eval/real_cards.json` (create or extend) ŌĆö 30ŌĆō50 annotated real cards

## Implementation steps

1. If `data/eval/real_cards.json` already exists from `p5-imgur-transcribe-script`, open it and fill in every `human_canonical: null` entry following `data/eval/ANNOTATION_GUIDE.md`.

2. If the file does not exist (full fallback path), create it manually with cards drawn from the album screenshots (`https://imgur.com/a/blank-white-cards-5-2012-5-2013-rWS9A`) or canonical TBWC card lists. The JSON array must contain 30ŌĆō50 entries. Below is a seed set of 10 representative examples ŌĆö expand to 30ŌĆō50 by adding more:

   ```json

         "timing": "immediate",

         "placement": "self",

         "magnitude_sign": "positive"
       }
     },
     {
       "image_url": "https://i.imgur.com/fallback_002.jpg",
       "title": "Everyone Loses 5 Points",

         "placement": "self",
         "trigger_event": null,
         "ops": [{"op": "add_points", "args": {"amount": -5, "target": "all"}}],
         "magnitude_sign": "negative"
       }
     },
     {
       "image_url": "https://i.imgur.com/fallback_003.jpg",
       "title": "Skip Your Turn",

       "human_canonical": {

         "target": "self",
         "placement": "self",

         "ops": [{"op": "skip_turn", "args": {"target": "self"}}],

       }
     },
     {
   ```
