   ```python
   from enum import StrEnum
   from typing import Any, Callable


   class GameEvent(StrEnum):
       """All event names the engine can emit. Hooks subscribe by name."""
       ON_PLAY           = "on_play"
       ON_SCORE_CHANGE   = "on_score_change"
       ON_TURN_START     = "on_turn_start"
       ON_TURN_END       = "on_turn_end"
       ON_DRAW_STEP      = "on_draw_step"
       ON_DESTROY_ATTEMPT = "on_destroy_attempt"
       ON_WIN_CHECK      = "on_win_check"


   @dataclass
   class HookContext:
       """Carries all per-event data into a hook handler.
       Fields that don't apply to a given event are left None."""
       event: GameEvent
       actor_id: str                        # player who triggered the event
       card_id: str | None = None           # card being played / destroyed
       chosen_player_id: str | None = None  # resolution of Target.chooser
       amount: int | None = None            # points delta, draw count, etc.
       target_player_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
       extra: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

       def with_amount(self, amount: int) -> "HookContext":
           """Convenience: return a shallow copy with amount updated."""
           from dataclasses import replace
           return replace(self, amount=amount)


   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   # EventBus: thin in-process dispatcher used by the engine
   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   # In phase 1 the bus is synchronous and stateless (no persistent listener
   # registration here ŌĆö that lives in engine/hooks.py via HookRegistry).
   # The bus's job is just to be the call-site interface so the engine doesn't
   # call hook machinery directly.

   class EventBus:
       """Lightweight synchronous event bus.
       The engine calls bus.emit(event, state, ctx); the bus delegates to
       fire_hooks from engine.hooks.  Import cycle avoided by late import."""

       def emit(
           self,
           event: GameEvent,
           state: Any,   # GameState ŌĆö typed Any to avoid circular import
           ctx: HookContext,
       ) -> Any:         # returns (potentially modified) GameState
           from engine.hooks import fire_hooks  # late import ŌĆö avoids cycle
           return fire_hooks(state, event, ctx)
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `from engine.events import GameEvent, HookContext, EventBus` works.
- [ ] `GameEvent.ON_PLAY == "on_play"` (StrEnum).
- [ ] `HookContext(event=GameEvent.ON_PLAY, actor_id="p1")` constructs.
- [ ] `ctx.with_amount(10).amount == 10` and original `ctx.amount` is still `None`.
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_events_bus.py` passes.

## References
- `src/engine/hooks.py` from `p1-hooks-registry-ordering` (will be late-imported)

title: models/game_state.py: GameState, Player, WinCondition, mutable loop config
description:
# models/game_state.py: GameState, Player, WinCondition (Pydantic v2)

## Context
`GameState` is the single immutable-snapshot data structure that the entire engine reads and writes. All reducers take a `GameState` and return a new `GameState`. This model defines players, the deck, the card registry, mutable loop config (direction, draw_count, skip_predicate), win condition, persistent hooks, house rules, phase, and log.

## Files
- `src/models/game_state.py` (create) ŌĆö GameState, Player, WinCondition models

## Implementation steps
1. Create `src/models/game_state.py`:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   from typing import Any, Literal
   from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
   import uuid


   class WinCondition(BaseModel):
       kind: Literal[
           "highest_points", "lowest_points", "first_to", "last_standing", "none"
       ] = "highest_points"
       threshold: int | None = None  # used by "first_to"


   class Player(BaseModel):
       id: str
       name: str
       score: int = 0
       hand: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)  # card ids
       connected: bool = True


   class GameState(BaseModel):
       room_code: str
       players: list[Player] = Field(default_factory=list)

       # Card registry grows during play as new cards are invented
       deck: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)       # card ids (ordered)
       discard: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)    # card ids
       cards: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict) # card_id -> Card

       turn_index: int = 0  # index into players list

       # Mutable loop configuration ŌĆö cards can rewrite these
       direction: Literal[1, -1] = 1   # 1 = clockwise, -1 = counter-clockwise
       draw_count: int = 1             # cards drawn at start of each turn
       # skip_predicate: None or a serializable rule-ref string.
       # The engine evaluates this string against a hook registry in phase 4+;
       # in phase 1 it is either None or a plain callable name string for tests.
       skip_predicate: str | None = None

       win_condition: WinCondition = Field(default_factory=WinCondition)

       # Hook registry: list of RegisteredHook ids (actual objects in HookRegistry)
       persistent_effects: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

       # House rules: ids of CENTER-scoped cards currently in effect
       house_rules: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

       phase: Literal["lobby", "setup", "playing", "epilogue", "ended"] = "lobby"

       log: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

       # Tracks whose turn it is to skip (engine-internal, reset each turn)
       _skip_next: set[str] = Field(default_factory=set, exclude=True)
       _extra_turn: set[str] = Field(default_factory=set, exclude=True)

       model_config = {"arbitrary_types_allowed": True}

       def active_player(self) -> Player:
           """Return the player whose turn it currently is."""
           return self.players[self.turn_index % len(self.players)]

       def get_player(self, player_id: str) -> Player:
           for p in self.players:
               if p.id == player_id:
                   return p
           raise KeyError(f"Player {player_id!r} not found")

       def with_log(self, msg: str) -> "GameState":
           """Return a copy of this state with msg appended to log."""
           return self.model_copy(update={"log": self.log + [msg]})
   ```
2. Note: `cards` is typed `dict[str, Any]` to avoid a forward-reference cycle. After `p1-models-cards` is merged you can tighten this to `dict[str, Card]` using `from __future__ import annotations` + a `TYPE_CHECKING` guard.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `from models.game_state import GameState, Player, WinCondition` works.
- [ ] `GameState(room_code="AAAA")` constructs with sensible defaults.
- [ ] `state.active_player()` returns the right player given `turn_index`.
- [ ] `state.with_log("hello")` does not mutate the original.
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_game_state.py` passes.

## References
- `src/models/cards.py` from `p1-models-cards`
- `src/models/effects.py` from `p1-effects-ops`

title: models/effects.py: Op discriminated union, Target, EffectProgram
description:
# models/effects.py: Immediate Op union, Target, EffectProgram (Pydantic v2)

## Context
Every card can carry a list of "immediate ops" ŌĆö discrete, one-shot mutations applied to game state when the card is played. This module defines a Pydantic v2 discriminated union of all op types (add_points, skip_turn, etc.), a `Target` enum covering all ways to address players, and `EffectProgram` which bundles ops + a flag indicating whether a human choice is required.

## Files
- `src/models/effects.py` (create) ŌĆö Op union, Target, EffectProgram

## Implementation steps
1. Create `src/models/effects.py`:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal, Union
   from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   # Target addresses
   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Target = Literal[
       "self",
       "left_neighbor",
       "right_neighbor",
       "all",
       "all_others",
       "chooser",           # actor chooses at play-time (requires_choice=True)
       "target_player",     # pre-resolved by engine from ctx.chosen_player_id
       "player_with_most_points",
       "player_with_least_points",
       "player_with_empty_hand",
   ]

   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   # Individual op models ŌĆö discriminated by the `op` literal field
   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   class AddPointsOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["add_points"] = "add_points"
       target: Target = "self"
       amount: int

   class SubtractPointsOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["subtract_points"] = "subtract_points"
       target: Target = "self"
       amount: int

   class SetPointsOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["set_points"] = "set_points"
       target: Target = "self"
       amount: int

   class SkipTurnOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["skip_turn"] = "skip_turn"
       target: Target = "self"

   class ExtraTurnOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["extra_turn"] = "extra_turn"
       target: Target = "self"

   class ReverseOrderOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["reverse_order"] = "reverse_order"

   class ChangeDrawCountOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["change_draw_count"] = "change_draw_count"
       amount: int  # new draw_count value (absolute, not delta)

   class StealPointsOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["steal_points"] = "steal_points"
       from_target: Target
       to_target: Target = "self"
       amount: int

   class DrawCardsOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["draw_cards"] = "draw_cards"
       target: Target = "self"
       amount: int = 1

   class DestroyCardOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["destroy_card"] = "destroy_card"
       card_id: str  # id of card to remove from target's hand

   class SetWinConditionOp(BaseModel):
       op: Literal["set_win_condition"] = "set_win_condition"
       kind: Literal[
           "highest_points", "lowest_points", "first_to", "last_standing", "none"
       ]
       threshold: int | None = None

   class CustomNoteOp(BaseModel):
       """A no-op that logs a flavour message; useful for cards that only register hooks."""
       op: Literal["custom_note"] = "custom_note"
       note: str

   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   # Discriminated union ŌĆö Pydantic v2 uses Annotated + Field(discriminator=...)
   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Op = Annotated[
       Union[
           AddPointsOp,
           SubtractPointsOp,
           SetPointsOp,
           SkipTurnOp,
           ExtraTurnOp,
           ReverseOrderOp,
           ChangeDrawCountOp,
           StealPointsOp,
           DrawCardsOp,
           DestroyCardOp,
           SetWinConditionOp,
           CustomNoteOp,
       ],
       Field(discriminator="op"),
   ]

   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   # EffectProgram: the full payload attached to a card play
   # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   class EffectProgram(BaseModel):
       ops: list[Op] = Field(default_factory=list)
       requires_choice: bool = False  # True when any op targets "chooser"
   ```
2. Update `src/models/cards.py` to replace the `Any` annotation on `immediate_ops`:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
   if TYPE_CHECKING:
       from models.effects import Op
   # ...
   immediate_ops: list["Op"] = Field(default_factory=list)
   ```
   Use a string forward reference so the import remains lazy at runtime (no circular import).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `from models.effects import EffectProgram, AddPointsOp, Op` works.
- [ ] Pydantic correctly discriminates: `EffectProgram.model_validate({"ops": [{"op": "add_points", "amount": 5}]})` produces an `AddPointsOp`.
- [ ] Invalid `op` literal raises `ValidationError`.
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_effects_ops.py` passes.

## References
- Pydantic v2 discriminated unions: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/unions/#discriminated-unions
- `src/models/cards.py` from `p1-models-cards`

title: models/cards.py: Card model with open-ended properties (Pydantic v2)
description:
# models/cards.py: Card model (Pydantic v2)

## Context
The `Card` model is the central data object in 1000 Blank White Cards. Each card has an id, title, description, creator, and an open-ended `properties` dict that agents set at runtime (e.g. `indestructible`, `uncounterable`, `playable_out_of_turn`). Cards also carry their effect payload ŌĆö a list of immediate ops and a list of registered hook ids ŌĆö which other engine modules consume.

## Files
- `src/models/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö empty package marker
- `src/models/cards.py` (create) ŌĆö Card Pydantic model

## Implementation steps
1. Create `src/models/__init__.py` as an empty file.
2. Create `src/models/cards.py`:
   ```python
   from __future__ import annotations
   from typing import Any
   from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


   class Card(BaseModel):
       """A single card in the game. `properties` is open-ended: agents set flags
       like 'indestructible', 'uncounterable', 'playable_out_of_turn' here."""

       id: str
       title: str
       description: str
       creator_id: str  # player id who created/wrote this card

       # Open-ended flags set by the LLM agent or hand-authored tests.
       # Keys are arbitrary strings; values are Any (bool, int, strŌĆ”).
       properties: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)

       # Immediate ops are stored by reference once the EffectProgram model exists.
       # For now use Any; phase p1-effects-ops replaces this with the real type.
       immediate_ops: list[Any] = Field(default_factory=list)

       # Hook ids (str uuids) pointing into the global HookRegistry.
       hook_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
   ```
3. No circular imports: `cards.py` must NOT import from `effects.py` or any engine module at module load time. The `immediate_ops` field is typed `Any` here; after `p1-effects-ops` is merged, change its type annotation to `list[Op]` (import inside TYPE_CHECKING block to avoid cycles).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `from models.cards import Card` works from project root with `PYTHONPATH=src`.
- [ ] `Card(id="c1", title="Test", description="", creator_id="p1")` constructs without error.
- [ ] `card.properties` defaults to `{}`.
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_models_cards.py` passes (write a minimal test alongside this issue).

## References
- `src/main.py` ŌĆö existing entry point showing project pythonpath convention
- `pyproject.toml` ŌĆö `pythonpath = ["src"]`

title: tbwc/app.py: FastAPI skeleton with CORS, lifespan stub, GET /health
description:
# p0-fastapi-app: app.py FastAPI skeleton with CORS, lifespan, and GET /health

## Context
The game server is a FastAPI application. This issue wires up the application object ŌĆö CORS middleware sourced from `Settings`, a lifespan context manager (stub that later phases populate with DB/agent startup), and a single `GET /health` route that confirms the server is alive. REST game routes and WebSocket mounts are added by later phases.

## Files
- `src/tbwc/app.py` (modify ŌĆö replace stub) ŌĆö FastAPI application
- `tests/test_app.py` (create) ŌĆö health-route smoke test

## Implementation steps

1. Replace the stub `src/tbwc/app.py` with:
   ```python
   """tbwc.app ŌĆö FastAPI application factory.

   Exposes `app` (the ASGI application) and `create_app()` for testing.
   REST game routes and /ws are mounted by later phases.
   """

   from __future__ import annotations

   from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
   from typing import AsyncGenerator

   from fastapi import FastAPI
   from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

   from tbwc.config import get_settings


   @asynccontextmanager
   async def lifespan(application: FastAPI) -> AsyncGenerator[None, None]:
       """Application lifespan: startup ŌåÆ yield ŌåÆ shutdown.

       Later phases will:
         - initialise the Qdrant collection
         - warm up the LangGraph agent
         - open the room registry
       """
       # startup
       yield
       # shutdown


   def create_app() -> FastAPI:
       """Create and configure the FastAPI application."""
       settings = get_settings()

       application = FastAPI(
           title="1000 Blank White Cards",
           description="AI-assisted party card game server.",
           version="0.1.0",
           lifespan=lifespan,
       )

       application.add_middleware(
           CORSMiddleware,
           allow_origins=settings.cors_origins,
           allow_credentials=True,
           allow_methods=["*"],
           allow_headers=["*"],
       )

       @application.get("/health", tags=["meta"])
       async def health() -> dict[str, str]:
           """Liveness probe ŌĆö returns {"status": "ok"}."""
           return {"status": "ok"}

       return application


   # Module-level app instance used by uvicorn and tests.
   app = create_app()
   ```

2. Create `tests/test_app.py`:
   ```python
   """Smoke tests for the FastAPI app skeleton."""

   from __future__ import annotations

   import pytest
   from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

   from tbwc.app import create_app
   from tbwc.config import get_settings


   @pytest.fixture()
   def client() -> TestClient:
       return TestClient(create_app())


   def test_health_returns_ok(client: TestClient) -> None:
       response = client.get("/health")
       assert response.status_code == 200
       assert response.json() == {"status": "ok"}


   def test_cors_headers_present(client: TestClient) -> None:
       """CORS preflight should include allowed origins."""
       settings = get_settings()
       origin = settings.cors_origins[0]
       response = client.options(
           "/health",
           headers={
               "Origin": origin,
               "Access-Control-Request-Method": "GET",
           },
       )
       # FastAPI returns 200 for preflight
       assert response.status_code == 200
       assert response.headers.get("access-control-allow-origin") in (origin, "*")
   ```

3. Confirm the app can be started manually (do NOT actually start it during CI ŌĆö just import-check):
   ```bash
   uv run python -c "from tbwc.app import app; print(app.title)"
   ```
   Expected output: `1000 Blank White Cards`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_app.py -v` ŌĆö both tests pass
- [ ] `uv run python -c "from tbwc.app import app; print(app.title)"` prints `1000 Blank White Cards`
- [ ] `GET /health` returns `{"status": "ok"}` with status 200 (verified by test or `curl http://localhost:8000/health` if server running)
- [ ] `uv run ruff check src/tbwc/app.py tests/test_app.py` exits 0
- [ ] Full test suite still passes: `uv run pytest`

## References
- FastAPI lifespan pattern: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/events/
- Settings import: `src/tbwc/config.py` (p0-config must be done first)

title: tbwc/config.py: pydantic-settings Settings class and .env.example
description:
# p0-config: config.py pydantic-settings Settings class and .env.example

## Context
All secrets and tuneable parameters (API keys, model names, CORS origins, Qdrant connection details) must be centralised in one place so every part of the app can import a single `Settings` object. This issue creates `src/tbwc/config.py` using `pydantic-settings` with environment-variable loading, plus a `.env.example` for local development.

## Files
- `src/tbwc/config.py` (modify ŌĆö replace stub) ŌĆö Settings class with all required fields
- `.env.example` (create) ŌĆö documented template for local dev secrets

## Implementation steps

1. Replace the stub in `src/tbwc/config.py` with the full settings class:
   ```python
   """tbwc.config ŌĆö Application settings loaded from environment / .env file."""

   from __future__ import annotations

   from functools import lru_cache

   from pydantic import AnyHttpUrl
   from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


   class Settings(BaseSettings):
       model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
           env_file=".env",
           env_file_encoding="utf-8",
           case_sensitive=False,
           extra="ignore",
       )

       # --- OpenAI ---
       openai_api_key: str = ""
       openai_chat_model: str = "gpt-5.4-mini"
       openai_embedding_model: str = "text-embedding-3-small"

       # --- LangSmith ---
       langchain_api_key: str = ""
       langchain_project: str = "tbwc"
       langchain_tracing_v2: bool = False

       # --- Tavily ---
       tavily_api_key: str = ""

       # --- Qdrant ---
       qdrant_url: str = "http://localhost:6333"
       qdrant_api_key: str = ""
       qdrant_collection: str = "tbwc_cards"

       # --- Server / CORS ---
       cors_origins: list[str] = ["http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:5173"]
       host: str = "0.0.0.0"
       port: int = 8000


   @lru_cache(maxsize=1)
   def get_settings() -> Settings:
       """Return a cached Settings singleton. Call get_settings.cache_clear() in tests."""
       return Settings()
   ```

2. Create `.env.example` at the repo root:
   ```
   # Copy this file to .env and fill in your values.
   # .env is git-ignored ŌĆö never commit real secrets.

   # OpenAI
   OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
   OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL=gpt-5.4-mini
   OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL=text-embedding-3-small

   # LangSmith (optional ŌĆö set LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true to enable)
   LANGCHAIN_API_KEY=
   LANGCHAIN_PROJECT=tbwc
   LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=false

   # Tavily (used by agent web-search tool)
   TAVILY_API_KEY=tvly-...

   # Qdrant (local Docker default ŌĆö change for cloud)
   QDRANT_URL=http://localhost:6333
   QDRANT_API_KEY=
   QDRANT_COLLECTION=tbwc_cards

   # Server
   CORS_ORIGINS=["http://localhost:3000","http://localhost:5173"]
   HOST=0.0.0.0
   PORT=8000
   ```

3. Ensure `.env` is in `.gitignore` (add if missing):
   ```
   .env
   ```

4. Write a test `tests/test_config.py`:
   ```python
   """Tests for tbwc.config.Settings."""

   from __future__ import annotations

   import pytest

   from tbwc.config import Settings, get_settings


   def test_defaults_load_without_env_file() -> None:
       """Settings should load with default values when no .env is present."""
       s = Settings(_env_file=None)  # type: ignore[call-arg]
       assert s.openai_chat_model == "gpt-5.4-mini"
       assert s.openai_embedding_model == "text-embedding-3-small"
       assert s.qdrant_collection == "tbwc_cards"
       assert s.port == 8000
       assert "http://localhost:3000" in s.cors_origins


   def test_get_settings_returns_singleton() -> None:
       get_settings.cache_clear()
       s1 = get_settings()
       s2 = get_settings()
       assert s1 is s2
       get_settings.cache_clear()


   def test_env_override(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
       get_settings.cache_clear()
       monkeypatch.setenv("OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL", "gpt-4o")
       s = Settings(_env_file=None)  # type: ignore[call-arg]
       assert s.openai_chat_model == "gpt-4o"
       get_settings.cache_clear()
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_config.py -v` ŌĆö all 3 tests pass
- [ ] `uv run python -c "from tbwc.config import get_settings; print(get_settings().openai_chat_model)"` prints `gpt-5.4-mini`
- [ ] `uv run ruff check src/tbwc/config.py tests/test_config.py` exits 0
- [ ] `.env.example` exists at repo root and documents all required keys
- [ ] `.env` is listed in `.gitignore`

## References
- pydantic-settings docs pattern: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/pydantic_settings/
- Reference config style: `/Users/msharp/projects/aiec1/09_Agent_Servers/app/`

title: pyproject.toml: add all runtime dependencies via uv add
description:
# p0-deps: Add runtime dependencies to pyproject.toml via uv add

## Context
The project's `pyproject.toml` currently has zero runtime dependencies ŌĆö only dev deps (pytest, ruff). This issue adds every runtime library the game server needs before any feature code is written, so later issues can import freely without hitting missing-module errors.

## Files
- `pyproject.toml` (modify) ŌĆö add runtime deps and pandas as a dev dep

## Implementation steps

1. Run `uv add` for runtime deps (run each command from the repo root; `uv` will resolve, pin, and write `uv.lock` automatically):
   ```bash
   uv add fastapi "uvicorn[standard]" websockets pydantic-settings \
       langgraph langchain langchain-openai langchain-qdrant qdrant-client \
       langchain-tavily langsmith tiktoken
   ```

2. Add `pandas` as a dev-only dependency:
   ```bash
   uv add --dev pandas
   ```

3. After running the commands, verify `pyproject.toml` `[project]` `dependencies` list includes all of the following (exact versions will vary ŌĆö just confirm the names are present):

   ```
   fastapi
   uvicorn[standard]
   websockets
   pydantic-settings
   langgraph
   langchain
   langchain-openai
   langchain-qdrant
   qdrant-client
   langchain-tavily
   langsmith
   tiktoken
   ```
   And `[dependency-groups] dev` includes `pandas`.

4. Confirm the lock file updated:

## Acceptance criteria

title: tbwc package: restructure src/main.py into src/tbwc/ subpackage layout
description:
# p0-package-skeleton: Restructure src/main.py into src/tbwc/ package

## Context
The project currently has a bare `src/main.py` placeholder. This issue establishes the `src/tbwc/` package layout ŌĆö the foundational directory structure every other phase depends on. It moves (and updates) the placeholder main entry point into the package and creates empty `__init__.py` stubs for all sub-packages so Python can import them.

## Files
- `src/tbwc/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö package root, re-exports `main()`
- `src/tbwc/app.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub (filled by p0-fastapi-app)
- `src/tbwc/config.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub (filled by p0-config)
- `src/tbwc/models/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub
- `src/tbwc/engine/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub
- `src/tbwc/agent/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub
- `src/tbwc/rag/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub
- `src/tbwc/sandbox/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub
- `src/tbwc/rooms/__init__.py` (create) ŌĆö empty stub
- `src/main.py` (delete) ŌĆö replaced by tbwc package
- `tests/test_main.py` (modify) ŌĆö update import to use `tbwc` package

## Implementation steps

1. Create `src/tbwc/__init__.py` with a minimal `main()` function so the existing test can be updated to point here:
   ```python
   """tbwc ŌĆö 1000 Blank White Cards game server package."""

   from __future__ import annotations


   def main() -> None:
       print("Hello from a-thousand-blank-white-cards!")


   __all__ = ["main"]
   ```

2. Create the sub-package stubs. Each file is identical ŌĆö a one-line docstring:

   `src/tbwc/models/__init__.py`:
   ```python
   """tbwc.models ŌĆö Pydantic data models (cards, game state, players)."""
   ```

   `src/tbwc/engine/__init__.py`:
   ```python
   """tbwc.engine ŌĆö Core game loop and rules engine."""
   ```

   `src/tbwc/agent/__init__.py`:
   ```python
   """tbwc.agent ŌĆö LangGraph AI agent for card generation and judging."""
   ```

   `src/tbwc/rag/__init__.py`:
   ```python
   """tbwc.rag ŌĆö Retrieval-Augmented Generation helpers (Qdrant)."""
   ```

   `src/tbwc/sandbox/__init__.py`:
   ```python
   """tbwc.sandbox ŌĆö Safe execution sandbox for LLM-generated card ops."""
   ```

   `src/tbwc/rooms/__init__.py`:
   ```python
   """tbwc.rooms ŌĆö WebSocket room management and session state."""
   ```

3. Create empty stubs for the modules that later issues will fill:

   `src/tbwc/config.py` (stub ŌĆö p0-config will replace this body):
   ```python
   """tbwc.config ŌĆö Application settings (filled by p0-config issue)."""
   ```

   `src/tbwc/app.py` (stub ŌĆö p0-fastapi-app will replace this body):
   ```python
   """tbwc.app ŌĆö FastAPI application (filled by p0-fastapi-app issue)."""
   ```

4. Delete `src/main.py`:
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/msharp/projects/a-thousand-blank-white-cards/src/main.py
   ```

5. Update `tests/test_main.py` to import from the new package:
   ```python
   from tbwc import main


   def test_main(capsys: pytest.CaptureFixture[str]) -> None:
       main()
       captured = capsys.readouterr()
       assert captured.out == "Hello from a-thousand-blank-white-cards!\n"
   ```
   Also add `import pytest` at the top.

6. Verify nothing else imports `src/main.py` by checking `pyproject.toml` ŌĆö it currently has no `[project.scripts]` entry, so no changes needed there.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `uv run pytest` passes (Ōēź80% coverage, 1 test green)
- [ ] `uv run ruff check src/ tests/` exits 0
- [ ] `python -c "import tbwc; tbwc.main()"` prints the greeting (run from repo root with `uv run python -c ...`)
- [ ] `python -c "import tbwc.models, tbwc.engine, tbwc.agent, tbwc.rag, tbwc.sandbox, tbwc.rooms"` exits 0
- [ ] `src/main.py` no longer exists

Tests: a scripted full game with a persistent hook, a center house-rule changing draw_count/direction, and a newest-wins conflict where an uncounterable card resists override, reaching phase=ended with correct scores. Everything else depends on this.

title: Phase 0 ŌĆö Scaffold: restructure repo, add deps, seed corpus
description: Restructure src/main.py into the src/tbwc/ package layout; add runtime deps (fastapi, uvicorn[standard], websockets, pydantic-settings, langgraph, langchain, langchain-openai, langchain-qdrant, qdrant-client, langchain-tavily, langsmith, tiktoken; pandas dev-only). Add config.py (pydantic-settings for OPENAI/TAVILY/LANGSMITH keys, model names, CORS). Add a FastAPI health route. Author data/seed_cards.json (~20 fully-annotated gold exemplars {title, description, canonical:{timing, target, placement, trigger, ops|snippet}} + ~40 text-only fillers). Foundation for all later phases.

title: sandbox_behavior: normalise_ops must strip default/falsy optional fields before hashing
description: From 2jp.5 investigation. normalise_ops (src/evals/fixtures.py:91-108) does bare json.dumps(sort_keys) per op. Generated plain-ops go through Pydantic model_dump() which always serializes optional defaults (EndGameOp.winners:[], DestroyCardOp.card_id:None; models/effects.py). Canonical sandbox helpers (src/engine/sandbox/api_surface.py:225-258) OMIT falsy optionals. So semantically identical ops hash to different strings and score 0. Confirmed: seed-gold-010 (end_game winners:[] vs omitted), seed-gold-009 (destroy_card card_id:null vs omitted). FIX: strip default/None/empty optional fields in normalise_ops before serialising, or re-serialise plain ops through the same omission convention.

title: sandbox_behavior: execute interaction steps / populate ctx['interactions'] in eval fixtures
description: From 2jp.5 investigation (DOMINANT cause of sandbox_behavior=0.42, ~27% of rows). _generated_effect_forms (src/evals/scorers.py:235-248) silently drops kind==interaction steps, and fixture ctx always has interactions={} (src/evals/fixtures.py:69-87). Agent-generated plans commonly model an effect as interaction step(s) + a trailing snippet that reads ctx['interactions'][...]; with no resolved interactions the snippet no-ops (empty diff vs canonical -> jaccard 0) or KeyErrors -> SnippetExecutionError -> 0. FIX: add resolved ctx['interactions'] entries to fixture_states() simulating a plausible choice/vote/text answer, OR give the scorer a canned resolver that executes interaction steps before the trailing snippet. Highest leverage - should fix ~50% of zero rows. Verify by re-scoring the existing run JSON, not a fresh eval.

title: Investigate low sandbox_behavior score (0.42) on seed benchmark
description:
WHY: Seed eval run data/eval/runs/20260714-173730_seed_us-anthropic-claude-opus-4-8.json shows sandbox_behavior=0.4227 while executability=0.9565 and dsl_validity=0.9710. That gap is suspicious: generated plans run fine and are valid DSL, yet their sandbox behavior rarely matches the canonical. Explicitly deferred from the corpus-growth work - this is an INVESTIGATION task; produce findings and follow-up issues, not necessarily a fix.

HOW THE SCORER WORKS (starting points): sandbox_behavior (src/evals/scorers.py:251) executes BOTH the expected canonical sandbox code and the generated snippet via engine.sandbox.runner.execute_snippet against canned fixture states from src/evals/fixtures.py (which already carry chosen_player_id=p2, chosen_card_id=c7 at lines 74-75), then compares emitted-op diffs by multiset Jaccard. N/A cases score 1.0. Child sandbox runner: src/engine/sandbox/runner.py and _child_runner.py.

HYPOTHESES TO TEST (rank by likelihood as you go):
1. Scorer artifact: multiset Jaccard over op diffs is too strict - e.g. equivalent behavior expressed as different op sequences (add_points twice vs once with double amount) scores near 0.
2. Representation mismatch: agent emits plain ops while canonical uses a sandbox snippet (or vice versa), and the comparison penalizes the form difference rather than behavior.
3. Stale canonical sandbox code: some canonicals sandbox fields may predate schema migrations and emit differently-shaped ops than their own ops field would.
4. Fixture sensitivity: behavior only diverges on the specific canned fixture states (e.g. hand contents, scores) rather than in general.
5. Genuine agent mismatch: the agent really does produce behaviorally different code.

METHOD: pull the per-row sandbox_behavior scores and metadata out of the run JSON (rows key, one per card), bucket the failures, and for 5-10 representative failures manually run both snippets through execute_snippet on the fixture states and eyeball the op diffs. Check whether the 0.42 is bimodal (mostly 0s and 1s) or spread.

DELIVERABLE: written findings (bd comment or notes field on this issue) naming the dominant cause with evidence, plus new issues for any concrete fixes discovered. Do NOT re-run the full seed eval - user does that manually; reuse the existing run JSON.
acceptance_criteria: Findings documented on this issue: per-row failure buckets with counts, dominant root cause identified with at least 5 concrete card-level examples, and follow-up issues filed for any fixes. No eval re-run performed.
notes:
FINDINGS (evidence-based, from run 20260714-173730). Distribution is strongly BIMODAL over 69 rows: 21 perfect (30%), 6 N/A skip (9%), 31 hard-zero disjoint (45%), 6 execution-crash (9%), 5 partial (7%). Only 7% are close misses, so Jaccard brittleness is secondary.

COMPARISON LOGIC: _sandbox_behavior_scorer (src/evals/scorers.py:251-287) executes canonical sandbox + generated code/ops over 2 canned fixtures (src/evals/fixtures.py:56-88), normalises via normalise_ops (fixtures.py:91-108), scores multiset_jaccard (fixtures.py:111-120). _generated_effect_forms (scorers.py:235-248) reads only kind==snippet/ops and SILENTLY DROPS kind==interaction steps. Fixture ctx always sets interactions={} (fixtures.py:69-87). normalise_ops aliases only 4 target strings (_CHOICE_TARGETS, fixtures.py:21) and does bare json.dumps per op with no default-stripping.

ROOT CAUSES (ranked): (1) DOMINANT ~27% of rows: interaction/context fixture gap - agent models effects as interaction steps + trailing snippet reading ctx['interactions']; scorer drops interaction steps and ctx['interactions'] is always {} so the snippet no-ops or KeyErrors (confirmed live: seed-filler-000/001/009/010/013/014/017/020/025/026/028/033/035/037 no-op; seed-gold-002/003/014, seed-filler-022 crash). (2) op-schema default mismatch: model_dump() emits winners:[]/card_id:null that canonical sandbox helpers (api_surface.py:225-258) omit, so identical ops hash differently (seed-gold-009/010). (3) incomplete _CHOICE_TARGETS allowlist + canonical ops-vs-sandbox vocab mismatch (seed-gold-005 next_player vs right_neighbor; seed-filler-024). (4) canonical under-mechanization/staleness (seed-gold-017 ignores its own still_in_hand condition; seed-filler-038/039 flavor-only). (5) genuine agent bugs: magnitude errors invisible to sign-only judges (seed-gold-007/009), self-vs-neighbor target confusion (seed-gold-005, seed-filler-023), register_hook-wrapping-register_hook double indirection (seed-gold-012/016), wrong DSL primitive (seed-filler-003), outright miss (seed-filler-032). Follow-up beads filed: see linked issues.
close_reason: Investigation complete. Findings recorded in notes; dominant cause is the interaction/ctx fixture gap (not broken cards). 5 follow-up beads filed for scorer/canonical/agent fixes.

title: Prune stale dev rooms in FileRoomStore on load
description:
FileRoomStore (dev-only, DEV_MODE) persisted a JSON file per room to .devstate/rooms and never reaped them. load_all() rehydrated every file on startup, so a 'fresh' server resurrected thousands of accumulated rooms (~1851 observed) and the list-rooms API returned them all.

Fix: load_all() now deletes any room whose created_at is older than _ROOM_MAX_AGE (1 day) instead of loading it. Timestamp-less pre-created_at files still load and backfill (existing contract preserved). Prod uses InMemoryRoomStore and is unaffected.

Files: src/board/rooms/store.py, tests/test_room_store.py (added test_stale_room_is_pruned_on_load, test_fresh_room_survives_load).
close_reason: Implemented pruning in load_all(); full suite green (1380 passed), ruff clean.

title: discard-from-hand now works: fix silent authoring-args drop + add per-player from_hand card_pick
description: Root cause: the LLM interpreter emits ops in authoring {op,args} shape into program.ops, which the flat runtime Op union accepted while silently dropping args -> destroy_card(card_target=None) -> _reduce_destroy_card resolved 0 cards and returned state unchanged with NO log ('the card did nothing'). Fixes: (1) EffectProgram.ops/OpsStep.ops flatten the args wrapper before validation; (2) _reduce_destroy_card logs a [destroy_card no-op] entry when it resolves nothing; (3) new CardPickInteraction.from_hand mode presents each audience member their own hand (per-player scoped send+validate), enabling 'everyone discards a card they choose' via a card_pick+snippet; (4) persona/read_engine_methods now document destroy_card as the discard verb and the from_hand pattern. Tests added across test_effects_ops, test_reducers, test_interactions, test_room_interactions.
close_reason: Implemented and tested in this session
