title: feat(epilogue): cumulative cross-game keep/destroy tallies
description:
DECIDED POLICY (user, 2026-07-12): keep/destroy votes accumulate across games. Example: game 1 kept 6-0, game 2 voted destroy 2-3 ŌåÆ cumulative 8-3 in favor ŌåÆ the card STAYS. Ties ŌåÆ keep. Only genuinely hated cards (cumulative net-negative) get destroyed. Per-game majorities no longer decide alone.

THE PROBLEM: votes currently live only in the in-memory EpilogueManager (src/board/rooms/epilogue.py:22-26) and die with the game; tally_votes (src/engine/epilogue.py:30-69) decides from this game's votes only; tally_and_persist (epilogue.py:49-79) upserts kept cards to RAG with no vote history. Epilogue state is also absent from the room store (_room_to_dict, src/board/rooms/store.py:139-146), so an in-progress epilogue is lost on reload.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/agent/rag/store.py ŌĆö carry cumulative keep_votes/destroy_votes in the card payload (survives upserts).
- src/board/rooms/epilogue.py tally_and_persist (:49-79) ŌĆö load prior totals for cards already in the corpus, add this game's votes, decide keep/destroy from the CUMULATIVE totals (ties ŌåÆ keep), persist updated totals; destroyed = removed/retired from the corpus so it stops re-entering decks.
- src/engine/epilogue.py tally_votes (:30-69) ŌĆö accept prior totals or return raw counts for the caller to aggregate; keep pure.
- src/board/rooms/store.py (_room_to_dict :139-146) ŌĆö include epilogue votes so reloads don't lose an in-progress vote.

DEPENDS ON: the vote-pool/skip bead (defines which cards are voted and when finalize happens).

NOTE: cumulative-keep interacts with the vote pool ŌĆö previously-kept cards keep appearing for voting in later games by design; their accumulated score is exactly what protects them.
acceptance_criteria: Per-card vote totals persist with the card in the corpus and accumulate across games (6-0 keep in game 1 + 2-3 destroy in game 2 = 8-3 ŌåÆ keep); ties keep; a card is destroyed/retired from the corpus only when cumulatively net-negative; in-progress epilogue votes survive a room-store reload; unit tests for the aggregation math + a two-game integration test; full suite green.
close_reason: Merged; agent died at spend limit mid-test-writing, work salvaged/finished in main loop. Cumulative totals in RAG payload, retirement, epilogue persistence all landed with tests.

title: feat(epilogue): vote pool = authored cards only; voting skippable
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest): "When the Epilogue started it shows all the cards (seed cards, blank cards)ŌĆ” Players shouldn't be forced to vote on all the cardsŌĆ” definitely not blank cardsŌĆ” also not cards that were never played." Verified: start_epilogue passes list(self.state.cards.values()) with NO filtering (src/board/rooms/room.py:904-914); completion requires EVERY expected player to vote on EVERY card (_all_votes_in, src/board/rooms/epilogue.py:44-47) ŌĆö a stall risk if anyone walks away; the client independently renders ALL cards from the snapshot (Object.values(gameState.cards), frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:149-152) and gates on allVoted (frontend/components/epilogue.tsx:25-26). Note the client ignores the EpilogueMsg card list entirely (no 'epilogue' case in frontend/lib/ws.ts) ŌĆö dead message.

DECIDED POLICY (user, 2026-07-12): vote pool = ALL AUTHORED cards ŌĆö authored this game or in previous games (RAG-kept re-entries) ŌĆö regardless of whether they were played this game. Never shipped seed cards, never blanks. Voting must be skippable.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/board/rooms/room.py start_epilogue (:904-914) ŌĆö filter to authored cards (creator/source markers distinguish authored vs source:"seed" vs blank; verify the card model's provenance fields and add one if missing).
- src/board/rooms/epilogue.py ŌĆö replace _all_votes_in (:44-47) full-coverage gate with per-player DONE (unvoted = abstain, which tally_votes in src/engine/epilogue.py:30-69 already supports) + host finalize (and/or finalize when all players done). New ws message for done/finalize in src/models/ws_messages.py.
- frontend/components/epilogue.tsx ŌĆö render the server-provided card list (wire the epilogue message in lib/ws.ts or a filtered state field); add Done/Skip; drop the allVoted gate (:25-26).
- frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:149-152 ŌĆö stop deriving epilogueCards from all state.cards.
acceptance_criteria: Epilogue presents only authored cards (authored this game or kept from previous games) ŌĆö never blanks, never shipped seed cards; client renders the server-provided list, not Object.values(gameState.cards); a player can finish with any number of votes cast (Done button; unvoted = abstain) and the room finalizes without full coverage (host-finalize and/or all-done), so epilogue cannot stall; room + frontend tests; full suite green.

title: feat(board): make end-of-game trigger scoring visible
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest): "I had cards in hand that stated I'd earn points at end of gameŌĆ” it looks like they were added, but there wasn't a visual clue." Root cause: resolve_end_of_game (src/engine/scoring.py:37-69, called from _end_game at src/board/rooms/room.py:483) applies each on_game_end card via apply_effect DIRECTLY ŌĆö bypassing Room._log_and_broadcast (room.py:951-960). No log line, no effect_applied message; clients only see the cumulative scores in the next full snapshot, so end-game scoring is invisible.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/engine/scoring.py resolve_end_of_game (:37-69) ŌĆö return per-application details (holder, card title, score deltas) instead of applying silently, OR accept a callback; keep the function pure/engine-layer (engine must not import board).
- src/board/rooms/room.py _end_game (:466-498) ŌĆö emit one log line per application via _log_and_broadcast BEFORE the "Game over! Winner(s): ŌĆ”" line (:487-491); reuse the delta-formatting of _describe_play (:443-459).
- Optional frontend polish (may split out): a score-delta cue in PlayerTile (frontend/components/game-table.tsx:84) ŌĆö today the number silently swaps.

Pairs with the results-screen bead: those lines land in state.log, so the history view makes end-game triggers discussable.
acceptance_criteria: Each end-of-game trigger application produces a per-card log line + effect_applied broadcast (e.g. "Game end: Alice's 'Worth 10 If Kept' ŌåÆ Alice +10") before the winner announcement; the lines appear in the snapshot log clients render; room-level test asserts the lines; full suite green.

title: feat(game): results ŌåÆ epilogue ŌåÆ results end-flow with history and vote outcomes
description:
THE PROBLEM (playtest): epilogue starts with no scores visible; the win screen shows only who won; history is never shown, so players can't discuss what happened or see end-of-game triggers; vote results are never shown at all. Desired flow: Results screen (scores + history) ŌåÆ Epilogue vote ŌåÆ Results screen (scores + history + vote outcomes).

WHAT EXISTS (verified 2026-07-12): the data is nearly all there ŌĆö gameState.log rides every snapshot (src/models/game_state.py:96; Room.snapshot room.py:928-949) and useGameSocket already accumulates log (frontend/lib/ws.ts:150,156); it is simply not rendered outside the playing phase. EndScreen (frontend/app/room/[code]/page.tsx:439-493) renders GameTable but no history; the epilogue block (page.tsx:337-349) renders neither scores nor history. Vote outcomes are never sent to clients ŌĆö only a count log line (room.py:925); no epilogue-result message exists (ServerMsg union src/models/ws_messages.py:172-181).

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
Backend:
- Phase flow: today playingŌåÆepilogueŌåÆended (room.py:466-498, :904-925). Either add a distinct results phase before epilogue, or model it as an epilogue sub-state; pick the minimal shape that lets the client show results-first (host/anyone advances to voting).
- Vote outcomes: extend state or add an epilogue_result message carrying kept/destroyed card lists from tally_and_persist (src/board/rooms/epilogue.py:49-79) so the final results screen can render them (src/models/ws_messages.py).
Frontend:
- Results screen component: GameTable (scores) + a read-only EffectLog (history) ŌĆö both exist; render them for the results step and keep them on the final screen (page.tsx:337-357, :439-493).
- Final results additionally lists kept/destroyed cards.

COORDINATE with the epilogue vote-pool/skip bead (same files, same phase machinery) ŌĆö implement together or sequence explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: At game end players see a results screen with final scores AND the full play history before voting; after the epilogue the results screen returns, additionally showing kept/destroyed vote outcomes; history is also visible on the final screen; transitions covered by a room-level phase test + frontend renders each step; full suite green.

title: feat(agent): dynamic phase E ŌĆö agent + RAG round-trip for dynamic behavior
description:
THE PROBLEM: even with snippets executing and hooks firing, the loop doesn't close: (1) the agent has no way to SAY "this card is a persistent hook on ON_TURN_START" ŌĆö SnippetEffect (src/agent/contract.py:21-32) has no trigger/scope; (2) kept cards lose their dynamic behavior ŌĆö the epilogue RAG upsert (src/board/rooms/epilogue.py:63-77) stores card["program"], so snippet/hook cards re-enter future games inert and need the LLM again; (3) compile_card knows nothing of the new authoring ops.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/agent/contract.py ŌĆö SnippetEffect gains trigger: GameEvent|None and scope; Room._resolve_program converts a triggered snippet into RegisterHookOp so hook registration flows through the ONE op pipeline (no second channel).
- src/board/rooms/room.py _handle_create_card (~:840-874) + src/board/rooms/epilogue.py tally_and_persist (:49-79) ŌĆö persist dynamic ops (including register_hook with its validated code) into canonical.ops before upsert.
- src/engine/compile.py ŌĆö compile register_hook/set_rule/set_condition/set_card_attribute/create_card authoring ops; RE-RUN validate_snippet at compile/deck-build time ŌĆö kept snippet cards replay deterministically next game; if validation has tightened since, degrade to CustomNoteOp instead of crashing deck build. Cap stored snippet size at upsert.
- src/agent/persona.py + src/agent/tools/read_engine_methods.py ŌĆö document target-prefix grammar, the rule/condition/attribute/hook ops, and WHEN to emit a persistent hook vs an immediate program.
- docs/architecture.md section 5 ŌĆö rewrite to describe the now-live sandbox/hook path.

DEPENDS ON: phases C and D.

UNLOCKS: a great card invented mid-game ŌĆö hook and all ŌĆö is voted keep, enters the corpus, and works identically and deterministically when drawn in a future game.
acceptance_criteria: SnippetEffect carries trigger/scope and Room turns persistent-hook interpretations into RegisterHookOp (single pipeline); kept cards persist their dynamic ops/snippets into canonical.ops before RAG upsert; compile_card compiles register_hook/set_rule/set_condition/set_card_attribute/create_card authoring ops and re-runs validate_snippet at deck build, degrading to CustomNoteOp on failure; a hook card kept in game 1 replays deterministically (no LLM) in game 2 (test); persona + read_engine_methods document the full surface incl. hook-vs-immediate guidance; docs/architecture.md section 5 rewritten for the live sandbox; full suite green.

title: feat(sandbox): dynamic phase D ŌĆö full-width SandboxGame facade + ON_VALIDATE_PLAY veto hooks
description:
THE PROBLEM: the sandbox facade covers 6 of 12 ops and almost no reads ŌĆö SandboxGame (src/engine/sandbox/api_surface.py:88-116) offers only add_points/subtract_points/set_points/skip/set_draw_count/note as mutators, and reads omit hand contents/counts, deck, rules, conditions, turn order, card attributes. The Chess card ("score 1 per card in hand") cannot even COUNT a hand from a snippet. And nothing can veto a play, so rule-enforcement cards (color match) are impossible.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/engine/sandbox/api_surface.py ŌĆö reads: hand(player_id) (full card list for the actor, count for others), deck_size, rules(), conditions(player_id), turn_order(), card(card_id) metadata incl. attributes. Mutators recording every phase A-C op: draw_cards, destroy_card, set_win_condition, end_game, set_rule, set_condition, set_card_attribute, create_card, shuffle_into_deck, register_hook(event, scope, code), reject_play(reason).
- src/engine/sandbox/revalidate.py ŌĆö pass diff ORIGIN into parse_diff; reject register_hook ops in hook-produced diffs (no self-replicating hooks); handle reject_play as a pseudo-op surfaced to the caller as a veto result, not a reducer.
- src/engine/events.py ŌĆö new ON_VALIDATE_PLAY.
- src/board/rooms/room.py _handle_play ŌĆö fire ON_VALIDATE_PLAY before resolving/applying; a veto messages the player with the reason, returns the card, does not consume the turn; apply rules.cannot_play (forced draw) when no legal play exists.
- src/engine/hooks.py ŌĆö validation ctx gains the played card_id + its attributes.

INVARIANT: no facade mutator bypasses the Op pipeline ŌĆö everything still round-trips through revalidate.py; that file is the security/consistency choke point.

DEPENDS ON: phase C (hooks-as-data).

UNLOCKS: wildest Uno (color-match enforcement on play), "no point cards while poisoned", full Chess-style hand-reading combos.
acceptance_criteria: SandboxGame exposes reads (actor hand contents, others' hand counts, deck_size, rules, conditions, turn_order, card attributes) and mutators at full op parity incl. end_game/set_rule/set_condition/set_card_attribute/create_card/shuffle_into_deck/register_hook/reject_play; every mutator round-trips through revalidate.py; register_hook inside a hook-produced diff is rejected (depth cap); ON_VALIDATE_PLAY veto returns the card, does not consume the turn, applies rules.cannot_play; wildest-Uno test: a validation hook enforces color-match on play; Chess-shape test: snippet reads hand and scores per card; full suite green.

title: feat(engine): dynamic phase C ŌĆö hooks-as-data, per-room EventBus, Room fires events
description:
THE PROBLEM: the hook/event system exists (src/engine/hooks.py, events.py) but the Room never registers hooks or fires events ŌĆö the EventBus emits ON_SCORE_CHANGE into an empty registry (src/engine/apply.py). Worse, the registry and snippet cache are process-GLOBAL module singletons (_default_registry hooks.py:116, _SNIPPET_CACHE hooks.py:58, module-level EventBus in apply.py:14 / loop.py:26): naive revival would leak one room's house rules into every other room. RegisteredHook also carries no code, so hooks are unrecoverable from persisted state.

DESIGN POSITION: hooks become DATA on GameState ŌĆö persistence, reconnects, cross-room isolation, and cache invalidation all fall out of one move.

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/game_state.py (or new models/hooks.py) ŌĆö HookSpec(id, source_card_id, event, scope, owner_id, code); GameState.hooks: list[HookSpec].
- src/models/effects.py ŌĆö RegisterHookOp(event, scope, code) + UnregisterHookOp(source_card_id); reducers append/remove HookSpec AFTER validate_snippet passes.
- src/engine/hooks.py ŌĆö replace the global registry/_SNIPPET_CACHE with build_registry(state) -> HookRegistry, cached per Room and invalidated when state.hooks ids change; handlers via existing make_snippet_handler.
- src/board/rooms/room.py ŌĆö Room owns an EventBus over its registry; pass bus into apply_effect (~:784) so ON_SCORE_CHANGE hooks fire; emit ON_PLAY (_handle_play post-apply), ON_TURN_START (_start_turn), ON_TURN_END (_advance_turn :408), ON_DRAW_STEP (_handle_draw :368), ON_GAME_END (_end_game :466).
- src/board/rooms/store.py ŌĆö hooks ride model_dump for free; update the docstring; verify rehydration rebuilds the registry lazily.
- Budgets: cap hooks fired per event (~8) and total snippet wall-clock per action; over-budget hooks log [hook error] and no-op.

DEPENDS ON: the _child_runner fix and the snippet-execution wiring beads.

UNLOCKS: persistent house rules that actually fire and survive restarts ŌĆö "whenever anyone scores, Bob draws a card"; "at the start of each turn, lowest player gains 1".
acceptance_criteria: HookSpec (id, source_card_id, event, scope, owner_id, code) serialized on GameState.hooks; RegisterHookOp/UnregisterHookOp reduce onto state after validate_snippet; hook registry + snippet cache are per-Room caches derived from state (module-global singletons gone); Room emits ON_PLAY/ON_TURN_START/ON_TURN_END/ON_DRAW_STEP/ON_GAME_END and score changes fire registered hooks; hooks survive store round-trip + reconnect; two concurrent rooms cannot see each other's hooks (test); hooks-per-event and per-action wall-clock capped, failures log [hook error] without blocking the turn; full suite green.

title: feat(engine): dynamic phase B ŌĆö open targets, conditions, card attributes, card creation
description:
THE PROBLEM: Target and CardTarget are frozen Literal vocabularies (src/models/effects.py:12-23, :50-55) ŌĆö a card cannot address "the player with the poisoned condition", "that specific player", "all red cards", and nothing can CREATE cards mid-game. Cards can do anything; the type system says otherwise.

DESIGN POSITION: keep pydantic validation, open the vocabulary. Target/CardTarget become validated open strings: existing literal set Ōł¬ id:<player_id> Ōł¬ has:<condition_key> (resolved against the bead-3vc per-player conditions map) Ōł¬ attr:k=v for cards (e.g. attr:color=red). Explicitly NO generic set-state-path op ŌĆö it can violate invariants no reducer can repair (card in two zones, turn_index out of range). The open-but-bounded surfaces are SetConditionOp (per-player k/v), SetCardAttributeOp (per-card k/v), SetRuleOp (phase A), plus hooks (phase C).

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/effects.py ŌĆö Target/CardTarget from Literal to Annotated[str, AfterValidator] with known-prefix grammar; new SetConditionOp(target, key, value), SetCardAttributeOp(card_target, key, value), CreateCardOp(title, description, ops, attributes, destination: deck_shuffle|deck_top|hand) capped <=10 per play.
- src/engine/reducers.py ŌĆö _resolve_targets/_resolve_card_targets (:36-93) handle the prefixed forms; three new reducers; CreateCardOp registers cards in state.cards with creator_id = source card author and STRUCTURED ops so created cards compile deterministically when later drawn/played.
- src/engine/compile.py + src/agent/persona.py ŌĆö compile paths + op list; add a target-prefix grammar note to src/agent/tools/read_engine_methods.py (op union introspection is automatic, the string grammar is not).

DEPENDS ON: beads 3vc (conditions map) and as9 (turn_order list ŌĆö next/insert targeting) and phase A.

UNLOCKS: spicy Uno with NO snippet (colors + created Draw 2/Draw 4/Reverse); "poison the player to your left"; "destroy all red cards".
acceptance_criteria: Target/CardTarget accept the literal set plus id:<player_id>, has:<condition_key>, attr:k=v (validated, garbage rejected); SetConditionOp/SetCardAttributeOp/CreateCardOp (capped <=10/play, created cards carry compilable ops) reduce correctly; resolvers handle prefixes; spicy-Uno room-level test: color attributes assigned + Draw2/Draw4/Reverse created and shuffled into the deck, later drawn and compiled deterministically; persona/read_engine_methods document the grammar; full suite green.

title: feat(engine): dynamic phase A ŌĆö rules-as-data + data-driven end condition
description:
AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN ŌĆö docs/state-example.jsonc "rules" section: {draw, play, cannotPlay, endCondition:{type}, winCondition:{type}} as mutable serialized data.

THE PROBLEM: rule-shaped state is scattered and frozen ŌĆö draw_count/win_condition/skip_predicate live top-level on GameState; the end condition isn't data at all (a Room-private _deck_exhausted latch, src/board/rooms/room.py:389-391/:424-425); there is no play-count rule. A card cannot say "win = empty hand, end = empty hand, draw = 0" (Uno v1).

EXACT CODE SURFACE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/game_state.py ŌĆö Rules model: draw:int=1, play:int=1, cannot_play:dict, end_condition:{type: deck_empty|empty_hand|points_reached|now, threshold}, win_condition (move existing WinCondition here; add empty_hand kind), extra:dict[str,Any] (open bag for agent-invented rules that only hooks read). GameState.rules: Rules; migrate top-level fields.
- src/models/effects.py ŌĆö SetRuleOp(op="set_rule", path, value); widen SetWinConditionOp/WinCondition kinds with empty_hand.
- src/engine/reducers.py ŌĆö _reduce_set_rule with validated path allowlist (draw, play, cannot_play.*, end_condition.*, win_condition.*, extra.*); _reduce_change_draw_count becomes a rules.draw write; EndGameOp reducer becomes end_condition={type:"now"}; register in _REDUCERS (:227-245).
- src/engine/scoring.py ŌĆö evaluate_win_condition reads state.rules.win_condition, add empty_hand; NEW pure evaluate_end_condition(state) -> bool.
- src/board/rooms/room.py ŌĆö _handle_draw (:368) reads rules.draw; replace the _deck_exhausted latch with evaluate_end_condition checked at draw time (preserving drawer-finishes-turn for deck_empty) AND at the two check sites established by the EndGameOp bead; enforce rules.play per turn.
- src/engine/compile.py + src/agent/persona.py (~:60) + src/engine/sandbox/api_surface.py (draw_count property :81-82) ŌĆö wire set_rule / rules reads.

SEQUENCING: depends on the EndGameOp bead (refactors its signal into end_condition). Land AFTER 3vc/as9 to avoid rebasing GameState repeatedly.

UNLOCKS: Uno v1 as pure ops: [set_rule(win_condition.kind, empty_hand), set_rule(end_condition, {type: empty_hand}), set_rule(draw, 0)].
acceptance_criteria: GameState has a serialized Rules model per state-example.jsonc (draw, play, cannot_play, end_condition, win_condition, open extra dict); top-level draw_count/win_condition/skip_predicate migrated off; SetRuleOp with path allowlist works; end_game = end_condition {type:now}; empty_hand win/end kinds evaluate; _handle_draw reads rules.draw (0 works); rules.play enforced; Uno v1 expressible as pure ops in a room-level test; full suite green.

title: epic(engine): dynamic engine ŌĆö no frozen vocabularies; the agent extends the game
description:
AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN ŌĆö docs/state-example.jsonc: rules are mutable DATA ({draw, play, cannotPlay, endCondition:{type}, winCondition:{type}}), players carry open-ended conditions maps, turnOrder is an explicit mutable list. Sibling beads 3vc/as9/iy4 already move the state model there.

THE PROBLEM (playtest notes, 2026-07-12): the engine reducer + sandbox + facade is close to the intent but too rigid. Ops, Effects, and Targets are frozen Literal vocabularies; the agent can look at the facade but cannot ADD to it. Target capability ladder (the Uno test): (1) a card sets win condition to empty-hand, end trigger to empty-hand, draw step to 0 ŌĆö pure rule data; (2) the card creates Draw 2 / Draw 4 / Reverse cards and shuffles them into the deck; (3) the card assigns colors to all cards and enforces color-match on play_card(). Design so the agent can change the game, not the source: the mechanism is the EXISTING (currently orphaned) snippet sandbox + hook system, made per-room and state-derived, plus rules/conditions/attributes as open data.

KEY STRUCTURAL FACTS (verified 2026-07-12):
- Sandbox pipeline (src/engine/sandbox/: validate ŌåÆ subprocess runner ŌåÆ SandboxGame facade ŌåÆ revalidate through the Op union) is fully built but has ZERO board call sites.
- Hook registry, snippet cache, and EventBus are process-GLOBAL singletons (src/engine/hooks.py:58,116; src/engine/apply.py:14; src/engine/loop.py:26) ŌĆö naive revival leaks house rules across rooms; hooks must become serialized state.
- The facade mutators cover only 6 of 12 ops (src/engine/sandbox/api_surface.py:88-116); reads omit hands/deck/rules/conditions.
- read_engine_methods introspects the Op union at call time, so every new op is automatically visible to the agent.

INVARIANT for every child bead: no facade mutator or dynamic path may bypass revalidate.py ŌĆö everything round-trips through the Op pipeline and the same pure reducers.

PHASES (children): A rules-as-data ŌåÆ B dynamic targets/conditions/attributes/card-creation ŌåÆ C hooks-as-data + per-room events ŌåÆ D full-width facade + play-veto hooks ŌåÆ E agent/RAG round-trip. Plus a P3 spike on true source self-modification (what sandboxing can never give: new interaction protocols).

RISKS to carry on each child: sandbox escape surface (subprocess+rlimits is the real boundary; RLIMIT_AS unenforced on macOS); snippet determinism (pure fn of state+ctx ŌĆö keep it that way); persistence across reconnect/restart (solved structurally by hooks/rules-as-data riding model_dump); op-cap pressure in revalidate.py (_MAX_OPS=50 ŌĆö cap create_card fan-out and hooks-per-event).
acceptance_criteria: All five phase children (rules-as-data, dynamic targets/conditions/card-creation, hooks-as-data, full-width facade, agent+RAG round-trip) closed; the Uno exemplar family works end-to-end at each tier (rule ops ŌåÆ created colored cards ŌåÆ play-validation hook); docs/architecture.md rewritten for the live sandbox path.
close_reason: All six children closed: phases A-E (rules-as-data, open targets/conditions/attributes/create-card, hooks-as-data + per-room events, full-width facade + play-veto, agent/RAG round-trip) plus the self-modification spike (docs/self-modification-spike.md: descriptors, not source edits). The Uno ladder works at every tier: rule ops ŌåÆ created colored cards ŌåÆ color-match validation hook. 972 tests green.

title: refactor(model): spectators as a separate simple list, not Player entries
description:
AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN ŌĆö docs/state-example.jsonc: `spectators: ["Person1","Person2"]` is a SEPARATE, flat list of watcher identities, distinct from `players`. Players and spectators are different kinds of thing.

THE REGRESSION (code diverged from the design): spectators are stored INSIDE players[] as `Player(spectator=True)` and then filtered out everywhere. Player is a COMPLEX, DYNAMIC entity (id, name, score, hand, in_play, connected, conditions, turn position); a spectator is SIMPLE and DETERMINISTIC (an identity that watches). Modeling the simple thing as the complex one forces an `if spectator` guard into nearly every player code path and invites bugs (a spectator accidentally dealt to, scored, given a turn, or counted for auto-start). A separate list removes the guards structurally.

EXACT CODE SURFACE TO CHANGE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/game_state.py:38 ŌĆö remove `spectator: bool` from Player. Add a separate `spectators` collection to GameState (e.g. `spectators: list[Spectator]` or `list[str]` ids/names ŌĆö pick the minimal shape the frontend needs).
- src/models/game_state.py:102-112 `turn_players()` and :114-120 `active_player()` ŌĆö the non-spectator filtering exists only to exclude spectators; simplify (players[] is now all real players).
- src/engine/scoring.py:80 (`p.connected and not p.spectator`) and :40,76 comments ŌĆö drop the spectator filter.
- src/engine/loop.py:107 ŌĆö `while players[next_idx].spectator` skip loop goes away (players has no spectators). (Coordinate with bead as9 which also rewrites advance_turn.)
- src/board/rooms/room.py ŌĆö add_player:99-109 (spectator param + Player(spectator=...)), _is_spectator:115-116, the message gate:146, authored-count:212, deal:270 (`not p.spectator`), pass guard:549-551. Reroute join-as-spectator to the new spectators list; keep the JOIN POLICY (late joiner after lobby becomes spectator) at manager.py:88.
- src/board/rooms/manager.py:73-98 ŌĆö returns (code, player_id, spectator); keep the tuple but populate from the new model.
- src/board/app.py:100,201-202,221 ŌĆö JoinRoomResponse.spectator + the /join wiring; keep the API field, source it from the new list.
- src/agent/tools/read_game_state.py:157-158 ŌĆö spectator tag; read from the new list.

TESTS asserting the regression (update): tests/test_spectator.py (asserts player.spectator / room.state.get_player(id).spectator), tests/test_resolve_end_of_game.py:71-74 (Player(...,spectator=True)), tests/test_rest_routes.py:84 (data['players'] ... p['spectator']), tests/test_rooms.py:51, conftest.py:29-30 helpers. The public JOIN behavior (late joiner = spectator; spectators can't play/author/score/take turns) MUST be preserved ŌĆö only the representation changes.
acceptance_criteria: Spectators live in a separate GameState collection, not in players[]; Player.spectator and every 'not p.spectator' / _is_spectator guard (game_state turn_players/active_player, scoring, loop skip, room deal/pass/authored-count/message-gate) are removed; join-as-spectator policy (late joiner becomes spectator; can't play/author/score/take a turn) is preserved via the new list; JoinRoomResponse.spectator + read_game_state tag still work; tests updated to the new shape; full suite green.

title: feat(model): open-ended, serialized per-player conditions map
description:
AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN ŌĆö docs/state-example.jsonc: each player has a `conditions` field that is either `null` or an OPEN-ENDED map, e.g. `"conditions": {"skipNextTurn": 1}`. It is a general, extensible bag of per-player statuses.

THE REGRESSION (code diverged from the design): there is no per-player conditions field. Instead GameState hardcodes exactly TWO statuses as NON-SERIALIZED private sets: `_skip_next` and `_extra_turn` (src/models/game_state.py:99-100, PrivateAttr). This is wrong for two reasons:
1. It can represent ONLY skip/extra-turn. The whole premise of 1000 Blank White Cards is emergent, player-invented effects ŌĆö a card might make a player "poisoned", "confused", or "pertwiddled" (a status that did not exist until someone wrote that card this game). An open-ended map handles any of these; two hardcoded sets handle none of them.
2. Being PrivateAttr, these statuses are NOT in model_dump(), so they never reach the WS snapshot the clients + agent see. Conditions must be visible so the UI can show "poisoned" and the agent can reason about it.

EXACT CODE SURFACE TO CHANGE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/game_state.py:38 (Player) ŌĆö add `conditions: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)` (serialized, per-player).
- src/models/game_state.py:99-100 ŌĆö delete the `_skip_next` / `_extra_turn` PrivateAttr sets.
- src/models/game_state.py:205-221 ŌĆö `copy_with_turn_flags` rebinds those private sets; replace with condition-map updates (immutably).
- src/engine/loop.py:81-111 ŌĆö advance_turn reads `state._extra_turn` / `state._skip_next`; read the well-known keys from player.conditions instead (e.g. conditions["extra_turn"], conditions["skip_next"]), and CONSUME them by writing the updated map.
- src/engine/reducers.py:129-136 ŌĆö `_reduce_skip_turn` / `_reduce_extra_turn` write the private sets; write conditions keys instead. Keep SkipTurnOp/ExtraTurnOp (effects.py:149,154) as the well-known writers, but the reducer path must be generic enough that a future op can set an ARBITRARY condition key (design for a generic set-condition op / CustomNoteOp-style path).
- src/board/rooms/store.py:16 ŌĆö docstring references the PrivateAttrs; update.
- src/engine/loop.py:11-13 docstring references PrivateAttr bookkeeping; update.

TESTS asserting the regression (update): tests/test_reducers.py:92-98 (assert new._skip_next == {...}) and any test reading _skip_next/_extra_turn. Add a test that an arbitrary condition key (e.g. {"poisoned": 2}) round-trips through model_dump() and is visible in the snapshot.

DESIGN NOTE: keep skip_next / extra_turn as reserved keys with defined loop semantics; everything else is free-form status the UI/agent can surface even if the engine has no special handling yet.
acceptance_criteria: Player has a serialized, open-ended conditions dict; the _skip_next/_extra_turn PrivateAttr sets and copy_with_turn_flags's use of them are gone; advance_turn + skip/extra reducers operate on conditions keys; an arbitrary condition (e.g. poisoned) round-trips through model_dump() into the WS snapshot; the reducer path can set an arbitrary condition key, not just the two hardcoded ones; docstrings (store.py, loop.py) updated; full suite green.

title: feat(model): explicit mutable turn_order, replacing the direction flag
description:
AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN ŌĆö docs/state-example.jsonc: turn order is `turnOrder: ["Player1","Player2"]`, an explicit ORDERED, MUTABLE list of player identities. There is NO concept of clockwise/counter-clockwise "direction" in the design.

THE REGRESSION (code diverged from the design): GameState models turn position as `turn_index: int` plus `direction: Literal[1,-1]` (src/models/game_state.py:74,77). A single-bit direction can express ONLY a reverse. It CANNOT express the other legal made-up cards in 1000 Blank White Cards: scramble/randomize the seating, swap two players, insert someone next, move a player to the end, etc. Order must be a first-class mutable list; "reverse" is then just one operation on it.

EXACT CODE SURFACE TO CHANGE (grepped 2026-07-12):
- src/models/game_state.py:77 ŌĆö remove `direction: Literal[1,-1]`. Add an explicit ordered turn list (e.g. `turn_order: list[str]` of player ids) + a current-position pointer, or make turn_index index into turn_order. Keep it SERIALIZED (it already broadcasts).
- src/engine/loop.py:85,91,99,108 ŌĆö advance_turn currently does `(turn_index + direction) % n`. Rewrite to step through turn_order (still honoring skip-next / extra-turn / spectator-skip at loop.py:81-111 ŌĆö but note spectator-skip is being removed by bead iy4; coordinate).
- src/engine/reducers.py:46,48 ŌĆö `_resolve_targets` right/left neighbor uses `(actor_idx -/+ state.direction) % n`. Recompute neighbors from turn_order position.
- src/engine/reducers.py:140 ŌĆö `_reduce_reverse_order` does `{"direction": -state.direction}`. Change to reverse turn_order (and reposition the pointer so the current player stays current).
- src/models/effects.py:159 ReverseOrderOp stays; ADD a ScrambleOrderOp (randomize turn_order) to prove the list model is expressive, wire it in compile.py + reducers.py + the OP_REDUCERS table (reducers.py:232 area) + persona.py:61 op list.
- src/engine/sandbox/api_surface.py:81-82 ŌĆö `direction` property reads state["direction"]; replace with a turn_order accessor.
- src/agent/tools/read_game_state.py:131-133 ŌĆö drops the "Turn direction: clockwise/ŌĆ”" line; surface turn order instead.

TESTS asserting the regression (update, don't preserve): tests/test_reducers.py (make_state(direction=...), test_right/left_neighbor_*, test_flips_direction:144-150), tests/test_sandbox_api_surface.py:18, tests/test_sandbox_feature_flag.py:14. Add a test that a scramble reorders turn_order.

NOTE: docs/architecture.md and any WRITEUP state-model text may mention direction ŌĆö sweep after.
acceptance_criteria: GameState stores turn order as an explicit, ordered, serialized, mutable list; the Literal[1,-1] direction field and all reads of it (loop.py, reducers.py neighbor + reverse, sandbox api_surface, read_game_state tool) are gone; ReverseOrderOp reverses the list; a new ScrambleOrderOp randomizes it (proving expressiveness); neighbor targeting derives from list position; tests updated off the list; full suite green.

title: feat(board): DEV_MODE endpoint to jump to end-game/epilogue
description:
Add a dev-only REST endpoint that forces a `playing` game to end immediately, so end-game triggers (on_game_end cards), win-condition scoring, and the epilogue vote flow can be tested WITHOUT exhausting the deck.

CONTEXT/DESIGN: End-game runs through Room._end_game() (src/board/rooms/room.py:419): it calls resolve_end_of_game(state) (applies on_game_end card ops ŌĆö engine/scoring.py), evaluate_win_condition(state) (sets winner_ids), then opens the epilogue via start_epilogue() (room.py:447) OR jumps straight to phase="ended" if no real players (room.py:450). Normally triggered by the deck-exhausted latch checked in _advance_turn (room.py:377). The dev shortcut must call the SAME _end_game() so behavior matches real end-game exactly ŌĆö DO NOT reimplement scoring.

FILES & CHANGES:
1. src/board/rooms/room.py ŌĆö expose a thin public dev wrapper: `async def dev_force_end_game(self)` that acquires self._lock and calls `await self._end_game()`, mirroring how handle_action serializes under the lock. (_end_game is an internal coroutine that must run under the lock and broadcasts.)
2. src/board/app.py ŌĆö add POST /rooms/{code}/dev/end-game. FIRST line: return 404 if not get_settings().dev_mode. Look up room via room_manager.get(code); 404 if missing. Require phase == "playing" else return 409 with a clear message. Then `await room.dev_force_end_game()`. Return room.snapshot().
   - OPTIONAL/minimal: accept a small JSON body to pre-seed a scenario (e.g. {"give_points": {player_id: n}}) so specific win conditions can be exercised. Keep it optional; the core requirement is "reach end-game now."
3. The epilogue-vs-ended branch (phase="epilogue" when real non-spectator connected players exist, else "ended") is already handled inside _end_game/start_epilogue (room.py:447-450) ŌĆö the dev endpoint inherits it for free; no extra work.

DEPENDS ON the DEV_MODE flag bead (sot.1).

GOTCHA: never write `except (A, B):` ŌĆö ruff-format corrupts it. Use `except Exception:`.
acceptance_criteria: With DEV_MODE=true: drive a room to playing (reuse drive_to_playing), give at least one player an on_game_end card in hand, POST /rooms/{code}/dev/end-game, then GET /rooms/{code}/state shows phase=='epilogue' (players present) with winner_ids populated and any on_game_end ops applied; with no real players it shows 'ended'. Calling on a non-playing room returns 409; DEV_MODE false returns 404. Uses the real _end_game() path (a test asserts winners/scoring match a deck-exhaustion end-game). Test added for the endpoint / Room.dev_force_end_game helper.

title: feat(board): DEV_MODE endpoint to skip author-creation
description:
Add a dev-only REST endpoint that fast-forwards a room from lobby/setup straight to `playing`, auto-filling each non-spectator's required authored cards so the agent/play phase can be tested immediately. Reuses the existing auto-start path.

CONTEXT/DESIGN: The room already auto-starts setup -> playing once every non-spectator has authored CARDS_TO_AUTHOR cards (src/board/rooms/room.py _handle_create_card ~lines 775-789 -> _start_playing(None)). The dev shortcut just automates that server-side ŌĆö exactly what tests/conftest.py:drive_to_playing does using real StartMsg / CreateCardMsg actions. Do the same in a dev method; DO NOT hand-mutate state.

Relevant constants/paths: CARDS_TO_AUTHOR (= BLANKS_PER_PLAYER, room.py:52), STARTING_HAND_SIZE (room.py:49), _authored_count(player_id) (room.py:184), message models StartMsg / CreateCardMsg in models.ws_messages, room_manager singleton in board/rooms/manager.py, existing debug endpoint GET /rooms/{code}/state in app.py (~line 200) returning room.snapshot().

FILES & CHANGES:
1. src/board/app.py ŌĆö add POST /rooms/{code}/dev/skip-setup. FIRST line of handler: return HTTP 404 (NOT 403 ŌĆö hide the surface) if not get_settings().dev_mode. Look up room via room_manager.get(code); 404 if missing. Then:
   - If phase == "lobby": dispatch a start action (host = state.players[0].id) via await room.handle_action(host_id, StartMsg()) to enter setup.
   - For each non-spectator player, dispatch create_card actions with placeholder cards (title f"dev-{pid}-{i}", description "gain 1 point") until _authored_count(pid) >= CARDS_TO_AUTHOR. This triggers the existing auto-start to playing.
   - If already "playing": no-op / return 409 with a clear message (do not crash).
   - Return room.snapshot().
   Use await room.handle_action(...) with the real StartMsg/CreateCardMsg models ŌĆö same objects drive_to_playing uses ŌĆö so all invariants/broadcasts fire naturally.
2. CONSIDER factoring the fill loop into a Room helper `async def dev_autofill_authoring(self)` living next to the phase logic so it's unit-testable, with the endpoint as a thin wrapper.

DEPENDS ON the DEV_MODE flag bead (sot.1).

GOTCHA: never write `except (A, B):` ŌĆö ruff-format corrupts it. Use `except Exception:`.
acceptance_criteria: With DEV_MODE=true: create a room + join 2 players, POST /rooms/{code}/dev/skip-setup, then GET /rooms/{code}/state shows phase=='playing', each player has STARTING_HAND_SIZE cards dealt, deck finalized. Calling when already playing returns a clear 409 (no crash). With DEV_MODE false: endpoint returns 404. Broadcasts to connected clients happen (state envelope), same as a normal start. Test added driving the endpoint (or the Room.dev_autofill_authoring helper) end-to-end.

title: fix(evals): unblock eval suite against configured LLM gateway
description:
Found during bead 82f.5 (eval vetting). The eval suite CANNOT run against the configured LLM gateway (.env: bifrost -> bedrock, LLM_CHAT_MODEL=us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-5). Three concrete, independently-confirmed defects:

1. DATA PATH FRAGILITY ŌĆö src/evals/harness.py DEFAULT_DATA uses Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / 'data'/'eval'/'eval_cards.json'. That hard-codes the repo depth; from a git worktree (.claude/worktrees/<b>/src/evals/harness.py) parents[3] resolves to the WRONG dir and raises FileNotFoundError. Locate data/eval/ robustly (walk up for a repo marker, or make it configurable) so harness/retriever_ab/improvement_ab work regardless of cwd.

2. HARDCODED JUDGE MODEL ŌĆö src/evals/judge.py JudgeLLM.__init__ defaults model='gpt-5.4-mini' and passes it to get_chat_model, OVERRIDING the configured LLM_CHAT_MODEL. Against the gateway this 400s: 'could not auto resolve a provider for the request'. The judge (and any other explicit-model call) should default to the configured chat model, not a hardcoded id.

3. UNCONDITIONAL temperature ŌĆö agent.llm.get_chat_model always passes temperature=0. The bedrock-routed Claude model rejects it: 400 '`temperature` is deprecated for this model.' Make temperature omittable (e.g. don't send it, or send only when the provider supports it). CONFIRMED: the identical gateway call with the configured model and NO temperature returns 'OK' cleanly.

Once fixed, run harness/retriever_ab/improvement_ab and feed the REAL numbers into docs/WRITEUP.md (bead 82f.1) and the relocated docs/RETRIEVER_ANALYSIS.md, replacing the current hand-authored placeholder tables.

NOTE: temperature handling touches agent.llm.get_chat_model which is also being edited in a parallel session's uncommitted bifrost work ŌĆö coordinate to avoid clobbering (see bd memory cross-session-uncommitted-bifrost-work).
acceptance_criteria: uv run python -m evals.harness --limit 3 completes against the bifrost/bedrock gateway and prints real scores; harness locates data/eval/ regardless of cwd/worktree depth; judge + agent use the configured LLM_CHAT_MODEL; no unconditional temperature is sent to models that reject it.
close_reason: Merged. harness --limit 3 verified against bifrost/bedrock gateway: all scores 1.0. Feeding real numbers into docs/WRITEUP.md remains follow-up (user has uncommitted edits there).

title: feat(config): generic OpenAI-compatible LLM gateway (replace openai/ollama branch)
description:
Project requirement: use an LLM Gateway. Today the code hardcodes an 'openai' vs 'ollama' choice; instead it should accept an optional base URL to hit ANY OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g. a company gateway like bifrost). Code should be a generic gateway, not provider-specific.

DECISIONS (from user): shared single gateway ŌĆö one base_url/api_key/model set drives BOTH chat and embeddings via LLM_* env vars. Clean break ŌĆö remove LLM_PROVIDER / OPENAI_* / OLLAMA_* and all is_ollama branching.

CURRENT DESIGN (confirmed): src/config.py hardcodes the choice via llm_provider (config.py:24) and provider-aware accessors is_ollama/chat_model/embedding_model/llm_base_url/llm_api_key/embedding_dimensions (config.py:97-133) plus require_openai_api_key (config.py:146-165). Consumers already read the accessors (not the raw provider), so the seam is clean. Library is langchain-openai (ChatOpenAI, OpenAIEmbeddings) ŌĆö both already accept any OpenAI-compatible base_url.
design:
CONFIG (src/config.py): replace two-provider fields + branching with generic settings ŌĆö llm_base_url:str='' (empty = OpenAI library default), llm_api_key:str='' (supply a placeholder for the OpenAI client's non-empty requirement when a keyless local gateway is used), llm_chat_model, llm_embedding_model, llm_embedding_dimensions. Remove llm_provider, all openai_*/ollama_* fields, is_ollama, and the hard require_openai_api_key gate (repurpose into a soft, non-fatal check). Replace check_embedding_ctx_length=not is_ollama logic with a generic switch: default to the safe raw-string behavior for any non-default llm_base_url, or expose an explicit llm_embedding_raw_input:bool.

CONSUMERS (verify, mostly unchanged): src/agent/llm.py:28-33 (ChatOpenAI base_url=settings.llm_base_url); src/agent/rag/embeddings.py:40-51 (OpenAIEmbeddings base_url + check_embedding_ctx_length ŌĆö update flag); src/agent/rag/store.py:48 (embedding_dimensions() sizes Qdrant collection ŌĆö keep reading new setting); src/board/app.py:118 (require_openai_api_key startup gate ŌĆö relax so a gateway URL doesn't hard-fail). MUST-FIX: src/evals/judge.py:70 constructs ChatOpenAI(...) directly, bypassing the factory and NOT gateway-aware ŌĆö route it through get_chat_model() (src/agent/llm.py).

DOCS: rewrite the LLM block in .env.example (currently :4-24) to the LLM_* scheme; update README/docs mentioning LLM_PROVIDER/ollama; note ollama now = LLM_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1 + LLM_API_KEY=ollama + 768-dim embedding model.
acceptance_criteria: Setting only LLM_BASE_URL/LLM_API_KEY/LLM_CHAT_MODEL/LLM_EMBEDDING_MODEL/LLM_EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS points chat, embeddings, RAG, and the eval judge at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (bifrost, ollama, or hosted OpenAI when base_url is blank). No openai/ollama conditionals remain. Tests updated (get_settings.cache_clear() between env changes).
close_reason: reconcile: re-close after rebase-abort reverted live Dolt DB; all merged to main

title: feat(setup): auto-start game when all players finish authoring (remove Start button)
description:
Manual playtest UX. During authoring a 'Start Game' button appears, but the game should just automatically start once all cards are authored ŌĆö no button.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR (confirmed): setup->playing is triggered only by a human {type:'start'} message; _handle_start routes setup -> _start_playing (src/board/rooms/room.py:197-218). A host-only 'Start game' button exists at frontend/components/setup-phase.tsx:150-157 (gated disabled={remaining>0}). No code auto-advances setup->playing when all players finish.
design: In _handle_create_card (setup branch, after registering the card), check whether every non-spectator has now authored CARDS_TO_AUTHOR ŌĆö mirror the 'behind' logic at room.py:241 using turn_players() / _authored_count. If none are behind, call _start_playing(...) directly. Then remove the setup Start button block in setup-phase.tsx:150-157. Keep _handle_start idempotent/guarded so a late manual 'start' after auto-start is a no-op ('Game already started', existing else branch room.py:197-218). Interacts with the authoring-cap bead (both edit the setup branch) ŌĆö recommend doing together. Layout note: code in src/board/ + frontend/.
acceptance_criteria: Once the final player authors their 5th card, the game transitions to 'playing' automatically with no button press; the Start button no longer appears in the setup UI.
close_reason: reconcile: re-close after rebase-abort reverted live Dolt DB; all merged to main

title: bug(setup): no upper limit on cards authored during setup phase
description:
Manual playtest bug. During the authoring/create-deck phase players can create MORE than the 5-card limit, as long as they do it before other players finish.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): _handle_create_card (src/board/rooms/room.py:718-741) has no upper-bound guard. The only limit check is a LOWER-bound start gate in _start_playing (room.py:241: players with authored count < CARDS_TO_AUTHOR block the start). CARDS_TO_AUTHOR = BLANKS_PER_PLAYER = 5 (room.py:52, deck.py:189). So any player can author 6+ cards until the host starts. The frontend never disables the authoring button on count (frontend/components/setup-phase.tsx:98-100).
design: In _handle_create_card, when phase == 'setup' and self._authored_count(player_id) >= CARDS_TO_AUTHOR, reject with an 'error' message (connections.send, not broadcast) and return BEFORE registering the card. Optionally disable the 'Author a card' button in setup-phase.tsx:98 when remaining === 0. Interacts with the auto-start bead (both edit the setup branch of _handle_create_card) ŌĆö recommend implementing together. Layout note: code lives in src/board/.
acceptance_criteria: A player cannot author more than 5 cards during setup; attempting a 6th yields a benign error and no card is added.
close_reason: reconcile: re-close after rebase-abort reverted live Dolt DB; all merged to main

title: C6/C7/C9: agent<->engine seam cluster (read_game_state + read_engine_methods + persona + comment)
description: ONE coherent sub-epic ŌĆö these share the new agent<->engine contract and can't be tested independently. (C6) read_game_state tool: expose an IMMUTABLE GameState snapshot to the agent including creator_id per card + the current actor, so the persona 'punish-author if player==author' branch is reachable. Must consume a PASSED-IN snapshot and never import board. (C7) read_engine_methods tool: introspect the available ops (models/effects.py Op union) + the GameEngine facade (bead B) + how custom snippet code is applied (sandbox), so the agent knows what already exists before generating custom code. (C9) persona logic wired to read_game_state + funny-comment output: the agent emits a short funny comment for every interpreted card (roast losers, mock overpowered/broken cards, deadpan on trivial ones) and, when it can't cleanly interpret, picks the in-character persona_action.

notes: Depends on C1 (skeleton) AND B (facade to introspect). Grouped so no bead lands a half-built contract.
