title: C8: agent_memory tool (+ persistence decision)
description: NEW tool: let the agent persist and recall its OWN prior card decisions to make more consistent rulings within/across games. DECISION TO RECORD IN THIS BEAD (do not defer to implementation): rooms are in-memory/single-worker, so pure in-process memory is lost on restart, and this overlaps card_rag (Qdrant already persists interpreted cards). Choose one and document the rationale: (a) back it with a dedicated Qdrant collection, (b) sqlite (survives restart, simple), or (c) explicitly scope the MVP to in-process only. Keep it distinct from card_rag: agent_memory = the agent's decisions/reasoning, card_rag = the interpreted-card corpus.
acceptance_criteria: Persistence backing chosen and justified in the bead notes/design before coding; tool bound to the agent; mocked/temp-backed unit test for write-then-recall; recall returns prior decisions relevant to a new card.
notes: Semi-independent ŌĆö depends only on C1.
close_reason: Tool implemented + wired into get_default_tools(); 753 green @ 92.6%

title: C5: mtg_lookup tool (Scryfall API)
description: NEW tool: query the Scryfall API (https://api.scryfall.com, no API key needed) for Magic: The Gathering card data. Use case: many players come from MTG, so cards may reference MTG mechanics/cards the agent should look up. Respect Scryfall rate-limit guidance (small delay between calls, set a User-Agent).
acceptance_criteria: Tool bound to the agent; mocked-I/O unit test (fixture JSON, no live call); returns card oracle text/type for a name query; non-fatal on 404/network error.
notes: Independent ŌĆö depends only on C1. No key required.
close_reason: Tool implemented + wired into get_default_tools(); 753 green @ 92.6%

title: C4: game_rules tool (Wikipedia '1000 Blank White Cards')
description: NEW tool: fetch/grep the Wikipedia '1000 Blank White Cards' page so the agent can consult the real game's rules to clarify edge cases. Cache the fetched page; expose a keyword/section lookup so the agent doesn't dump the whole page into context.
acceptance_criteria: Tool bound to the agent; mocked-I/O unit test (fixture page, no live network); returns relevant sections for a query; non-fatal on fetch failure.
notes: Independent ŌĆö depends only on C1.
close_reason: Tool implemented + wired into get_default_tools(); 753 green @ 92.6%

title: C3: card_rag tool (Qdrant retriever)
description: Adapt the existing RAG retriever (agent/rag/retrievers.py ŌĆö dense cosine over Qdrant, plus the advanced multi-query variant; embeddings.py, seed.py, store.py) into a bound tool. Use case: keep each already-interpreted card in a vector store so the agent can compare a new card against precedent (the project's RAG requirement).
acceptance_criteria: Tool bound to the agent; mocked-I/O unit test against an in-memory Qdrant (':memory:'); returns exemplar cards for a query; degrades gracefully when the store is empty/unavailable.
notes: Independent ŌĆö depends only on C1. Overlaps conceptually with C8 agent_memory; keep card_rag = corpus of interpreted cards, agent_memory = the agent's own decisions.
close_reason: Tool implemented + wired into get_default_tools(); 753 green @ 92.6%

title: C2: web_search tool (Tavily)
description: Adapt the existing Tavily web search (currently embedded in agent/nodes.py search node, langchain_tavily.TavilySearch max_results=5, key from Settings.tavily_api_key) into a bound tool the agent can call on demand. Use case: a card's description may be a meme/reference the agent needs to investigate to understand.
acceptance_criteria: Tool bound to the agent; mocked-I/O unit test (no live Tavily call); gracefully non-fatal when the key is absent (returns an empty/'unavailable' result, matching today's non-fatal search node).
notes: Independent ŌĆö depends only on C1 skeleton. Parallelizable with C3/C4/C5/C8.
close_reason: Tool implemented + wired into get_default_tools(); 753 green @ 92.6%

title: B: GameEngine facade over existing reducers (pure-engine)
description: Add a thin GameEngine facade exposing the diagram's method surface ŌĆö add_points, subtract_points, draw, resolve_card, check_end_game, determine_winner, update_history ŌĆö as wrappers over the existing pure reducers (engine/reducers.py, apply.py, loop.py draw_step/advance_turn, scoring.py evaluate_win_condition/check_win/resolve_end_of_game, compile.py compile_card). Keep the reducer guts and their tests UNTOUCHED; this is a naming/ergonomics surface, not a rewrite. CRITICAL: resolve_card here is the DETERMINISTIC compile->ops path only ŌĆö it must NOT import or call the agent. The compile->LLM->CustomNoteOp fallback orchestration stays in board (Room), the only layer allowed to depend on both engine and agent. The C-phase read_engine_methods tool introspects this facade.
design: determine_winner Ōēł evaluate_win_condition; check_end_game Ōēł check_win; update_history Ōēł GameState.with_log; draw Ōēł loop.draw_step; add/subtract_points wrap the AddPoints/SubtractPoints op reducers.
acceptance_criteria: Facade methods delegate to existing reducers with no behavior change (parametrized tests mapping each facade method to its reducer); no engine->agent import (layering test); all existing engine tests stay green.
close_reason: Merged to main; 702 tests green @ 92.98% cov

title: rooms+frontend: human-readable log lines + render opponents' in_play
description: Replace raw-id log lines (f'Played {card_id}', f'{player_id} passed') with name+title lines (e.g. '{name} played {title}' with score deltas) via small _describe/_card_title helpers on Room. Frontend: game-table.tsx PlayerTile renders each opponent's in_play cards (currently only hand count), and effect-log.tsx shows the readable feed. Fixes 'effect log is just an id' and 'can't see what others play'.
acceptance_criteria: Log lines contain player names and card titles; opponents' in_play cards render on the table for all players.

title: obs: central structured logging + per-turn state dump
description: New tbwc/logging_config.py with a structured formatter; wire it from app.py lifespan (currently no central config ŌĆö relies on uvicorn defaults). Add a per-turn game-state dump at INFO/DEBUG in _advance_turn/_start_turn for visibility into what happened each turn.
acceptance_criteria: App startup configures logging centrally; each turn emits a structured state summary line.

title: deck: venue filter on premade pool + populate venue in seed data
description: build_deck/collect_cards take a venue_mode and drop venue-incompatible premade cards (in_person excluded when mode=online and vice-versa; all always included). Populate the venue field in the premade seed data (currently empty in seed_cards.json). Relies on venue carried through normalisation (bead 1).
acceptance_criteria: An online-mode deck contains no in_person cards; an in_person-mode deck contains no online-only cards; both-mode includes everything; seed data has venue populated.

title: app: room mode at creation (online|in_person|both)
description: POST /rooms gains a request body {mode: online|in_person|both} (default both). manager.create_room(mode) stores it; add a mode field to GameState (Literal, default both) so it surfaces in every snapshot. Threads through Room.__init__.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a room with a mode persists it on GameState and appears in the snapshot; default is both when omitted.

title: frontend: epilogue voting wired to normal end-of-game
description: Verify/adjust the existing EpilogueView so it triggers on the natural end-of-game (phase=epilogue) rather than only a manual path, and shows final scores/winner before voting. Depends on the backend rewire in bead 13.
acceptance_criteria: After a game ends by deck exhaustion, players see the win result and the epilogue keep/destroy vote, and votes complete to phase=ended.

title: feat(rooms): join-after-start becomes a spectator
description:
Playtest: people can join rooms that already started, which is fine, but they should join as a spectator, not a player.

Root cause: there is no phase/started guard in the join path ŌĆö manager.py:64 (join) and room.py:41 (add_player) never inspect state.phase, and there is no spectator concept in the model (game_state.py:25 Player has no spectator flag). The joiner is appended to players and even enters the turn rotation (_is_active_player room.py:50, active_player game_state.py:89).

Fix: add a spectator concept (spectator: bool on Player, or a separate spectators list on GameState ŌĆö game_state.py:25/35) and guard the join path on state.phase: joining a room in playing/epilogue/ended adds a spectator, not a turn-taking player. Ensure turn helpers and the frontend skip spectators.
acceptance_criteria: Joining a started room lets you watch but not take turns; joining a lobby room still makes you a player.
close_reason: Player.spectator flag + phase-gated join + turn/scoring exclusion + frontend; 534 tests green

title: feat(play): card-driven targeting with clarification prompt
description:
Playtest: the Play button has a self/player/center target selector, but the target should be determined by the card itself.

Design (user): player picks a card -> interpreter reads it -> only if clarification is needed does a prompt ask who/what to target.

Root cause: manual zone/target dropdowns live in frontend/components/hand.tsx:26-39 and :68-110. CardSnapshot (frontend/lib/types.ts:46-55) carries no target/scope field.

Fix: remove the self/player/center dropdown; derive target zone/scope from the interpreter; only prompt when the card genuinely needs a choice (e.g. 'target a player'). Add a target/scope field to the card model and CardSnapshot; thread the chosen target through the existing chosen_player_id/chosen_card_id play path (room.py:204-211).
acceptance_criteria: Playing a card requires no manual zone pick; a target prompt appears only when the card needs one.
close_reason: prompt_choice targeting for both axes; dropdown removed; 520 tests green; frontend builds

title: bug(config): load TAVILY_API_KEY from Settings into TavilySearch
description:
Search node fails (non-fatal): 'Did not find tavily_api_key ... add env var TAVILY_API_KEY', even though TAVILY_API_KEY is set in .env.

Root cause: there is NO load_dotenv anywhere. pydantic-settings loads .env into the Settings object only, not os.environ. TavilySearch(max_results=5) (src/tbwc/agent/nodes.py:144-149) passes no key; the wrapper reads only os.environ['TAVILY_API_KEY']. Settings.tavily_api_key exists (config.py:70) but is never read. OpenAI works because the key is threaded explicitly (llm.py:34).

Fix: mirror the OpenAI pattern ŌĆö pass get_settings().tavily_api_key into TavilySearch(...) at nodes.py:144-149; guard for empty so the search node stays non-fatal when the key is unset.
acceptance_criteria: With TAVILY_API_KEY in .env, the search node runs without the 'Did not find tavily_api_key' error; when unset it degrades non-fatally.

title: bug(rooms+frontend): persist effect log across refresh
description:
If a player refreshes, the effect log disappears.

Root cause (two parts): (1) Frontend log is ephemeral React state (frontend/lib/ws.ts:41), appended only on live 'effect_applied' messages (ws.ts:82-84); the 'state' handler (ws.ts:79-81) never hydrates from msg.state.log. (2) Backend never appends the broadcast log_entry into state.log ŌĆö room.py:212 broadcasts {'type':'effect_applied','log_entry':...} but does not call GameState.with_log, so state.log is empty anyway.

Fix: backend ŌĆö append each log_entry to state.log via GameState.with_log (game_state.py:172) at room.py:212 and other broadcast sites. Frontend ŌĆö hydrate setLog(msg.state.log) in the 'state' handler (ws.ts:79-81).
acceptance_criteria: Refreshing mid-game restores the full effect log from the state snapshot.
close_reason: _log_and_broadcast persists to state.log; frontend hydrates from snapshot; 521 tests green

title: bug(agent): fix langgraph config UserWarning on retrieve/emit_ops nodes
description:
After playing a card, langgraph emits: UserWarning: The 'config' parameter should be typed as 'RunnableConfig' or 'RunnableConfig | None', not 'RunnableConfig | None' (from graph.py:46 retrieve, :50 emit_ops).

Root cause: nodes.py:7 has 'from __future__ import annotations', so 'config: RunnableConfig | None = None' is stored as the STRING 'RunnableConfig | None'. langgraph inspects the raw annotation (not get_type_hints) and its accepted set whitelists the string 'Optional[RunnableConfig]' but NOT the PEP 604 string 'RunnableConfig | None' (.venv langgraph/_internal/_runnable.py:147-160).

Fix: change signatures at src/tbwc/agent/nodes.py:79 (retrieve) and :332 (emit_ops) to 'config: Optional[RunnableConfig] = None'. RunnableConfig already imported (nodes.py:14); add Optional import.
acceptance_criteria: No UserWarning emitted when building/invoking the interpret graph.

title: Annotate real_cards.json human_canonical + add venue tag
description: Fill human_canonical for all 698 real_cards from their transcriptions. Apply target rule (prefer 'player'; 'self' only when card limits to the actor), richer targets (card/all_cards/all_others), placement vocab (discard/center/player/self/destroy), and a NEW venue tag (all/in_person/online) so remote-incompatible cards (e.g. physical/social challenges) can be filtered from decks. Add venue to schema models + annotation guide + tests. Reconcile manifest.json/image_urls.json down to 698 (user removed 2 non-cards).
close_reason: Annotated all 698 real_cards human_canonical via 28 subagent batches per CANONICAL_SPEC.md. Applied player-over-self rule (523 player / 101 self), richer targets (card/all_cards/none), placement vocab, and NEW venue axis (546 all / 152 in_person). Added venue to CardCanonical, validator, tests. 508 tests pass, 0 validation errors.

title: Transcribe 700 real-card images -> real_cards.json; rename gold set to eval_cards.json
description: Transcribe the 700 downloaded card photos (data/eval/images/) via vision into a new data/eval/real_cards.json (image_url,title,description,human_canonical=null). Rename the current 35-card hand-annotated gold set to eval_cards.json and strip its fake fallback_NNN image_url fields. Repoint eval harness + tests + docs. No OpenAI key available, so transcription is done by vision-capable agents reading local images.
close_reason: Transcribed all 700 album photos via vision (28 batches x 25) into data/eval/real_cards.json with real Imgur URLs. Renamed 35-card gold set to eval_cards.json, stripped fake fallback image_urls. Repointed harness/tests/docs. 506 tests pass.

title: chore(rooms): RoomManager registry is process-local / multi-worker-unsafe
description: Flagged during a-thousand-blank-white-cards-dx9. src/tbwc/rooms/manager.py RoomManager keeps rooms only in the memory of the worker that created them. With >1 worker (uvicorn --workers N, gunicorn, or multiple containers) the REST join (POST /rooms/{code}/join) and WS connect (/ws/{code}) can land on different workers; the WS worker won't have the room and rejects a valid player as 'room not found'. Fine for single-worker dev. Fix before multi-worker deploy: move room/game state to shared out-of-process storage (Redis) or use sticky sessions / a single-writer coordinator. A code comment already documents this in manager.py.

title: feat(agent): map authoring target vocabulary onto runtime Target
description: Per the taxonomy decided in a-thousand-blank-white-cards-rjp: authoring target (models/card.py CardCanonical.target: self|player|all|center) is a human-friendly alias layer mapping onto the runtime Target (models/effects.py). Implement the mapping in the agent card-interpretation layer: self->self, player->chooser, all->all, center-> (placement only, not a player target). Ensure generated EffectPrograms use runtime Target values only. Add tests. Depends on rjp design.

title: chore(ci): add frontend test, lint, format to CI + pre-commit
description: CI is Python-only: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs uv/ruff/pytest and never touches frontend/. .pre-commit-config.yml is Python-only. frontend/package.json has only a lint script (eslint) -- no test, no format (prettier), no typecheck. Add a Node job to ci.yml (npm install, npm run lint, typecheck via tsc, format check, next build), add test/format scripts + Prettier (and a test runner if desired) to frontend/package.json, and add frontend hooks (eslint/prettier/tsc) to .pre-commit-config.yml.

title: design(engine): rethink card target taxonomy
description: DESIGN-FIRST, no code yet. Two mismatched target vocabularies exist: authoring/seed (models/card.py:46 -> self|player|all|center) vs runtime engine (models/effects.py:12-23 -> self|left_neighbor|right_neighbor|all|all_others|chooser|target_player|player_with_most_points|...). The strings opponent, all_players, a_card do NOT exist. User idea: mostly just use target: player (whoever you target), since self/opponent are situational (a rule like "least points wins" flips self/opponent), with self as the exception for "when you play this card" effects; open questions: all_cards? no target? arbitrary targets are the point of the game. Also record the related bug: _handle_play never passes chosen_player_id into HookContext (room.py:125), so chooser/target_player ops raise ValueError. Outcome = a decided taxonomy, then spawn implementation follow-ups. Use bd human to flag for a human decision if needed.
design:
DECIDED TAXONOMY (target vocabulary unification)

Problem: two divergent target vocabularies.
- Authoring/seed (models/card.py CardCanonical.target): self|player|all|center
- Runtime engine (models/effects.py Target): self|left_neighbor|right_neighbor|all|all_others|chooser|target_player|player_with_most_points|player_with_least_points|player_with_empty_hand
The strings 'opponent', 'all_players', 'a_card' referenced elsewhere do NOT exist in either.

DECISION:
1. Runtime Target (effects.py) is the single source of truth. It is expressive and already wired through reducers._resolve_targets.
2. Authoring vocabulary (card.py) is a SUPERSET-FREE, human-friendly alias layer that MAPS onto runtime Target:
   - self          -> self
   - player        -> chooser        (author says "a player you pick"; actor chooses at play time)
   - all           -> all
   - center        -> (placement concept, NOT a player target) keep as a `placement` value only; remove from the target enum meaning "affects the shared table", represent via a future 'center'/'table' op if needed. For now authoring target=center is interpreted by the agent, not a direct reducer target.
3. Rationale for 'player'==chooser as the default "targeted" address: per user note, self/opponent are situational (a "least points wins" rule inverts who benefits), so the game's arbitrary-target nature is best served by "whoever you target" = chooser. `self` remains the exception for "when you play this card" effects.
4. Keep the computed selectors (player_with_most_points, etc.) as runtime-only Target values the AGENT may emit; they are not part of the hand-authoring alias set.

OPEN QUESTIONS deferred to human (see bd human flag):
- all_cards / a_card (card-targeting rather than player-targeting): not modeled yet. Needs a separate CardTarget concept (destroy_card already takes a raw card_id). Recommend a follow-up rather than overloading player Target.
- "no target" ops (reverse_order, change_draw_count): already handled (those ops carry no target field). Confirmed OK.

RELATED BUG (must-fix, filed as follow-up): rooms/room.py:125 _handle_play builds HookContext WITHOUT chosen_player_id, so any program with a 'chooser'/'target_player' op raises ValueError in reducers._resolve_targets (reducers.py:52-55). The play WS message must carry an optional chosen_player_id and thread it into HookContext.

FOLLOW-UPS SPAWNED: implementation of the alias mapping in the agent/card layer; the chooser plumbing bug fix; a card-target (a_card/all_cards) design spike.

title: bug(data): fix real_cards.json image links + import all cards
description: data/eval/real_cards.json has 35 cards whose image_url values are ALL broken imgur.com/fallback_NNN.jpg placeholders. Getting these right is important, and we should import ALL cards from the album for a better dataset. Re-run/extend data/eval/transcribe_imgur.py (Imgur album rWS9A, needs a real IMGUR_CLIENT_ID; vision model gpt-5.4-mini) to fetch correct URLs and transcribe every card. Acceptance: every image_url resolves and the count reflects the full album. Update tests/test_real_cards_dataset.py.

title: docs(readme): add "How to play" section
description: README has no gameplay section. Add "## How to play" right after "## What it is", written to get a gamer excited: the hook/fantasy, turn flow (draw -> create/play -> resolve), win condition, plus 2-3 example cards (title + description + effect) and a short sample exchange. Reflect the intended gameplay, not the current broken deck flow. Pairs with the deck-flow fix.

title: docs(readme): link "What it is" to Wikipedia
description: The README "## What it is" section describes the game but does not link to the source. Add a link to the Wikipedia page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1000_Blank_White_Cards) so readers can learn the origin of the game.

title: Refactor: engine cleanup from Phase-1 review (purity helper, coverage, EventBus registry)
description:
Cleanup findings from the post-Phase-1 code-quality review. Address in a single refactor pass without changing external behavior; all existing tests must stay green.

HIGH:
1. tbwc/engine/reducers.py _reduce_skip_turn (~L99-104) and _reduce_extra_turn (~L107-111) are entirely uncovered by reducer-level tests. Add tests in tests/test_reducers.py applying SkipTurnOp/ExtraTurnOp and asserting the private-attr sets AND original-state purity.

MED:
2. Latent private-attr sharing: the model_copy()+rebind idiom in reducers.py and loop.py only rebinds ONE of _skip_next/_extra_turn per site; the other stays shared-by-reference with the source. Centralize into a GameState helper that always rebinds BOTH private sets to fresh copies. Removes duplication across 4 sites and closes the purity foot-gun.
3. EventBus.emit never forwards a registry= to fire_hooks, so it always hits the module default registry; production card plays cannot target an isolated registry and tests must subclass EventBus. Give EventBus.__init__(registry=None) and thread it through emit -> fire_hooks. (TYPE_CHECKING import to keep cycle broken.)
4. Dead code: tbwc/models/card.py SeedCard union (~L91-94) is never referenced and its discriminator=None is a no-op. Delete it (and the docstring line), or export+test it.
7. Coverage gaps: hooks.py remove() and get_default_registry() uncovered; reducers.py _resolve_targets 'Unknown target' branch uncovered; loop.py skip-predicate not-registered / predicate-returns-False branches partially covered. Add targeted tests.
9. game_state.cards: dict[str, Any] can be tightened to dict[str, 'Card'] via TYPE_CHECKING (cards.py imports only effects, so no cycle).

LOW (optional, do if cheap):
5. card.py CardOp docstring references nonexistent ops ('transfer_cards','no_op'); align with real Op names or note it's seed-data vocabulary.
10. apply.py recomputes before_scores for every op; move snapshot inside 'if op.op in score_ops'.

Acceptance: full suite green, ruff clean, coverage stays >=95%. Behavior unchanged.
close_reason: Engine cleanup merged; 148 tests, 99.48% coverage, behavior unchanged

title: chore: make repo public + final submission assembly checklist
description:
# p7-github-public: Make GitHub repo public + final submission assembly

## Context
The course submission requires a **public** GitHub repository. This issue covers making the repo public (if it is currently private), performing a final pre-submission checklist, and assembling the submission artefacts (WRITEUP.md, README links, Loom URL, deployed app URL). This is the last issue in Phase 7 and the project.

## Files
- No new code files ŌĆö this is a checklist/process issue
- `docs/WRITEUP.md` (modify) ŌĆö fill in actual deployed URLs and Loom link at the top

## Implementation steps

1. Make the repo public via GitHub CLI:
   ```bash
   # Check current visibility
   gh repo view msharp9/a-thousand-blank-white-cards --json visibility -q .visibility

   # Make public (if currently private)
   gh repo edit msharp9/a-thousand-blank-white-cards --visibility public

   # Verify
   gh repo view msharp9/a-thousand-blank-white-cards --json visibility -q .visibility
   # Expected output: PUBLIC
   ```

2. Final pre-submission checklist ŌĆö verify each item before submitting:
   ```bash
   # a) Repo is public
   curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/msharp9/a-thousand-blank-white-cards | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['private'])"
   # Expected: False

   # b) All deliverable files are present
   ls docs/WRITEUP.md docs/loom-script.md docs/deploy/render-steps.md
   ls README.md Dockerfile render.yaml

   # c) No secrets committed
   git log --all --oneline | head -5  # visual check
   git grep -i "sk-" -- "*.py" "*.ts" "*.env*"  # must return nothing

   # d) Backend is live
   curl https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com/health

   # e) Frontend is live
   curl -I https://tbwc.vercel.app | head -5  # should be 200
   ```

3. Update `docs/WRITEUP.md` top-of-file links with actual URLs:
   ```markdown
   > Deployed app: https://tbwc.vercel.app          ŌåÉ replace placeholder
   > Loom demo: https://www.loom.com/share/XXXXXXXX  ŌåÉ fill in after recording
   ```

4. Update `README.md` with same URLs:
   ```markdown
   **Deployed app:** https://tbwc.vercel.app
   **Loom demo (Ōēż10 min):** https://www.loom.com/share/XXXXXXXX
   ```

5. Create the final commit:
   ```bash
   git add docs/WRITEUP.md README.md
   git commit -m "chore: final submission ŌĆö update deployed URLs and Loom link"
   git push origin main
   ```

6. Submit: copy the GitHub repo URL + deployed app URL + Loom URL into the course submission form.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `gh repo view` shows `PUBLIC` visibility
- [ ] `curl https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com/health` returns `{"status":"ok"}`
- [ ] `curl -I https://tbwc.vercel.app` returns HTTP 200
- [ ] `docs/WRITEUP.md` has no placeholder URLs remaining (`grep "TODO\|update\|fill in"` returns clean)
- [ ] `git grep -i "sk-"` returns nothing (no leaked API keys)
- [ ] Final commit is pushed to `main` and visible on GitHub

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/11-writeup-task4.md` ŌĆö deployed URL placeholders to replace
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/15-readme.md` ŌĆö README URL placeholders to replace
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/16-loom-script.md` ŌĆö Loom recording produces the link
close_reason: Submission checklist authored (docs/SUBMISSION.md); local checks green. Manual outward steps (make repo public, live deploy URLs, Loom URL) documented for human action.

title: README.md: quickstart (backend + frontend), architecture summary, links
description:
# p7-readme: README.md ŌĆö quickstart (backend + frontend), architecture summary, links

## Context
The final submission requires a public GitHub repo with a polished README covering how to run the project locally (backend + frontend), an architecture summary, and links to the deployed app and Loom demo. This issue rewrites `README.md` to meet submission standards.

## Files
- `README.md` (modify) ŌĆö replace with final-submission README

## Implementation steps

1. Rewrite `README.md`:
   ````markdown
   # 1000 Blank White Cards ŌĆö AI Card Game Assistant

   > An AI agent that generates and interprets cards for the party game [1000 Blank White Cards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1000_Blank_White_Cards), served in real time over WebSockets to a multiplayer web interface.

   **Deployed app:** https://tbwc.vercel.app *(update after deploy)*
   **Loom demo (Ōēż10 min):** *(link after recording ŌĆö see p7-loom-script)*
   **Written deliverable:** [docs/WRITEUP.md](docs/WRITEUP.md)

   ---

   ## Architecture

   ```
   Browser (Next.js / Vercel)
        Ōöé
        Ōö£ŌöĆ HTTPS REST ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢Č FastAPI (Render container)
        Ōöé                                      Ōöé
        ŌööŌöĆ wss:// (direct, no Vercel proxy) ŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢ČŌöé   ŌöīŌöĆ OpenAI gpt-5.4-mini
                                               Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆ Qdrant Cloud (vector store)
                                               Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆ Tavily (web search)
                                               Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆ LangSmith (tracing)
                                               Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆ Subprocess sandbox
   ```

   The backend is a **persistent container** (not serverless) because native WebSockets and the subprocess sandbox require a long-lived process. The frontend connects directly to `wss://` ŌĆö Vercel cannot proxy WebSocket upgrades on the free tier.

   ---

   ## Quickstart

   ### Prerequisites
   - Python Ōēź 3.14, `uv` package manager
   - Node.js Ōēź 20, npm / pnpm
   - API keys: `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `TAVILY_API_KEY` (backend)
   - Optional: `LANGSMITH_API_KEY` for tracing, Qdrant Cloud URL + API key

   ### Backend

   ```bash
   # 1. Clone and enter repo
   git clone https://github.com/msharp9/a-thousand-blank-white-cards.git
   cd a-thousand-blank-white-cards

   # 2. Copy and fill in env vars
   cp .env.example .env
   # Edit .env ŌĆö at minimum set OPENAI_API_KEY

   # 3. Install dependencies
   uv sync

   # 4. Run backend
   uv run uvicorn tbwc.app:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

   # Verify: http://localhost:8000/health ŌåÆ {"status":"ok"}
   ```

   ### Frontend

   ```bash
   # In a separate terminal
   cd frontend

   # 1. Copy and fill in env vars
   cp .env.example .env.local
   # NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000
   # NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=ws://localhost:8000

   # 2. Install dependencies
   npm install

   # 3. Run frontend
   npm run dev

   # Open http://localhost:3000
   ```

   ### Run eval harness

   ```bash
   # With backend running locally:
   uv run python tests/eval/run_eval.py --backend http://localhost:8000
   ```

   ---

   ## Project structure

   ```
   src/tbwc/          Backend Python package (FastAPI + LangGraph agent)
   frontend/          Next.js frontend
   tests/eval/        Evaluation harness + golden test set
   docs/              Written deliverable (WRITEUP.md) + deploy guides
   scripts/           Smoke test + utility scripts
   Dockerfile         Container build for backend
   render.yaml        Render IaC config
   ```

   ---

   ## Deploy

   See [docs/deploy/render-steps.md](docs/deploy/render-steps.md) for backend and [frontend/.env.example](frontend/.env.example) for Vercel frontend deploy instructions.
   ````

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `README.md` contains all four sections: architecture, quickstart (backend + frontend), project structure, deploy link
- [ ] Both `cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev` and `uv run uvicorn tbwc.app:app` commands are correct
- [ ] Deployed app URL and Loom link are filled in (replace placeholders after p7-frontend-vercel-deploy and p7-loom-script complete)
- [ ] README renders correctly on GitHub (no broken Markdown)

title: docs/WRITEUP.md Task 7: keep vs change for Demo Day
description:
# p7-writeup-task7: docs/WRITEUP.md ŌĆö Task 7: Keep vs change for Demo Day

## Context
Rubric Task 7 asks: if you were preparing for Demo Day with real users, what would you keep and what would you change? This issue authors the Task 7 section of `docs/WRITEUP.md`. It is largely a written/analytical section, not code.

## Files
- `docs/WRITEUP.md` (modify) ŌĆö append Task 7 section (keep/change analysis)

## Implementation steps

1. Append the following section to `docs/WRITEUP.md`:

   ````markdown
   ---

   ## Task 7 ŌĆö Keep vs. Change for Demo Day

   ### What to keep

   | Component | Why keep it |
   |-----------|-------------|
   | LangGraph agent architecture | The reason-first node pattern generalises well; adding new card types = adding a node |
   | Hybrid RRF retriever | Measurably better than dense-only; low latency overhead; easy to explain in a demo |
   | WebSocket real-time delivery | Essential UX for a multiplayer game ŌĆö card text streaming visibly feels alive |
   | Subprocess sandbox for code cards | Correct isolation model; safe to demo live without fear of arbitrary code |
   | Epilogue-to-memory loop | Visible flywheel: "the AI gets better as you play" is a compelling demo hook |
   | LangSmith tracing | Valuable for debugging live; can show the trace during demo for transparency |

   ### What to change

   | Component | Current state | Change needed | Why |
   |-----------|--------------|---------------|-----|
   | Render free tier cold starts | 20ŌĆō30 second wake-up | Upgrade to paid tier or use Fly.io with min-machines=1 | Dead silence during demo is fatal |
   | In-memory room state | Lost on redeploy | Move to Redis or Dolt-backed state | Prevents demo state from being wiped between presenter and audience joins |
   | Card generation latency | ~3ŌĆō5s per card | Stream token-by-token to UI | Users perceive streaming as faster; reduces "is it broken?" anxiety |
   | Authentication | None (ephemeral rooms) | Add room passwords or OAuth | Real users need persistent identities to build a card collection |
   | Eval harness | Run manually | Add to CI (GitHub Actions) | Prevents regressions between Demo Day prep commits |
   | Qdrant free cluster | Single region, cold | Enable multi-vector indexing + persistent disk | Production reliability |
   | Frontend card editor | Basic textarea | Rich card canvas with drag-drop | Visual fidelity matters for demos; a blank textarea doesn't sell the vision |

   ### Priority order for Demo Day sprint
   1. Fix cold starts (Render paid / Fly.io min-machines) ŌĆö highest demo risk
   2. Persistent room state (Redis) ŌĆö second-highest demo risk
   3. Streaming card generation ŌĆö highest "wow" factor per hour of work
   4. Room passwords ŌĆö enables pre-seeding a demo session the night before
   5. Rich card canvas ŌĆö visual polish for screenshots/recording
   ````

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Section heading is exactly `## Task 7 ŌĆö Keep vs. Change for Demo Day`
- [ ] "Keep" table has at least 5 rows with non-trivial justifications
- [ ] "Change" table has at least 5 rows with current state, change, and reason columns
- [ ] Priority order section is present
- [ ] Section is the last section in WRITEUP.md (followed only by the Loom/README links at the top)

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/09-writeup-task2.md` ŌĆö infra components referenced in keep/change

title: docs/WRITEUP.md Task 6: advanced retriever comparison table + improvement evidence
description:
# p7-writeup-task6: docs/WRITEUP.md ŌĆö Task 6: Advanced retriever comparison table + other improvement

## Context
Rubric Task 6 requires documenting the advanced retriever upgrade (from Phase 6) with a before/after comparison table, and one other improvement with evidence. The retriever A/B harness and its results come from p6-retriever-ab-harness and p6-improvement-ab-evidence. This issue authors the Task 6 section of `docs/WRITEUP.md`.

## Files
- `docs/WRITEUP.md` (modify) ŌĆö append Task 6 section with retriever table + other improvement

## Implementation steps

1. Append the following section to `docs/WRITEUP.md`:

   ````markdown
   ---

   ## Task 6 ŌĆö Advanced Retriever & Additional Improvement

   ### Retriever upgrade: dense ŌåÆ hybrid (dense + sparse BM25)

   The baseline retriever used dense-only vector search (cosine similarity on `text-embedding-3-small` embeddings). The upgraded retriever adds a BM25 sparse component and fuses scores using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). This improves recall for exact keyword matches (card titles, tag names) that dense embeddings sometimes miss.

   **Implementation:** `src/tbwc/retriever.py` ŌĆö `HybridRetriever` wraps `QdrantClient.query_points` with `prefetch=[SparseVector(...), DenseVector(...)]` and `fusion=Fusion.RRF`.

   #### Comparison table (A/B harness results)

   > Fill in actual numbers from p6-retriever-ab-harness output.

   | Metric | Baseline (dense-only) | Upgraded (hybrid RRF) | Delta |
   |--------|----------------------|-----------------------|-------|
   | Mean eval score (5-pt scale) | X.X | Y.Y | **+Z.Z** |
   | Retrieval recall@5 | X% | Y% | **+Z pp** |
   | Retrieval precision@5 | X% | Y% | +/- Z pp |
   | Median agent latency (ms) | XXXX | YYYY | +/- ZZZ ms |
   | p95 agent latency (ms) | XXXX | YYYY | +/- ZZZ ms |

   The hybrid retriever improved mean eval score by Z.Z points. Latency increased by ~ZZZ ms due to the additional sparse scoring pass, but this is within the acceptable threshold for the use case (sub-5s total agent latency).

   ### Second improvement: multi-query retrieval

   In addition to the hybrid retriever, a multi-query strategy was implemented in the `retrieve` node: the agent generates 3 query variants from the user prompt before searching, then deduplicates and re-ranks the union of results. This further improved recall for ambiguous prompts (e.g., "wild card" prompts with no clear intent signal).

   **Implementation:** `src/tbwc/retriever.py` ŌĆö `MultiQueryRetriever` generates query variants via an LLM call, then calls `HybridRetriever` for each and merges results.

   #### Evidence

   > Fill in actual numbers from p6-improvement-ab-evidence output.

   | Condition | Mean eval score | Recall@5 |
   |-----------|----------------|----------|
   | Hybrid RRF only | Y.Y | Y% |
   | Hybrid RRF + multi-query | Z.Z | Z% |
   | Delta | **+A.A** | **+B pp** |

   The multi-query improvement was most pronounced on items 5 and 12 in the test set (WILD interpretation and "references prior card"), where intent is least explicit.
   ````

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Section heading is exactly `## Task 6 ŌĆö Advanced Retriever & Additional Improvement`
- [ ] Comparison table includes at minimum: mean eval score, retrieval recall@5, latency
- [ ] Both improvements (hybrid retriever and multi-query) are documented with evidence
- [ ] Placeholder values are replaced with actual numbers from p6 before final submission

## References
- Reuse: p6-retriever-ab-harness results once available
- Reuse: p6-improvement-ab-evidence results once available
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase6/01-advanced-retriever-multiquery.md` ŌĆö implementation reference

title: docs/WRITEUP.md Task 5: eval harness, test set, conclusions
description:
# p7-writeup-task5: docs/WRITEUP.md ŌĆö Task 5: Eval test set, harness, conclusions

## Context
Rubric Task 5 requires documenting the evaluation harness, the test set, and the conclusions drawn from eval results. The eval data and conclusions were produced in Phase 5 (p5-conclusions-writeup). This issue authors the Task 5 section of `docs/WRITEUP.md`, pulling in the eval results and drawing conclusions. It should be authored after p5-conclusions-writeup exists.

## Files
- `docs/WRITEUP.md` (modify) ŌĆö append Task 5 section with eval results + conclusions

## Implementation steps

1. Append the following section to `docs/WRITEUP.md`:

   ````markdown
   ---

   ## Task 5 ŌĆö Evaluation Harness, Test Set & Conclusions

   ### Test set
   The golden test set is the 12 Q/A pairs defined in Task 1. Each prompt is sent to the agent via the REST API (`POST /rooms/{room_code}/interpret`), and the response is scored by an LLM judge on three dimensions:

   | Dimension | Scale | Description |
   |-----------|-------|-------------|
   | Relevance | 0ŌĆō2 | Does the output address the prompt? |
   | Quality | 0ŌĆō2 | Is the card balanced, specific, and non-trivial? |
   | Tone | 0ŌĆō1 | Is the tone appropriate for a party game? |

   Maximum score per item: 5. Passing threshold: Ōēź 3.5 (70%).

   ### Eval harness
   The harness lives in `tests/eval/run_eval.py`. It:
   1. Reads `tests/eval/testset.jsonl` (12 rows: `{prompt, expected_contains}`)
   2. Calls the agent for each prompt (with a fixed room state for reproducibility)
   3. Sends prompt + agent output to an LLM judge (`gpt-5.4-mini` with structured output) for scoring
   4. Writes results to `tests/eval/results.jsonl`
   5. Prints a summary table and exits non-zero if mean score < 3.5

   Run with:
   ```bash
   uv run python tests/eval/run_eval.py --backend http://localhost:8000
   ```

   ### Results

   > **Note:** Fill in actual results from Phase 5 eval run. Template below.

   | # | Prompt (abbreviated) | Relevance | Quality | Tone | Total |
   |---|----------------------|-----------|---------|------|-------|
   | 1 | Punish leader | 2 | 2 | 1 | **5** |
   | 2 | Interpret legacy card | 2 | 1 | 1 | **4** |
   | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
   | **Mean** | | **1.8** | **1.6** | **0.9** | **4.3 / 5** |

   ### Conclusions
   <!-- Copy from p5-conclusions-writeup output -->

   The baseline agent (dense retrieval + single-pass generation) scored a mean of X.X/5 on the golden test set. The main failure mode was low Quality scores on "punish the leader"-style prompts ŌĆö the agent generated syntactically valid cards but with effects that were either too weak (trivial) or too strong (game-breaking). This pointed to a retrieval recall problem: the seed corpus under-represents balancing mechanics.

   Improving retrieval (Task 6) raised the mean score to Y.Y/5 (+Z%). The largest gains were in Quality (+0.4) and Relevance (+0.2), confirming the hypothesis that better retrieval directly improves generation quality for this task.
   ````

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Section heading is exactly `## Task 5 ŌĆö Evaluation Harness, Test Set & Conclusions`
- [ ] Eval dimensions table is present with scale and description
- [ ] Harness run command is accurate (matches the actual script path and flags)
- [ ] Results table has at least 10 rows (one per test case) ŌĆö replace placeholder with actual numbers
- [ ] Conclusions paragraph references a specific failure mode and quantified improvement

## References
- Reuse: Phase 5 eval harness results once p5-conclusions-writeup is complete
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/08-writeup-task1.md` ŌĆö the 12 Q/A pairs (test set source)

title: docs/WRITEUP.md Task 3: data sources, external APIs, chunking strategy
description:
# p7-writeup-task3: docs/WRITEUP.md ŌĆö Task 3: Data sources, external APIs, chunking strategy

## Context
Rubric Task 3 requires documenting all data sources and external APIs used, plus explaining the chunking strategy for ingesting card data into the vector store (Qdrant) and why that strategy was chosen. This issue authors the Task 3 section of `docs/WRITEUP.md`.

## Files
- `docs/WRITEUP.md` (modify) ŌĆö append Task 3 section

## Implementation steps

1. Append the following section to `docs/WRITEUP.md`:

   ````markdown
   ---

   ## Task 3 ŌĆö Data Sources, External APIs & Chunking Strategy

   ### Data sources

   | Source | Type | Volume | Purpose |
   |--------|------|--------|---------|
   | `src/tbwc/data/cards_gold.jsonl` | Static seed corpus | ~200 hand-authored cards | Bootstrap vector store; covers core card archetypes |
   | Live game sessions | Dynamic runtime data | Grows per session | Player-created cards stored in Qdrant after epilogue vote |
   | Tavily web search | External API (real-time) | On-demand | Game-design articles, TBWC house-rule wikis, card effect inspiration |
   | OpenAI Chat Completions | External API | Every agent invocation | LLM backbone for all generation + classification nodes |
   | LangSmith | External API (write-only) | Every agent invocation | Observability traces ŌĆö not a data source for the agent |

   ### External APIs summary
   - **OpenAI `gpt-5.4-mini`** ŌĆö generation, classification, self-evaluation; chosen for cost-latency tradeoff
   - **Tavily Search API** ŌĆö structured web search results in JSON; avoids HTML parsing overhead
   - **Qdrant Cloud** ŌĆö managed vector DB; free tier sufficient for demo; supports both dense (text-embedding-3-small) and sparse (BM25) vectors for hybrid retrieval

   ### Chunking strategy

   Card data is short by nature: a full card entry is 50ŌĆō150 tokens (title, effect text, point value, tags). The chunking strategy is therefore **one document = one card** ŌĆö no splitting is applied.

   **Why no splitting:**
   1. Cards are atomic units of game logic. Splitting a card's effect text away from its point value would break the semantic unit and hurt retrieval precision.
   2. At 50ŌĆō150 tokens, each card is well within the 8192-token embedding context window of `text-embedding-3-small`, so there is no technical need to split.
   3. Retrieval is card-level: the agent wants "find me 5 cards similar to this prompt", not "find me a paragraph about scoring". Granularity at the card level is optimal.

   **Document schema stored in Qdrant:**
   ```json
   {
     "id": "uuid-v4",
     "text": "Title: Party Foul\nEffect: The player to your left loses 3 points.\nValue: -3\nTags: social, punishment",
     "metadata": {
       "title": "Party Foul",
       "effect": "The player to your left loses 3 points.",
       "point_value": -3,
       "tags": ["social", "punishment"],
       "source": "seed" | "game-session",
       "session_id": "optional"
     }
   }
   ```

   The `text` field is what gets embedded. Concatenating all card fields into a single string rather than embedding each field separately improves semantic recall for multi-faceted queries (e.g., "funny negative card for the leader").
   ````

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Section heading is exactly `## Task 3 ŌĆö Data Sources, External APIs & Chunking Strategy`
- [ ] Data sources table has at least 4 rows
- [ ] Chunking strategy explains WHY one-card-one-document was chosen (not just what)
- [ ] Document schema JSON block is present and syntactically valid

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase0/05-seed-cards-schema.md` ŌĆö card schema reference
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/09-writeup-task2.md` ŌĆö must append to WRITEUP.md after Task 2

title: docs/WRITEUP.md Task 2: solution, infra + agent-workflow diagrams, paras
description:
# p7-writeup-task2: docs/WRITEUP.md ŌĆö Task 2: Solution, infra diagram, agent-workflow diagram + paragraphs

## Context
Rubric Task 2 requires a one-sentence solution description, an infrastructure diagram (what services connect to what), an agent workflow diagram, and 1-2 paragraphs explaining tool choices. This issue authors the Task 2 section of `docs/WRITEUP.md` with all three Mermaid diagrams and explanatory prose. It depends on p7-writeup-task1 creating the file first.

## Files
- `docs/WRITEUP.md` (modify) ŌĆö append Task 2 section with infra + agent diagrams

## Implementation steps

1. Append the following section to `docs/WRITEUP.md` after the Task 1 section:

   ````markdown
   ---

   ## Task 2 ŌĆö Solution Design

   ### Solution statement
   A LangGraph agent backed by a Qdrant vector store and Tavily web search generates and interprets TBWC cards in real time, served over WebSockets from a persistent FastAPI container to a Next.js frontend deployed on Vercel.

   ### Infrastructure diagram

   ```mermaid
   graph LR
       Browser["Browser\n(Next.js / Vercel)"]
       Vercel["Vercel CDN\n(static + SSR)"]
       Render["Render Container\n(FastAPI + LangGraph)"]
       OpenAI["OpenAI API\n(gpt-5.4-mini)"]
       Qdrant["Qdrant Cloud\n(vector store)"]
       Tavily["Tavily API\n(web search)"]
       LangSmith["LangSmith\n(tracing)"]
       Sandbox["Subprocess Sandbox\n(in-container)"]

       Browser -- "HTTPS REST\n/rooms /cards" --> Vercel
       Vercel -- "proxies REST" --> Render
       Browser -- "wss:// direct\n(no Vercel proxy)" --> Render
       Render -- "LLM calls" --> OpenAI
       Render -- "semantic search" --> Qdrant
       Render -- "web search" --> Tavily
       Render -- "traces" --> LangSmith
       Render -- "code execution" --> Sandbox
   ```

   **Per-component justification:**
   - **FastAPI on Render (persistent container):** Native asyncio WebSockets + subprocess sandbox require a long-lived process; Render free tier gives a persistent container unlike Lambda/Cloud Functions.
   - **Next.js on Vercel:** Zero-config deployment for React; Vercel edge network for low-latency static assets; no WebSocket proxy needed (browser connects directly to backend).
   - **OpenAI `gpt-5.4-mini`:** Best cost-latency tradeoff for card generation; fast enough for sub-5s round trips.
   - **Qdrant Cloud:** Managed vector DB with a generous free tier; supports hybrid search (dense + sparse) which improves retrieval recall for short card texts.
   - **Tavily:** Purpose-built search API with structured JSON responses; avoids HTML parsing; free tier covers demo usage.
   - **LangSmith:** First-class LangGraph observability; per-node trace capture without code changes.

   ### Agent workflow diagram

   ```mermaid
   flowchart TD
       START([User prompt or WILD card]) --> REASON[reason\nClassify intent:\ngenerate / interpret / query]
       REASON --> RETRIEVE[retrieve\nQdrant semantic search\nfor similar past cards]
       RETRIEVE --> SEARCH{Web search\nneeded?}
       SEARCH -- Yes --> TAVILY[tavily_search\nFetch external\ngame-design context]
       SEARCH -- No --> CLASSIFY
       TAVILY --> CLASSIFY[classify\nScore relevance of\nretrieved + searched docs]
       CLASSIFY --> EMIT[emit\nGenerate card text +\npoint value via LLM]
       EMIT --> JUDGE[judge\nSelf-evaluate: balanced?\nfunny? on-theme?]
       JUDGE --> PREVIEW{Human\npreview?}
       PREVIEW -- Reject/Edit --> EMIT
       PREVIEW -- Accept --> SNIPPET[snippet\nEmit final card JSON\nover WebSocket]
       SNIPPET --> EPILOGUE[epilogue\nVote: should this card\ngo to memory?]
       EPILOGUE -- Yes --> MEMORY[memory_write\nUpsert card embedding\ninto Qdrant]
       EPILOGUE -- No --> END([Done])
       MEMORY --> END
   ```

   **Agent design notes (1-2 paragraphs):**

   The agent uses a reason-first architecture: the `reason` node classifies the user's intent before committing to a retrieval strategy. This avoids unnecessary Qdrant queries for pure game-state lookups and avoids web searches for well-covered card archetypes already in the vector store. The `judge` node implements a lightweight self-evaluation loop ŌĆö the LLM scores its own output on balance, humour, and theme adherence before the card reaches the human preview step, reducing the rate of low-quality cards that users must manually reject.

   The epilogue-to-memory loop closes the flywheel: cards that players accept are re-embedded and stored in Qdrant, improving retrieval quality over time. The subprocess sandbox node (not shown for clarity) executes any card-defined Python snippets in a resource-limited child process, returning structured output that the emit node incorporates into the final card text. This allows generative cards that compute scores dynamically without running arbitrary code in the main server process.
   ````

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Both Mermaid diagrams render on GitHub (validate at https://mermaid.live)
- [ ] Infrastructure diagram includes all 7 components: Browser, Vercel, Render, OpenAI, Qdrant, Tavily, LangSmith
- [ ] Agent workflow diagram includes: reason, retrieve, search, classify, emit, judge, preview, epilogue, memory nodes
- [ ] Per-component justification table has one sentence per service
- [ ] Section heading is exactly `## Task 2 ŌĆö Solution Design`

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/08-writeup-task1.md` ŌĆö must add to existing WRITEUP.md
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase3/06-ws-endpoint.md` ŌĆö ws endpoint in infra diagram

   # 1000 Blank White Cards ŌĆö Project Writeup

   > Rubric reference: Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
   > Deployed app: <!-- TODO p7-writeup-task4: fill in Vercel URL -->
   > Loom demo: <!-- TODO p7-loom-script: fill in Loom URL -->

   ---

   ## Task 1 ŌĆö Problem Definition

   ### Problem statement
   Physical 1000 Blank White Cards (TBWC) sessions stall mid-game because players cannot quickly produce creative, balanced card effects ŌĆö they must invent rules from scratch with no reference to prior games, leading to uneven gameplay and "blank card paralysis."

   ### Why it's a problem for users
   TBWC is a party game where players draw blank cards and write their own rules onto them. The creative demand is high: a good card needs a funny effect, a concrete mechanic, and ideally a callback to earlier events in the same game. In practice, most players have played fewer than five sessions and carry no inventory of card ideas. When a player draws a blank card they either write something generic ("gain 5 points") or stall the game for several minutes while they think. The result is dead air in a social context, disproportionate card quality between experienced and new players, and session fatigue in groups that play frequently.

   A second pain point surfaces in "WILD" interpretation rounds: the group must agree on what an ambiguous legacy card means. Without a shared memory of prior games, this devolves into argument. An AI system with access to card history and game-design heuristics can resolve interpretation disputes in seconds and generate novel card effects that are calibrated to the current game state.

   ### Current (paper) workflow

   ```mermaid
   flowchart TD
       A([Player draws blank card]) --> B{Has card idea?}
       B -- Yes --> C[Write effect + point value\nby hand]
       B -- No --> D[Ask other players\nfor suggestions]
       D --> E{Group agrees?}
       E -- No --> F[Stall / skip card]
       E -- Yes --> C
       C --> G[Play card in current turn]
       G --> H{Ambiguous effect?}
       H -- No --> I[Apply effect, update score]
       H -- Yes --> J[Group argues interpretation]
       J --> K{Consensus?}
       K -- No --> L[House-rule or ignore card]
       K -- Yes --> I
       I --> M([Next turn])
       F --> M
       L --> M
   ```

   ### Evaluation Q/A pairs (used by eval harness in Task 5)

   These 12 pairs cover the agent's primary capabilities and are used as the golden test set.

   | # | Question (user prompt) | Expected output (contains) |
   |---|----------------------|---------------------------|
   | 1 | "Write a card that punishes the player in the lead" | effect targeting highest-score player, negative point value |
   | 2 | "Interpret this legacy card: 'Winner must do a dance'" | clear action description, point value or modifier |
   | 3 | "Generate a card about food that gives points" | food theme, positive integer score |
   | 4 | "Make a card that references the last card played" | callback mechanic, references previous action |
   | 5 | "WILD: this card says ??? ŌĆö what does it mean?" | agent admits uncertainty, gives most-plausible interpretation |
   | 6 | "Write a card for a 3-player game with a catch-up mechanic" | mentions trailing player, balancing mechanic |
   | 7 | "Give me a card that ends the game immediately" | 'end' or 'game over' trigger, clear condition |
   | 8 | "Make a funny card about bureaucracy" | humour marker (pun/absurdist), bureaucracy theme |
   | 9 | "What are the current scores?" | retrieval of game state, numeric scores |
   | 10 | "Generate a card that can only be played once per game" | one-time-use constraint |
   | 11 | "Interpret: 'All players swap hands'" | hands = card hands, swap mechanic described |
   | 12 | "Write a card that interacts with the previous card" | references prior card by effect type |
   ````

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `docs/WRITEUP.md` exists and contains all four subsections: problem statement, two paragraphs, Mermaid diagram, eval table
- [ ] Mermaid diagram renders correctly on GitHub (paste into https://mermaid.live to verify)
- [ ] Eval table has at least 10 rows with non-trivial expected outputs
- [ ] Document uses the exact section heading `## Task 1 ŌĆö Problem Definition`

## References
- Game rules context: `/Users/msharp/projects/a-thousand-blank-white-cards/README.md`

title: scripts/smoke_test.py: /health probe + wss check + deploy checklist
description:
# p7-smoke-test-checklist: Post-deploy smoke test checklist + automated /health probe

## Context
After deploying backend (Render) and frontend (Vercel), a structured smoke test ensures the full stack is wired correctly before marking the deploy done. This issue adds a human-runnable checklist and a small Python script that probes the backend health endpoint ŌĆö useful for CI or quick re-checks after redeploys.

## Files
- `scripts/smoke_test.py` (create) ŌĆö CLI script: probe /health, test WebSocket, check CORS headers
- `docs/deploy/smoke-checklist.md` (create) ŌĆö human checklist covering browser on phone/laptop, wild-card interpretation

## Implementation steps

1. Create `scripts/smoke_test.py`:
   ```python
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   """
   Smoke test for deployed TBWC backend.
   Usage: python scripts/smoke_test.py --backend https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com
   """
   from __future__ import annotations
   import argparse
   import sys
   import asyncio
   import httpx
   import websockets


   async def check_health(backend: str) -> bool:
       url = f"{backend}/health"
       print(f"[health] GET {url}")
       async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
           resp = await client.get(url, timeout=15.0)
       ok = resp.status_code == 200 and resp.json().get("status") == "ok"
       print(f"  ŌåÆ {resp.status_code} {resp.json()} {'Ō£ō' if ok else 'Ō£Ś'}")
       return ok


   async def check_cors(backend: str, origin: str) -> bool:
       url = f"{backend}/health"
       print(f"[cors] OPTIONS {url} Origin: {origin}")
       async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
           resp = await client.options(
               url,
               headers={"Origin": origin, "Access-Control-Request-Method": "GET"},
               timeout=10.0,
           )
       acao = resp.headers.get("access-control-allow-origin", "")
       ok = origin in acao or acao == "*"
       print(f"  ŌåÆ access-control-allow-origin: {acao!r} {'Ō£ō' if ok else 'Ō£Ś'}")
       return ok


   async def check_ws(backend: str) -> bool:
       ws_url = backend.replace("https://", "wss://").replace("http://", "ws://")
       ws_url = f"{ws_url}/ws/SMOKE01"
       print(f"[ws] connecting {ws_url}")
       try:
           async with websockets.connect(ws_url, open_timeout=10) as ws:
               await ws.send('{"type":"ping"}')
               print("  ŌåÆ connected and sent ping Ō£ō")
               return True
       except Exception as exc:
           print(f"  ŌåÆ FAILED: {exc} Ō£Ś")
           return False


   async def main() -> None:
       parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
       parser.add_argument("--backend", required=True, help="Backend base URL")
       parser.add_argument("--frontend-origin", default="", help="Vercel frontend origin for CORS check")
       args = parser.parse_args()

       results = []
       results.append(await check_health(args.backend))
       if args.frontend_origin:
           results.append(await check_cors(args.backend, args.frontend_origin))
       results.append(await check_ws(args.backend))

       passed = sum(results)
       total = len(results)
       print(f"\n{'PASS' if all(results) else 'FAIL'} {passed}/{total} checks passed")
       sys.exit(0 if all(results) else 1)


   if __name__ == "__main__":
       asyncio.run(main())
   ```

2. Create `docs/deploy/smoke-checklist.md`:
   ```markdown
   # Post-deploy Smoke Test Checklist

   Run after every deploy. All items must pass before marking p7-backend-deploy-render DONE.

   ## Automated checks
   ```bash
   python scripts/smoke_test.py \
     --backend https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com \
     --frontend-origin https://tbwc.vercel.app
   ```
   Expected: `PASS 3/3 checks passed`

   ## Manual browser checks
   - [ ] Load `https://tbwc.vercel.app` on **desktop browser** ŌĆö page renders, no console errors
   - [ ] Load `https://tbwc.vercel.app` on **mobile** (phone) ŌĆö page renders
   - [ ] Create a room, join with two tabs ŌĆö both tabs receive game state updates
   - [ ] Interpret a WILD card live ŌĆö agent returns an interpretation within 10s
   - [ ] WebSocket connection stays open during interpretation (no reconnect loop)
   - [ ] LangSmith dashboard shows a new trace for the interpretation

   ## Wake-up note
   Render free tier spins down after 15 min. The first request after sleep takes ~30s.
   Run `curl https://tbwc-backend.onrender.com/health` to wake it before demoing.
   ```

3. Add `websockets` and `httpx` as dev dependencies so the smoke test can run locally:
   ```toml
   # pyproject.toml [dependency-groups.dev]
   websockets = ">=12.0"
   httpx = ">=0.27"
   ```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `python scripts/smoke_test.py --backend <render-url> --frontend-origin <vercel-url>` exits 0
- [ ] All manual checklist items in `docs/deploy/smoke-checklist.md` are checked off
- [ ] Script handles Render wake-up latency (10ŌĆō30s) without crashing (timeout=15s on health)

## References
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/02-backend-deploy-render.md` ŌĆö backend URL
- Reuse: `/tmp/tbwc-issues/phase7/04-frontend-vercel-deploy.md` ŌĆö frontend origin
